## Setup
```{r setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# התקן את כל החבילות
#install.packages(c("ggplot2", "dplyr", "tidyr", "reshape2", "stringr", "data.table", 
           #        "gridExtra", "hms", "lubridate", "cowplot", "googlesheets4", 
            #       "epitools","openxlsx", "openair", "ggpubr", "slider", "pracma", "zoo",

              #   dependencies = TRUE)

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(reshape2)
library(stringr)
library(data.table)
library(gridExtra)
library(hms)
library(lubridate)
library(cowplot)
library(googlesheets4)
library(epitools)
library(openair)
library(ggpubr)
library(slider)
library(pracma)
library(zoo)
library(purrr)
library(openxlsx)


#Rmarkdown assembly

library(formattable)
library(systemfonts)
library(kableExtra)
library(tinytex) #if this installation does not work, read section below in RStudio 





```

## FCM (Flow Cytometry)
### unstained samples: Load required packages and import raw FCM data (unstained samples)
```{r}
################################################################################
## FCM (Flow Cytometry) — Unstained samples
## 1A. SAFE Excel Import using openxlsx + SANITIZE (fixes "unknown type #29")
##
## WHY THIS CHUNK EXISTS
##   Sometimes Excel contains internal cell types that crash printing functions
##   in R (e.g., str(), glimpse()) with:
##     "unknown type #29"
##
## IMPORTANT
##   - The read itself may succeed, but str()/glimpse() can crash.
##   - So we:
##       1) Read with openxlsx
##       2) Immediately "sanitize" the table -> convert all columns to plain text
##       3) Do safe inspections (dim, names, head)
##       4) (Optional) keep both versions:
##           * fcm_raw_clean  = sanitized (safe to print)
##           * fcm_raw        = original (if you want types later)
##
## OUTPUT OBJECTS
##   - fcm_preview_clean : sanitized preview (first ~50 rows)
##   - fcm_raw_clean     : sanitized full table (SAFE to print/inspect)
##   - sheet_name        : chosen sheet name (usually "FCS Express Report")
################################################################################

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0) Libraries
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
library(openxlsx)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) File path + existence check
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
path <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/FCS files/Unstained_Meds2022.xlsx"
stopifnot(file.exists(path))
cat("✓ File exists:", path, "\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Get sheet names + choose sheet
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
sheets_available <- openxlsx::getSheetNames(path)
cat("Available sheets:\n")
print(sheets_available)

# Usually there is only one sheet: "FCS Express Report"
sheet_name <- sheets_available[1]
cat("Using sheet:", sheet_name, "\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Helper: SANITIZE any column to plain character safely
#    (handles list-like columns / weird Excel cell representations)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
safe_as_char <- function(x) {
  if (is.list(x)) {
    return(vapply(x, function(z) paste(z, collapse = " "), character(1)))
  }
  as.character(x)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Preview import (small) + sanitize
#    NOTE: DO NOT call str() on the raw preview (can crash).
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
fcm_preview_raw <- openxlsx::read.xlsx(
  xlsxFile     = path,
  sheet        = sheet_name,
  rows         = 1:50,
  colNames     = TRUE,
  detectDates  = TRUE
)

fcm_preview_clean <- fcm_preview_raw %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), safe_as_char))

cat("\n--- PREVIEW (SANITIZED) ---\n")
cat("Dimensions: ", nrow(fcm_preview_clean), " × ", ncol(fcm_preview_clean), "\n", sep = "")
cat("First 30 column names:\n")
print(head(names(fcm_preview_clean), 30))

cat("\nFirst 3 rows (first 10 columns):\n")
print(fcm_preview_clean[1:min(3, nrow(fcm_preview_clean)), 1:min(10, ncol(fcm_preview_clean))],
      row.names = FALSE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Full import + sanitize (this is what you continue with downstream)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
fcm_raw <- openxlsx::read.xlsx(
  xlsxFile     = path,
  sheet        = sheet_name,
  colNames     = TRUE,
  detectDates  = TRUE
)

fcm_raw_clean <- fcm_raw %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), safe_as_char))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) Structural diagnostics (SAFE)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n--- FULL DATA (SANITIZED) ---\n")
cat("Rows:    ", nrow(fcm_raw_clean), "\n", sep = "")
cat("Columns: ", ncol(fcm_raw_clean), "\n", sep = "")

cat("\nColumn names:\n")
print(names(fcm_raw_clean))

# Show a compact "type" summary AFTER sanitize (should all be character)
cat("\nColumn classes (after sanitize):\n")
print(sort(table(sapply(fcm_raw_clean, class)), decreasing = TRUE))

cat("\n✓ Safe import completed (sanitized). You can now use names(), head(), glimpse() safely.\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7) OPTIONAL: If you want a "text-only" object name for your pipeline
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
fcm_raw_text <- fcm_raw_clean
```



### Unstained FCM (Flow Cytometry) Unstained – cleaning + long-format + time reconstruction
```{r, fig.width=15,fig.height=10}

################################################################################
# FCM Unstained – Full tidy pipeline
# (cleanup → parse Sample_ID → join Unique_code+InEx from CSV → wide->long events
#  → medians → propagate beads references → (optional) remove beads rows
#  → time reconstruction from fraction-of-day → final ordering)
#
# RULES (same as Stained):
#   - Parse ONLY Sample_ID from the FCS filename (e.g., "S7")
#   - Join Unique_code + InEx from Unique_code.csv using Sample_ID
#   - Do NOT parse IN/EX from filename
#
# IMPORTANT:
#   Unstained export often DOES NOT include:
#     - `Total volume (nL)`
#     - `Flow Rate (ul/min)`
#   So we supply defaults if missing (set them below).
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(lubridate)

# ==============================================================================
# USER SETTINGS (edit these ONCE)
# ==============================================================================
map_path <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/FCS files/Unique_code.csv"

# If Unstained Excel does not contain these columns, we will fill with defaults:
total_volume_nL_default  <- 80000   # <- put the real value for Unstained runs
flow_rate_ul_min_default <- 25      # <- put the real value for Unstained runs

# Remove Beads rows at the end (keeps Beads_* reference columns)
REMOVE_BEADS_ROWS <- TRUE

# ==============================================================================
# 0) Read mapping table: Unique_code.csv  (Sample_ID -> Unique_code + InEx)
# ==============================================================================
stopifnot(file.exists(map_path))

unique_map_raw <- read_csv(map_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

unique_map <- unique_map_raw %>%
  rename_with(~ str_replace_all(.x, "\\s+", "_")) %>%
  rename(
    Sample_ID   = Sample_ID,
    Unique_code = Unique_code,
    InEx        = InEx
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_ID   = as.character(Sample_ID),
    Unique_code = as.character(Unique_code),
    InEx        = toupper(as.character(InEx)),
    InEx        = na_if(InEx, "N/A"),
    InEx        = na_if(InEx, "NA"),
    InEx        = na_if(InEx, "")
  ) %>%
  distinct(Sample_ID, .keep_all = TRUE)

# HARD QC: mapping must be 1-to-1 by Sample_ID
dup_map <- unique_map_raw %>%
  count(Sample_ID, name = "n") %>%
  filter(n > 1)

if (nrow(dup_map) > 0) {
  message("❌ ERROR: duplicate Sample_ID mappings in Unique_code.csv (must be 1-to-1).")
  print(dup_map, n = 200)
  stop("Stop: fix Unique_code.csv duplicates.")
}

# ==============================================================================
# 1) Input: fcm_raw_text must exist (imported with openxlsx, coerced to character)
# ==============================================================================
stopifnot(exists("fcm_raw_text"))
fcm_raw <- fcm_raw_text

# Expected columns (based on your export)
needed_cols <- c("FCS.file.name", "Analysis.date_x0000_", "Start.Time", "End.Time")
missing_cols <- setdiff(needed_cols, names(fcm_raw))
if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop("Missing required columns in fcm_raw_text:\n- ",
       paste(missing_cols, collapse = "\n- "),
       "\n\nAvailable columns:\n- ",
       paste(names(fcm_raw), collapse = "\n- "))
}

# Helper: robust numeric parsing from text-like columns (##ERROR## -> NA)
pnum <- function(x) readr::parse_number(as.character(x), na = c("", "NA", "N/A", "##ERROR##"))

# ==============================================================================
# 2) Parse Sample_ID from FCS filename + join mapping (Unique_code + InEx)
# ==============================================================================
fcm_parsed <- fcm_raw %>%
  mutate(
    FCS_file  = .data[["FCS.file.name"]],
    Sample_ID = str_extract(FCS_file, "(?<=_)S\\d+(?=_)")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_ID = if_else(is.na(Sample_ID), str_extract(FCS_file, "S\\d+"), Sample_ID)
  ) %>%
  left_join(unique_map, by = "Sample_ID")

# HARD QC: Sample_ID parsing failures
bad_sid <- fcm_parsed %>% filter(is.na(Sample_ID) | Sample_ID == "")
if (nrow(bad_sid) > 0) {
  message("❌ ERROR: Could not parse Sample_ID from some FCS filenames.")
  print(bad_sid %>% select(FCS_file) %>% head(20))
  stop("Stop: fix Sample_ID parsing regex.")
}

# HARD QC: unmapped Sample_ID
unmapped <- fcm_parsed %>% filter(is.na(Unique_code) | is.na(InEx) | Unique_code == "" | InEx == "")
if (nrow(unmapped) > 0) {
  message("❌ ERROR: Unmapped Sample_IDs found (exist in Excel but not in Unique_code.csv).")
  print(unmapped %>% select(Sample_ID, FCS_file) %>% distinct() %>% head(200), n = 200)
  stop("Stop: complete Unique_code.csv mapping.")
}

# ==============================================================================
# 3) Parse time fractions + (optional) set Total volume/Flow rate columns
# ==============================================================================
# If these columns exist in your Unstained export, we use them.
# Otherwise we create them from defaults (same value for all rows).
has_total_vol <- "Total volume (nL)" %in% names(fcm_parsed)
has_flow_rate <- "Flow Rate (ul/min)" %in% names(fcm_parsed)

fcm_time0 <- fcm_parsed %>%
  mutate(
    Start_Time_frac = pnum(.data[["Start.Time"]]),
    End_Time_frac   = pnum(.data[["End.Time"]]),
    `Total volume (nL)`  = if (has_total_vol) pnum(.data[["Total volume (nL)"]]) else total_volume_nL_default,
    `Flow Rate (ul/min)` = if (has_flow_rate) pnum(.data[["Flow Rate (ul/min)"]]) else flow_rate_ul_min_default
  )

# ==============================================================================
# 4) Parse Analysis_Date from Hebrew format like "10-פבר-2025"
# ==============================================================================
he_months <- c(
  "ינו"="01","פבר"="02","מרץ"="03","אפר"="04",
  "מאי"="05","יונ"="06","יול"="07","אוג"="08",
  "ספט"="09","אוק"="10","נוב"="11","דצמ"="12"
)

parse_he_date <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  parts <- str_split(x, "-", simplify = TRUE)
  if (ncol(parts) < 3) return(as.Date(NA))
  dd <- parts[,1]
  mm <- he_months[parts[,2]]
  yy <- parts[,3]
  as.Date(paste0(yy, "-", mm, "-", dd), format = "%Y-%m-%d")
}

fcm_time1 <- fcm_time0 %>%
  mutate(
    Analysis_Date = parse_he_date(.data[["Analysis.date_x0000_"]])
  )

if (any(is.na(fcm_time1$Analysis_Date))) {
  message("⚠️ WARNING: Some Analysis_Date values could not be parsed. Examples:")
  print(fcm_time1 %>% filter(is.na(Analysis_Date)) %>% select(Analysis.date_x0000_) %>% distinct() %>% head(20))
}

# ==============================================================================
# 5) Build Start_DateTime / End_DateTime from date + fraction-of-day
# ==============================================================================
fcm_time2 <- fcm_time1 %>%
  mutate(
    Start_DateTime = as.POSIXct(Analysis_Date) + seconds(Start_Time_frac * 86400),
    End_DateTime   = as.POSIXct(Analysis_Date) + seconds(End_Time_frac   * 86400)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    End_DateTime = if_else(!is.na(End_DateTime) & !is.na(Start_DateTime) & End_DateTime < Start_DateTime,
                           End_DateTime + days(1),
                           End_DateTime)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Run_duration_min = as.numeric(difftime(End_DateTime, Start_DateTime, units = "mins"))
  )

# ==============================================================================
# 6) Define population columns (Events + Medians) and pivot wide->long
#    This is where we compute Concentration_uL exactly like Stained:
#      Volume_uL = `Total volume (nL)` / 1000
#      Concentration_uL = Events / Volume_uL
# ==============================================================================
# In your Unstained final you had these Groups (incl Beads):
#   Beads, Euk, PEuk, NEuk, Pro, Syn
# Event columns in the export (with dots):
event_cols <- c(
  "Beads.1um.Events.#",
  "Euk.Events.#",
  "PEuk.Events.#",
  "NEuk.Events.#",
  "Pro.Events.#",
  "Syn.Events.#"
)

# Median columns in the export:
median_cols <- c(
  "Beads.FSC.median","Euk.FSC.median","PEuk.FSC.median","NEuk.FSC.median","Pro.FSC.median","Syn.FSC.median",
  "Beads.SSC.median","Euk.SSC.median","PEuk.SSC.median","NEuk.SSC.median","Pro.SSC.median","Syn.SSC.median",
  "Beads.Red.FL.median","Euk.Red.FL.median","PEuk.Red.FL.median","NEuk.Red.FL.median","Pro.Red.FL.median","Syn.Red.FL.median",
  "Beads.Orange.FL.median","Syn.Orange.FL.median"
)

event_cols  <- intersect(event_cols,  names(fcm_time2))
median_cols <- intersect(median_cols, names(fcm_time2))

if (length(event_cols) == 0) stop("No event columns found in fcm_raw_text (check column names).")

# ---- Events long ----
events_long <- fcm_time2 %>%
  select(
    FCS_file,
    Project.name_x0000_,
    Cytometer,
    Analysis_Date, Start_DateTime, End_DateTime, Run_duration_min,
    `Total volume (nL)`, `Flow Rate (ul/min)`,
    Sample_ID, Unique_code, InEx,
    any_of(event_cols)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = any_of(event_cols),
    names_to  = "raw_group",
    values_to = "Events_raw"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Group = case_when(
      raw_group == "Beads.1um.Events.#" ~ "Beads",
      raw_group == "Euk.Events.#"      ~ "Euk",
      raw_group == "PEuk.Events.#"     ~ "PEuk",
      raw_group == "NEuk.Events.#"     ~ "NEuk",
      raw_group == "Pro.Events.#"      ~ "Pro",
      raw_group == "Syn.Events.#"      ~ "Syn",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    Events = pnum(Events_raw),
    Volume_uL = `Total volume (nL)` / 1000,
    Concentration_uL = Events / Volume_uL
  ) %>%
  select(-raw_group, -Events_raw)

# ---- Medians long helper (same idea as stained) ----
make_group_median_long_unstained <- function(df, suffix_regex, value_name) {
  # suffix_regex example: "FSC\\.median$"
  cols <- names(df)[str_detect(names(df), suffix_regex)]
  if (length(cols) == 0) return(df %>% transmute(FCS_file, Group = NA_character_, !!value_name := NA_real_) %>% slice(0))

  df %>%
    select(FCS_file, all_of(cols)) %>%
    mutate(across(all_of(cols), as.character)) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = all_of(cols),
      names_to = "raw_name",
      values_to = "raw"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      Group = case_when(
        str_starts(raw_name, "Beads") ~ "Beads",
        str_starts(raw_name, "Euk")   ~ "Euk",
        str_starts(raw_name, "PEuk")  ~ "PEuk",
        str_starts(raw_name, "NEuk")  ~ "NEuk",
        str_starts(raw_name, "Pro")   ~ "Pro",
        str_starts(raw_name, "Syn")   ~ "Syn",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ),
      "{value_name}" := pnum(raw)
    ) %>%
    select(FCS_file, Group, all_of(value_name)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Group))
}

fsc_long <- make_group_median_long_unstained(fcm_time2, "FSC\\.median$", "FSC_median")
ssc_long <- make_group_median_long_unstained(fcm_time2, "SSC\\.median$", "SSC_median")

# Join medians into event-long
fcm_long2 <- events_long %>%
  left_join(fsc_long, by = c("FCS_file","Group")) %>%
  left_join(ssc_long, by = c("FCS_file","Group"))

# ==============================================================================
# 7) Propagate beads reference values to ALL rows within each run
# ==============================================================================
fcm_final <- fcm_long2 %>%
  mutate(
    `Project name` = as.character(Project.name_x0000_)  # rename to match stained
  ) %>%
  group_by(`Project name`, Sample_ID, Unique_code, InEx, Analysis_Date, Start_DateTime, End_DateTime) %>%
  mutate(
    Beads_FSC_median       = first(FSC_median[Group == "Beads"]),
    Beads_Concentration_uL = first(Concentration_uL[Group == "Beads"])
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# ==============================================================================
# 8) Optionally remove Beads rows (keeps Beads_* reference columns)
# ==============================================================================
if (REMOVE_BEADS_ROWS) {
  fcm_final <- fcm_final %>% filter(Group != "Beads")
}

# ==============================================================================
# 9) Final Unstained table: EXACT column names like stained_final_time
# ==============================================================================
fcm_unstained_final_time <- fcm_final %>%
  transmute(
    FCS_file = as.character(FCS_file),
    `Project name` = as.character(`Project name`),
    Analysis_Date = as.Date(Analysis_Date),
    Start_DateTime = as.POSIXct(Start_DateTime, tz="UTC"),
    End_DateTime   = as.POSIXct(End_DateTime,   tz="UTC"),
    `Total volume (nL)`  = as.numeric(`Total volume (nL)`),
    `Flow Rate (ul/min)` = as.numeric(`Flow Rate (ul/min)`),

    Sample_ID   = as.character(Sample_ID),
    Unique_code = as.character(Unique_code),
    InEx        = toupper(trimws(as.character(InEx))),

    Group = trimws(as.character(Group)),

    Volume_uL          = as.numeric(Volume_uL),
    Concentration_uL   = as.numeric(Concentration_uL),
    Beads_Concentration_uL = as.numeric(Beads_Concentration_uL),

    FSC_median         = as.numeric(FSC_median),
    Beads_FSC_median   = as.numeric(Beads_FSC_median),
    SSC_median         = as.numeric(SSC_median),

    Run_duration_min   = as.numeric(Run_duration_min)
  ) %>%
  relocate(Sample_ID, Unique_code, InEx, Group, .before = Volume_uL)

# If you want the object name to match your merge chunk:
fcm_unstained_final <- fcm_unstained_final_time

# ==============================================================================
# 10) QC prints
# ==============================================================================
message("✓ fcm_unstained_final created")
print(str(fcm_unstained_final))

fcm_unstained_final %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    n_missing_conc = sum(is.na(Concentration_uL)),
    n_missing_beads = sum(is.na(Beads_Concentration_uL)),
    n_missing_vol = sum(is.na(`Total volume (nL)`)),
    n_missing_flow = sum(is.na(`Flow Rate (ul/min)`))
  ) %>% print()

################################################################################
# End chunk
################################################################################
```
### FCM Stained – Load raw FCM data (stained samples)
```{r}

################################################################################
# 2. Load raw FCM data (stained samples)
#
# Purpose:
#   - Import the stained flow cytometry (FCM) dataset from Excel
#   - Verify file existence and available sheets
#   - Load the raw stained data for downstream cleaning and merging
################################################################################

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Load required packages
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

library(readxl)   # Reading Excel files
library(dplyr)    # Data manipulation and inspection

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Define file path (Windows format)
# NOTE: Use forward slashes (/) instead of backslashes (\)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

path_stained <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/FCS files/Stained_Meds2022.xlsx"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Safety check: ensure the file exists before attempting to read it
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

stopifnot(file.exists(path_stained))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Inspect available sheets in the Excel file
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

sheets_stained <- excel_sheets(path_stained)
print(sheets_stained)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Load raw stained FCM data
# Default behavior: read the first sheet in the file
# (Change to sheets_stained[2] or a specific sheet name if needed)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

fcm_raw_stained <- read_excel(path_stained, sheet = sheets_stained[1])

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Quick structural overview of the imported dataset
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

glimpse(fcm_raw_stained)

```

### FCM Stained – Full tidy pipeline
```{r}
################################################################################
# FCM Stained – Full tidy pipeline
# (cleanup → parse Sample_ID → join Unique_code+InEx from CSV → wide->long events
#  → medians → propagate beads references → remove beads rows → typing
#  → time reconstruction → final ordering)
#
# NEW RULE (same as Unstained):
#   - Parse ONLY Sample_ID from the FCS filename (e.g., "S7")
#   - Join Unique_code + InEx from Unique_code.csv using Sample_ID
#   - Do NOT parse IN/EX from filename
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(lubridate)

# ==============================================================================
# 0) Read mapping table: Unique_code.csv  (Sample_ID -> Unique_code + InEx)
#    This is the ONLY source of truth for InEx.
# ==============================================================================
map_path <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/FCS files/Unique_code.csv"
unique_map_raw <- read_csv(map_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

unique_map <- unique_map_raw %>%
  rename_with(~ str_replace_all(.x, "\\s+", "_")) %>%
  rename(
    Sample_ID   = Sample_ID,
    Unique_code = Unique_code,
    InEx        = InEx
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_ID   = as.character(Sample_ID),
    Unique_code = as.character(Unique_code),
    InEx        = toupper(as.character(InEx)),
    InEx        = na_if(InEx, "N/A"),
    InEx        = na_if(InEx, "NA"),
    InEx        = na_if(InEx, "")
  )

# QC: duplicates in mapping
dup_map <- unique_map %>%
  count(Sample_ID, name = "n") %>%
  filter(n > 1)

if (nrow(dup_map) > 0) {
  message("WARNING: duplicate Sample_ID entries found in Unique_code.csv:")
  print(dup_map, n = 200)
}

# ==============================================================================
# 1) Initial column cleanup
#    Drop columns dominated by "##ERROR##" or not used in stained workflow
#    (stained focus: Beads + Bact)
# ==============================================================================
cols_to_drop <- c(
  # Concentrations with no valid data
  "HNA-Ls Conc (#/mL)",
  "HNA-HS Conc (#/mL)",
  "LNA Conc (#/mL)",

  # Event counts for populations not analyzed further
  "HNA-HS # events",
  "HNA-Ls # events",
  "LNA # of events",

  # FSC medians for HNA/LNA populations
  "HNA-Hs FSC median",
  "HNA-Ls FSC median",
  "LNA FSC median",

  # Derived ratios (can be recomputed later if needed)
  "HNA-Hs FSC/ Beads FSC median ratio",
  "LNA FSC/ Beads FSC median ratio",
  "HNA-Ls FSC/ Beads FSC median ratio",

  # SSC medians with mostly missing values
  "Phyto SSC median",
  "LNA SSC median",
  "HNA-Ls SSC median",
  "HNA-Hs SSC median",

  # Green fluorescence medians/ratios for HNA/LNA (mostly missing)
  "LNA Green FL median (Blue Laser)",
  "HNA-Ls Green FL median (Blue Laser)",
  "HNA-Hs Green FL median (Blue Laser)",
  "HNA-Ls Green FL/Beads FL median ratio",
  "HNA-Hs Green FL/Beads FL median ratio",
  "LNA Green FL/Beads FL median ratio"
)

fcm_stained_clean <- fcm_raw_stained %>%
  select(-any_of(cols_to_drop))

# ==============================================================================
# 2) Parse ONLY Sample_ID from the FSC file name + join Unique_code/InEx from CSV
#    Example filename:
#      "Medes_expedition_2022_Stained_20250210_S7_A_IN_stained.fcs"
#    We parse only "S7" and IGNORE the "_IN_" part in the filename.
# ==============================================================================
fcm_stained_parsed <- fcm_stained_clean %>%
  mutate(
    # in stained file the column is "FSC file name"
    FCS_file  = `FSC file name`,
    Sample_ID = str_extract(FCS_file, "(?<=_)S\\d+(?=_)")
  ) %>%
  left_join(unique_map, by = "Sample_ID")

# QC: missing Sample_ID parsing
fcm_stained_parsed %>%
  summarise(n_missing_Sample_ID = sum(is.na(Sample_ID))) %>%
  print()

# QC: unmapped Sample_ID (exists in files, missing in CSV)
unmapped <- fcm_stained_parsed %>%
  filter(!is.na(Sample_ID) & (is.na(Unique_code) | is.na(InEx))) %>%
  distinct(Sample_ID, FCS_file)

if (nrow(unmapped) > 0) {
  message("WARNING: Sample_ID not mapped to Unique_code/InEx (fix Unique_code.csv):")
  print(unmapped, n = 200)
}

# Quick sanity view: one row per sample mapping
fcm_stained_parsed %>%
  distinct(Sample_ID, Unique_code, InEx) %>%
  arrange(Sample_ID) %>%
  print(n = 100)

# ==============================================================================
# 3) Wide -> Long (Events) for ONLY the two groups in stained: Beads + Bact
#    Concentration (cells/µL) = Events / (Total volume (nL) / 1000)
# ==============================================================================
event_cols_stained <- c(
  "1 um Beads # events",
  "Bact # events"
)

fcm_stained_long <- fcm_stained_parsed %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(event_cols_stained),
    names_to  = "Group",
    values_to = "Events_raw"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Group = Group %>%
      str_replace("^1\\s*um\\s*Beads\\s*#\\s*events$", "Beads") %>%
      str_replace("^Bact\\s*#\\s*events$", "Bact"),

    # numeric events ("##ERROR##" -> NA)
    Events = parse_number(as.character(Events_raw)),

    # nL -> µL
    Volume_uL = `Total volume (nL)` / 1000,

    # cells/µL
    Concentration_uL = Events / Volume_uL
  ) %>%
  select(-Events_raw)

# QC (optional)
fcm_stained_long %>%
  summarise(
    n_na_events = sum(is.na(Events)),
    n_na_conc   = sum(is.na(Concentration_uL)),
    min_vol_uL  = min(Volume_uL, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  print()

# ==============================================================================
# 4) Helper: convert group-specific median columns from wide -> long
#    Handles mixed types ("##ERROR##") by forcing character before pivoting.
# ==============================================================================
make_group_median_long <- function(df, suffix, value_name) {
  df %>%
    select(FCS_file, ends_with(suffix)) %>%
    mutate(across(ends_with(suffix), as.character)) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = ends_with(suffix),
      names_to = "Group",
      values_to = "raw"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      Group = str_replace(Group, paste0("\\s*", suffix, "\\s*$"), ""),
      "{value_name}" := parse_number(raw) # "##ERROR##" -> NA (expected warning)
    ) %>%
    select(FCS_file, Group, all_of(value_name))
}

# Stained: only need FSC + SSC
fsc_long_stained <- make_group_median_long(fcm_stained_parsed, "FSC median", "FSC_median")
ssc_long_stained <- make_group_median_long(fcm_stained_parsed, "SSC median", "SSC_median")

# Join medians into the long table
fcm_stained_long2 <- fcm_stained_long %>%
  left_join(fsc_long_stained, by = c("FCS_file", "Group")) %>%
  left_join(ssc_long_stained, by = c("FCS_file", "Group"))

# ==============================================================================
# 5) Keep tidy (analysis-ready) columns
#    IMPORTANT: identifiers include Sample_ID + Unique_code + InEx (from CSV)
# ==============================================================================
fcm_stained_final <- fcm_stained_long2 %>%
  select(
    FCS_file,
    `Project name`,
    `Analysis date`,
    `Start time`,
    `End time`,
    `Total volume (nL)`,
    `Flow Rate (ul/min)`,

    # identifiers
    Sample_ID,
    Unique_code,
    InEx,

    # long measures
    Group,
    Events,
    Volume_uL,
    Concentration_uL,
    FSC_median,
    SSC_median
  ) %>%
  relocate(Sample_ID, Unique_code, InEx, Group, .before = Events)

# ==============================================================================
# 6) Propagate Beads reference values to ALL rows within each run/sample
#    Why:
#      We want to remove Beads rows later, but keep beads metrics for QC/dedup.
#    Grouping:
#      Use the NEW identifiers (Sample_ID + Unique_code + InEx) + run timestamps.
# ==============================================================================
fcm_stained_final2 <- fcm_stained_final %>%
  group_by(`Project name`, Sample_ID, Unique_code, InEx, `Analysis date`, `Start time`, `End time`) %>%
  mutate(
    Beads_FSC_median          = first(FSC_median[Group == "Beads"]),
    Beads_Concentration_uL    = first(Concentration_uL[Group == "Beads"])
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  relocate(Beads_FSC_median, .after = FSC_median) %>%
  relocate(Beads_Concentration_uL, .after = Concentration_uL)

# ==============================================================================
# 7) Remove Beads rows (Beads kept as reference columns)
# ==============================================================================
fcm_stained_noBeads <- fcm_stained_final2 %>%
  filter(Group != "Beads")

# ==============================================================================
# 8) Enforce consistent data types
# ==============================================================================
fcm_stained_typed <- fcm_stained_noBeads %>%
  mutate(
    # IDs / labels
    FCS_file        = as.character(FCS_file),
    `Project name`  = as.character(`Project name`),
    `Analysis date` = as.character(`Analysis date`),

    Sample_ID       = as.character(Sample_ID),
    Unique_code     = as.character(Unique_code),
    InEx            = as.character(InEx),
    Group           = as.character(Group),

    # Excel clock times (date reconstructed later)
    `Start time` = as.POSIXct(`Start time`, origin = "1899-12-30", tz = "UTC"),
    `End time`   = as.POSIXct(`End time`,   origin = "1899-12-30", tz = "UTC"),

    # numeric
    `Total volume (nL)`       = as.numeric(`Total volume (nL)`),
    `Flow Rate (ul/min)`      = as.numeric(`Flow Rate (ul/min)`),
    Events                    = as.numeric(Events),
    Volume_uL                 = as.numeric(Volume_uL),
    Concentration_uL          = as.numeric(Concentration_uL),
    Beads_Concentration_uL    = as.numeric(Beads_Concentration_uL),
    FSC_median                = as.numeric(FSC_median),
    Beads_FSC_median          = as.numeric(Beads_FSC_median),
    SSC_median                = as.numeric(SSC_median)
  )

# ==============================================================================
# 9) Time reconstruction: Hebrew Analysis date + Start/End clock times
# ==============================================================================
he_months <- c(
  "ינו"="01","פבר"="02","מרץ"="03","אפר"="04",
  "מאי"="05","יונ"="06","יול"="07","אוג"="08",
  "ספט"="09","אוק"="10","נוב"="11","דצמ"="12"
)

fcm_stained_time_fixed <- fcm_stained_typed %>%
  mutate(
    day      = str_extract(`Analysis date`, "^\\d{1,2}"),
    month_he = str_extract(`Analysis date`, "(?<=-)[^-]+(?=-)"),
    year     = str_extract(`Analysis date`, "\\d{4}$"),
    month    = unname(he_months[month_he]),

    Analysis_Date = as.Date(paste(year, month, day, sep = "-")),

    Start_clock = format(`Start time`, "%H:%M:%S"),
    End_clock   = format(`End time`,   "%H:%M:%S"),

    Start_DateTime = ymd_hms(paste(Analysis_Date, Start_clock), tz = "UTC"),
    End_DateTime   = ymd_hms(paste(Analysis_Date, End_clock),   tz = "UTC"),

    Run_duration_min = as.numeric(difftime(End_DateTime, Start_DateTime, units = "mins"))
  )

# QC duration (optional)
# summary(fcm_stained_time_fixed$Run_duration_min)
# fcm_stained_time_fixed %>% filter(Run_duration_min < 0 | Run_duration_min > 30)

# ==============================================================================
# 10) Final stained table: drop helper columns + final ordering
# ==============================================================================
fcm_stained_final_time <- fcm_stained_time_fixed %>%
  select(
    FCS_file,
    `Project name`,
    Analysis_Date,
    Start_DateTime,
    End_DateTime,
    `Total volume (nL)`,
    `Flow Rate (ul/min)`,

    Sample_ID,
    Unique_code,
    InEx,

    Group,
    Volume_uL,
    Concentration_uL,
    Beads_Concentration_uL,
    FSC_median,
    Beads_FSC_median,
    SSC_median,
    Run_duration_min
  ) %>%
  relocate(Sample_ID, Unique_code, InEx, Group, .before = Volume_uL)

str(fcm_stained_final_time)
################################################################################
# EARLY HARD FIX — remove STAINED Bact rows for specific Unique_code
# (and fail loudly if anything remains)
################################################################################

library(dplyr)

# 1) Define the problematic Unique_code (THE REAL ONES)
bad_uc <- c("10.10.1", "10.13.1", "10.32.1", "13.54.1")

# 2) Normalize text (prevents hidden spaces / encoding issues)
fcm_stained_final_time <- fcm_stained_final_time %>%
  mutate(
    Unique_code = trimws(as.character(Unique_code)),
    InEx        = toupper(trimws(as.character(InEx))),
    Group       = trimws(as.character(Group))
  )

bad_uc_clean <- trimws(as.character(bad_uc))

# 3) QC BEFORE
qc_before <- fcm_stained_final_time %>%
  filter(Unique_code %in% bad_uc_clean, Group == "Bact") %>%
  count(Unique_code, InEx, name = "n_rows") %>%
  arrange(Unique_code, InEx)

message("QC BEFORE removal:")
print(qc_before, n = Inf)

# 4) REMOVE (only stained table; only Bact; only those Unique_code)
fcm_stained_final_time <- fcm_stained_final_time %>%
  filter(!(Unique_code %in% bad_uc_clean & Group == "Bact"))

# 5) QC AFTER + hard fail if anything remains
qc_after <- fcm_stained_final_time %>%
  filter(Unique_code %in% bad_uc_clean, Group == "Bact") %>%
  count(Unique_code, InEx, name = "n_rows") %>%
  arrange(Unique_code, InEx)

message("QC AFTER removal:")
print(qc_after, n = Inf)

if (nrow(qc_after) > 0) {
  stop("❌ Removal failed: some bad Unique_code are still present in fcm_stained_final_time (Bact).")
} else {
  message("✅ Removal succeeded: bad Unique_code removed from stained Bact.")
}

################################################################################
# End
################################################################################

```


### FCM integration → Δ(IN−EX) per Unique_code × Group
```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=10, results='asis'}

################################################################################
# FCM integration → Δ(IN−EX) per Unique_code × Group
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GOAL
#   1) Merge Unstained + Stained into ONE *full* dataset (KEEP ALL COLUMNS):
#        fcm_all_full
#   2) In parallel, compute Δ(IN−EX) in Concentration_uL per Unique_code × Group:
#        fcm_all_delta -> fcm_collapsed -> fcm_delta_ok
#
# IMPORTANT (UPDATED TO YOUR CURRENT PIPELINE)
#   - Unstained final table: fcm_unstained_final
#   - Stained final table  : fcm_stained_final_time
#
# NOTE:
#   Your "volume/flow" column names are:
#     - `Total volume (nL)`
#     - `Flow Rate (ul/min)`
#   (NOT Total_Volume_nL / FlowRate_uL_min)
################################################################################

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0) Libraries + safety checks
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)

stopifnot(exists("fcm_stained_final_time"))
stopifnot(exists("fcm_unstained_final"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
pick_first_existing <- function(df, candidates) {
  hit <- candidates[candidates %in% names(df)]
  if (length(hit) == 0) NA_character_ else hit[1]
}
num <- function(x) suppressWarnings(readr::parse_number(as.character(x)))

# ==============================================================================
# 1) Unstained: already standardized to stained-like schema
# ==============================================================================
fcm_unstained_clean <- fcm_unstained_final %>%
  mutate(Project_type = "Unstained")

# Hard QC: ensure minimum set exists (UPDATED NAMES)
req_unstained <- c(
  "Project name","Analysis_Date","Start_DateTime","End_DateTime","Run_duration_min",
  "Total volume (nL)","Flow Rate (ul/min)",
  "Sample_ID","Unique_code","InEx","Group","Concentration_uL"
)
miss_un <- setdiff(req_unstained, names(fcm_unstained_clean))
if (length(miss_un) > 0) {
  stop("Unstained missing required columns: ", paste(miss_un, collapse = ", "))
}

# ==============================================================================
# 2) Stained: harmonize to the same schema (robust to different headers)
# ==============================================================================
dfS <- fcm_stained_final_time

# Candidate pools (include both styles just in case)
cand_project <- c("Project name","Project.name","Project.name_x0000_","Project")
cand_date    <- c("Analysis_Date","Analysis date","Analysis.date","Analysis.date_x0000_")
cand_startdt <- c("Start_DateTime","Start DateTime","Start_Date_Time","Start")
cand_enddt   <- c("End_DateTime","End DateTime","End_Date_Time","End")
cand_sample  <- c("Sample_ID","Sample ID","Sample.ID","Sample")
cand_uc      <- c("Unique_code","unique_code")
cand_inex    <- c("InEx","INEX","inex")
cand_group   <- c("Group","Taxon","FCM_Group")
cand_conc    <- c("Concentration_uL","Concentration (cells/uL)","Conc_uL","Concentration")

# IMPORTANT: your stained file uses these exact names:
cand_vol     <- c("Total volume (nL)","Total_Volume_nL","Total.Volume.(nL)","Total Volume  (nL)","Total volume (nL)")
cand_flow    <- c("Flow Rate (ul/min)","FlowRate_uL_min","Flow.Rate.(ul/min)_x0000_","Flow Rate (uL/min)")
cand_durmin  <- c("Run_duration_min","Run duration (min)","duration_min","Duration_min")
cand_dursec  <- c("duration_sec","duration_s","Duration_sec")

# Pick actual columns (if present)
nm_project <- pick_first_existing(dfS, cand_project)
nm_date    <- pick_first_existing(dfS, cand_date)
nm_startdt <- pick_first_existing(dfS, cand_startdt)
nm_enddt   <- pick_first_existing(dfS, cand_enddt)
nm_sample  <- pick_first_existing(dfS, cand_sample)
nm_uc      <- pick_first_existing(dfS, cand_uc)
nm_inex    <- pick_first_existing(dfS, cand_inex)
nm_group   <- pick_first_existing(dfS, cand_group)
nm_conc    <- pick_first_existing(dfS, cand_conc)
nm_vol     <- pick_first_existing(dfS, cand_vol)
nm_flow    <- pick_first_existing(dfS, cand_flow)
nm_durmin  <- pick_first_existing(dfS, cand_durmin)
nm_dursec  <- pick_first_existing(dfS, cand_dursec)

# Build standardized stained table
fcm_stained_clean <- dfS %>%
  mutate(
    Project_type = "Stained",

    `Project name` = if (!is.na(nm_project)) as.character(.data[[nm_project]]) else NA_character_,

    Analysis_Date = if (!is.na(nm_date)) suppressWarnings(as.Date(.data[[nm_date]])) else NA,
    Start_DateTime = if (!is.na(nm_startdt)) suppressWarnings(as.POSIXct(.data[[nm_startdt]], tz = "UTC")) else NA,
    End_DateTime   = if (!is.na(nm_enddt))   suppressWarnings(as.POSIXct(.data[[nm_enddt]],   tz = "UTC")) else NA,

    Sample_ID   = if (!is.na(nm_sample)) as.character(.data[[nm_sample]]) else NA_character_,
    Unique_code = if (!is.na(nm_uc))     as.character(.data[[nm_uc]])     else NA_character_,
    InEx        = if (!is.na(nm_inex))   toupper(as.character(.data[[nm_inex]])) else NA_character_,
    Group       = if (!is.na(nm_group))  as.character(.data[[nm_group]]) else NA_character_,

    Concentration_uL = if (!is.na(nm_conc)) as.numeric(.data[[nm_conc]]) else NA_real_,

    # Keep stained naming as in your table:
    `Total volume (nL)`  = if (!is.na(nm_vol))  num(.data[[nm_vol]])  else NA_real_,
    `Flow Rate (ul/min)` = if (!is.na(nm_flow)) num(.data[[nm_flow]]) else NA_real_,

    Run_duration_min = dplyr::coalesce(
      if (!is.na(nm_durmin)) as.numeric(.data[[nm_durmin]]) else NA_real_,
      if (!is.na(nm_dursec)) as.numeric(.data[[nm_dursec]]) / 60 else NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_ID   = str_trim(Sample_ID),
    Unique_code = str_trim(Unique_code),
    InEx        = toupper(str_trim(InEx)),
    Group       = str_trim(Group)
  )

# Hard QC: stained must have keys needed for delta
req_stained <- c("Sample_ID","Unique_code","InEx","Group","Concentration_uL")
miss_st <- setdiff(req_stained, names(fcm_stained_clean))
if (length(miss_st) > 0) {
  stop("Stained missing required columns after harmonization: ", paste(miss_st, collapse = ", "))
}

# ==============================================================================
# 3) FULL merged dataset (KEEP ALL COLUMNS)
# ==============================================================================
fcm_all_full <- bind_rows(fcm_unstained_clean, fcm_stained_clean)

qc_full <- fcm_all_full %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    n_unstained = sum(Project_type == "Unstained"),
    n_stained   = sum(Project_type == "Stained"),
    n_missing_uc = sum(is.na(Unique_code) | Unique_code == ""),
    n_missing_inex = sum(is.na(InEx) | InEx == ""),
    n_missing_group = sum(is.na(Group) | Group == "")
  )
print(qc_full)

# ==============================================================================
# 4) Reduced table ONLY for delta analysis
# ==============================================================================
fcm_all_delta <- fcm_all_full %>%
  select(
    Project_type, Analysis_Date, Start_DateTime, End_DateTime,
    Sample_ID, Unique_code, InEx, Group, Concentration_uL
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_ID        = str_trim(as.character(Sample_ID)),
    Unique_code      = str_trim(as.character(Unique_code)),
    InEx             = toupper(str_trim(as.character(InEx))),
    Group            = str_trim(as.character(Group)),
    Concentration_uL = as.numeric(Concentration_uL)
  )

bad_keys <- fcm_all_delta %>%
  filter(is.na(Unique_code) | Unique_code == "" | is.na(InEx) | InEx == "" | is.na(Group) | Group == "")
if (nrow(bad_keys) > 0) {
  message("WARNING: rows with missing Unique_code/InEx/Group will be excluded from delta:")
  print(bad_keys %>% distinct(Project_type, Sample_ID, Unique_code, InEx, Group) %>% head(200), n = 200)
}

# ==============================================================================
# 5) Collapse technical replicates + compute Δ(IN−EX)
# ==============================================================================
fcm_collapsed <- fcm_all_delta %>%
  filter(!is.na(Unique_code), Unique_code != "",
         !is.na(InEx), InEx != "",
         !is.na(Group), Group != "") %>%
  group_by(Unique_code, Group, InEx) %>%
  summarise(
    Concentration_uL = median(Concentration_uL, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_rep            = sum(!is.na(Concentration_uL)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

fcm_delta_ok <- fcm_collapsed %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = InEx,
    values_from = Concentration_uL,
    values_fn   = median,
    values_fill = NA_real_
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(IN), !is.na(EX)) %>%
  mutate(
    Delta_IN_minus_EX = IN - EX,
    Delta_pct         = (Delta_IN_minus_EX / IN) * 100
  )

cat("✓ fcm_delta_ok rows:", nrow(fcm_delta_ok), "\n")
print(summary(fcm_delta_ok$Delta_IN_minus_EX))


# ==============================================================================
# 6) Plots – publication style: Δ with inset + Δ%
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# You already defined these in your script; keeping here self-contained:
group_order <- c("Bact", "Pro", "Syn", "PEuk", "NEuk", "Euk")
group_cols <- c(
  "Bact" = "#F4A6A6",
  "Pro"  = "#9DBCE3",
  "Syn"  = "#F6C28B",
  "Euk"  = "#A8D5BA",
  "PEuk" = "#CDEB9D",
  "NEuk" = "#7FBF9B"
)

plot_df <- fcm_delta_ok %>%
  filter(!is.na(Group), !is.na(Delta_IN_minus_EX)) %>%
  mutate(
    Group = str_trim(as.character(Group)),
    Group = factor(Group, levels = group_order)
  )

plot_df_pct <- fcm_delta_ok %>%
  filter(!is.na(Group), !is.na(Delta_pct)) %>%
  mutate(
    Group = str_trim(as.character(Group)),
    Group = factor(Group, levels = group_order)
  )

# You control these:
y_full <- c(-50, 200)     # main Δ plot range
y_zoom <- c(-10, 10)    # inset range around 0
y_pct  <- c(-100, 100)    # Δ% range

# Main Δ plot
p_main <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Group, y = Delta_IN_minus_EX, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, linewidth = 1.1, color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Group), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.55, size = 1.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = y_full) +
  labs(
    x = "FCM Group",
    y = expression(Delta*"IN-EX [cells/"*mu*"L]")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Inset Δ plot
p_inset <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Group, y = Delta_IN_minus_EX, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, linewidth = 1.0, color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Group), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.55, size = 1.2, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = y_zoom) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title = element_blank()
  )

# Combine main + inset
p_delta_with_inset <- ggdraw(p_main) +
  draw_plot(p_inset, x = 0.55, y = 0.55, width = 0.4, height = 0.4)

p_delta_with_inset

# Δ% plot
p_delta_pct <- ggplot(plot_df_pct, aes(x = Group, y = Delta_pct, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, linewidth = 1.1, color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Group), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.55, size = 1.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = y_pct) +
  labs(
    x = "FCM Group",
    y = expression(Delta*"IN-EX / IN [%]")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p_delta_pct

# ==============================================================================
# 7) Save figures
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/FCS files"
out_dir <- normalizePath(out_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "FCM_Delta_IN_minus_EX_with_inset.png"),
  plot     = p_delta_with_inset,
  width    = 18, height = 12, units = "cm", dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "FCM_Delta_percent_boxplot.png"),
  plot     = p_delta_pct,
  width    = 18, height = 12, units = "cm", dpi = 300
)

################################################################################
# End
################################################################################
```





### # FCM QC — Audit negative Δ (IN − EX) + attach Sample_IDs + apply new rules
```{r}
################################################################################
# FCM QC — Audit negative Δ (IN − EX) + attach Sample_IDs + apply new rules
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# NEW RULES (as you defined):
#   Rule A (DROP WHOLE Unique_code):
#     - If Delta_IN_minus_EX < 0 in Syn OR Bact  -> sponge "not working"
#     - Drop the entire Unique_code (all Groups) from downstream
#
#   Rule B (DROP ROW ONLY):
#     - If Delta_IN_minus_EX < 0 in Pro / PEuk / NEuk / Euk
#     - Drop ONLY that specific (Unique_code × Group) row (not the whole sample)
#
# OUTPUTS:
#   - neg_audit_tbl        : full audit table of negative cases + Sample_IDs (IN/EX)
#   - drop_whole_uc        : Unique_codes to drop entirely
#   - drop_row_uc_group    : (Unique_code × Group) rows to drop only
#   - fcm_delta_ok_qc      : filtered delta table after applying both rules
#   - suspicious_audit     : negatives with missing IN/EX IDs or multiple runs
#   - Saved CSVs in out_dir
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0) Safety checks
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
stopifnot(exists("fcm_delta_ok"))
stopifnot(exists("fcm_all_full"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) User settings (rules)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
drop_whole_groups <- c("Syn", "Bact")
drop_row_groups   <- c("Pro", "PEuk", "NEuk", "Euk")

out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/FCS files"
out_dir <- normalizePath(out_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Standardize keys in both tables
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
delta_tbl <- fcm_delta_ok %>%
  transmute(
    Group = str_trim(as.character(Group)),
    Unique_code = str_trim(as.character(Unique_code)),
    IN, EX, Delta_IN_minus_EX, Delta_pct
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Group), Group != "",
         !is.na(Unique_code), Unique_code != "",
         is.finite(Delta_IN_minus_EX))

long_tbl <- fcm_all_full %>%
  transmute(
    Project_type = if ("Project_type" %in% names(.)) as.character(Project_type) else NA_character_,
    Group        = str_trim(as.character(Group)),
    Unique_code  = str_trim(as.character(Unique_code)),
    InEx         = toupper(str_trim(as.character(InEx))),
    Sample_ID    = str_trim(as.character(Sample_ID))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Group), Group != "",
         !is.na(Unique_code), Unique_code != "",
         InEx %in% c("IN","EX"),
         !is.na(Sample_ID), Sample_ID != "") %>%
  distinct(Project_type, Group, Unique_code, InEx, Sample_ID)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Find all negative delta cases (for ALL groups)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
neg_base_all <- delta_tbl %>%
  filter(Delta_IN_minus_EX < 0) %>%
  arrange(Group, Unique_code)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Apply rules: build two "drop lists"
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rule A: drop whole Unique_code if Syn/Bact negative
drop_whole_uc <- neg_base_all %>%
  filter(Group %in% drop_whole_groups) %>%
  distinct(Unique_code) %>%
  arrange(Unique_code)

# Rule B: drop only specific rows if Pro/Euk negative
drop_row_uc_group <- neg_base_all %>%
  filter(Group %in% drop_row_groups) %>%
  distinct(Unique_code, Group) %>%
  arrange(Group, Unique_code)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Build Sample_ID list per Unique_code × Group × InEx from LONG table
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
sample_ids_by_inex <- long_tbl %>%
  group_by(Project_type, Group, Unique_code, InEx) %>%
  summarise(
    Sample_IDs = paste(sort(unique(Sample_ID)), collapse = ", "),
    n_runs     = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = InEx,
    values_from = c(Sample_IDs, n_runs),
    names_sep   = "_"
  ) %>%
  rename(
    Sample_ID_IN = Sample_IDs_IN,
    Sample_ID_EX = Sample_IDs_EX,
    n_runs_IN    = n_runs_IN,
    n_runs_EX    = n_runs_EX
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) Full audit table: negatives + Sample_IDs + reason flags
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
neg_audit_tbl <- neg_base_all %>%
  left_join(sample_ids_by_inex, by = c("Group","Unique_code")) %>%
  mutate(
    rule_action = case_when(
      Group %in% drop_whole_groups ~ "DROP_WHOLE_UNIQUE_CODE",
      Group %in% drop_row_groups   ~ "DROP_ROW_ONLY",
      TRUE                         ~ "NEGATIVE_OTHER_GROUP"
    ),
    missing_pair = is.na(Sample_ID_IN) | is.na(Sample_ID_EX),
    multi_runs   = (coalesce(n_runs_IN, 0) > 1) | (coalesce(n_runs_EX, 0) > 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(rule_action, Group, Delta_IN_minus_EX)

suspicious_audit <- neg_audit_tbl %>%
  filter(missing_pair | multi_runs)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7) Apply filters to build QC-clean delta table
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
fcm_delta_ok_qc <- delta_tbl %>%
  # drop whole samples first (removes ALL groups for those unique_codes)
  anti_join(drop_whole_uc, by = "Unique_code") %>%
  # then drop specific rows
  anti_join(drop_row_uc_group, by = c("Unique_code","Group"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 8) Report + save outputs
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("Negative cases total:", nrow(neg_base_all), "\n")
cat("DROP WHOLE unique_code (Syn/Bact negative):", nrow(drop_whole_uc), "\n")
cat("DROP ROW ONLY (Pro/PEuk/NEuk/Euk negative):", nrow(drop_row_uc_group), "\n")
cat("Suspicious negatives (missing IN/EX IDs or multiple runs):", nrow(suspicious_audit), "\n")
cat("Rows before QC:", nrow(delta_tbl), "\n")
cat("Rows after  QC:", nrow(fcm_delta_ok_qc), "\n")

#write_csv(neg_audit_tbl,        file.path(out_dir, "FCM_Negative_Delta_Audit_ALLGROUPS.csv"))
#write_csv(drop_whole_uc,        file.path(out_dir, "FCM_DropWhole_UniqueCodes_SynBact.csv"))
#write_csv(drop_row_uc_group,    file.path(out_dir, "FCM_DropRowsOnly_ProEuk.csv"))
#write_csv(suspicious_audit,     file.path(out_dir, "FCM_Negative_Delta_Suspicious.csv"))
#write_csv(fcm_delta_ok_qc,      file.path(out_dir, "FCM_Delta_Table_QCclean.csv"))

# Show key tables (optional)
drop_whole_uc
drop_row_uc_group
neg_audit_tbl
suspicious_audit
```
### FCM Δ plots AFTER QC cleaning (uses fcm_delta_ok_qc from the new rules chunk)
```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=10}
################################################################################
# FCM Δ plots AFTER QC cleaning (uses fcm_delta_ok_qc from the new rules chunk)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# REQUIREMENTS:
#   - fcm_delta_ok_qc must exist (output of the "FCM QC — Audit negative Δ..." chunk)
#
# OUTPUT:
#   - p_delta_with_inset_qc
#   - p_delta_pct_qc
#   - Saved PNGs with "_QC" suffix
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)

stopifnot(exists("fcm_delta_ok_qc"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Settings (keep same palette/order)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
group_order <- c("Bact", "Pro", "Syn", "PEuk", "NEuk", "Euk")
group_cols <- c(
  "Bact" = "#F4A6A6",
  "Pro"  = "#9DBCE3",
  "Syn"  = "#F6C28B",
  "Euk"  = "#A8D5BA",
  "PEuk" = "#CDEB9D",
  "NEuk" = "#7FBF9B"
)

# You control these:
y_full <- c(0, 200)     # main Δ plot range
y_zoom <- c(0, 10)      # inset range around 0
y_pct  <- c(-100, 100)    # Δ% range

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Prepare plot data FROM QC-clean table
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
plot_df <- fcm_delta_ok_qc %>%
  mutate(
    Group = str_trim(as.character(Group)),
    Group = factor(Group, levels = group_order)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Group), !is.na(Delta_IN_minus_EX), is.finite(Delta_IN_minus_EX))

plot_df_pct <- fcm_delta_ok_qc %>%
  mutate(
    Group = str_trim(as.character(Group)),
    Group = factor(Group, levels = group_order)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Group), !is.na(Delta_pct), is.finite(Delta_pct))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main Δ plot
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_main_qc <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Group, y = Delta_IN_minus_EX, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, linewidth = 1.1, color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Group), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.55, size = 1.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = y_full) +
  labs(
    x = "FCM Group",
    y = expression(Delta*"IN-EX [cells/"*mu*"L]")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Inset Δ plot
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_inset_qc <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Group, y = Delta_IN_minus_EX, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, linewidth = 1.0, color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Group), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.55, size = 1.2, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = y_zoom) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title = element_blank()
  )

# Combine main + inset
p_delta_with_inset_qc <- ggdraw(p_main_qc) +
  draw_plot(p_inset_qc, x = 0.55, y = 0.55, width = 0.4, height = 0.4)

p_delta_with_inset_qc

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Δ% plot
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_delta_pct_qc <- ggplot(plot_df_pct, aes(x = Group, y = Delta_pct, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, linewidth = 1.1, color = "black") +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Group), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.55, size = 1.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = y_pct) +
  labs(
    x = "FCM Group",
    y = expression(Delta*"IN-EX / IN [%]")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p_delta_pct_qc

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Save figures (QC versions)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/FCS files"
out_dir <- normalizePath(out_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "FCM_Delta_IN_minus_EX_with_inset_QC.png"),
  plot     = p_delta_with_inset_qc,
  width    = 18, height = 12, units = "cm", dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "FCM_Delta_percent_boxplot_QC.png"),
  plot     = p_delta_pct_qc,
  width    = 18, height = 12, units = "cm", dpi = 300
)

################################################################################
# End
################################################################################
```
### FCM sizing AFTER QC (Option A): add FSC_ratio, Diameter_um, Volume_um3

```{r}
################################################################################
# FCM sizing AFTER QC (Option A): add FSC_ratio, Diameter_um, Volume_um3
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Option A meaning:
#   - If Syn/Bact Delta < 0  -> DROP WHOLE Unique_code (all taxa, IN+EX)
#   - If Pro/PEuk/NEuk/Euk Delta < 0 -> DROP ONLY that (Unique_code × Group)
#   - Then compute sizing on the QC-filtered long table
#
# Requires (already built in previous chunks):
#   - fcm_all_full
#   - drop_whole_uc
#   - drop_row_uc_group
#
# Output:
#   - fcm_all_full_qc   : long FCM after QC removal rules (Option A)
#   - fcm_all_size      : (same name as downstream pipeline expects) with sizing cols
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

stopifnot(exists("fcm_all_full"))
stopifnot(exists("drop_whole_uc"))
stopifnot(exists("drop_row_uc_group"))

# -----------------------------
# USER SETTING
# -----------------------------
bead_diameter_um <- 1  # beads are 1 µm

# -----------------------------
# QC: required columns exist
# -----------------------------
req <- c("FSC_median", "Beads_FSC_median", "Group", "Unique_code", "InEx")
miss <- setdiff(req, names(fcm_all_full))
if (length(miss) > 0) {
  stop("fcm_all_full missing required columns for sizing: ", paste(miss, collapse=", "))
}

# -----------------------------
# Normalize drop lists
# -----------------------------
drop_whole_uc2 <- drop_whole_uc %>%
  transmute(Unique_code = str_trim(as.character(Unique_code))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Unique_code), Unique_code != "") %>%
  distinct()

drop_row_uc_group2 <- drop_row_uc_group %>%
  transmute(
    Unique_code = str_trim(as.character(Unique_code)),
    Group       = str_trim(as.character(Group))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Unique_code), Unique_code != "",
         !is.na(Group), Group != "") %>%
  distinct()

# -----------------------------
# Build QC-filtered LONG table (Option A)
# -----------------------------
fcm_all_full_qc <- fcm_all_full %>%
  mutate(
    Unique_code = str_trim(as.character(Unique_code)),
    Group       = str_trim(as.character(Group)),
    InEx        = toupper(str_trim(as.character(InEx)))
  ) %>%
  # Rule A: drop whole sample
  anti_join(drop_whole_uc2, by = "Unique_code") %>%
  # Rule B: drop only the specific taxon rows (both IN and EX)
  anti_join(drop_row_uc_group2, by = c("Unique_code", "Group"))

# -----------------------------
# SIZING on QC-filtered table
# -----------------------------
fcm_all_size <- fcm_all_full_qc %>%
  mutate(
    FSC_ratio = FSC_median / Beads_FSC_median,

    Diameter_um = case_when(
      is.na(FSC_ratio) ~ NA_real_,
      FSC_ratio <= 0   ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE             ~ bead_diameter_um * FSC_ratio
    ),

    # Sphere volume: V = (pi/6) * d^3   [µm^3]
    Volume_um3 = case_when(
      is.na(Diameter_um) ~ NA_real_,
      Diameter_um <= 0   ~ NA_real_,
      TRUE               ~ (pi / 6) * (Diameter_um^3)
    )
  )

# -----------------------------
# QC quick summary
# -----------------------------
qc_size <- fcm_all_size %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    n_unique_codes = n_distinct(Unique_code),
    n_na_beads_fsc = sum(is.na(Beads_FSC_median)),
    n_na_fsc_ratio = sum(is.na(FSC_ratio)),
    n_na_diameter  = sum(is.na(Diameter_um)),
    n_na_volume    = sum(is.na(Volume_um3))
  )

print(qc_size)

# Optional: typical ranges by group
fcm_all_size %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarise(
    n = sum(!is.na(Volume_um3)),
    median_FSC_ratio = median(FSC_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_d_um      = median(Diameter_um, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_v_um3     = median(Volume_um3, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups="drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  print(n = 50)


################################################################################
# QC PLOTS — size distributions by Group (Diameter + Volume) — log scale
################################################################################

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

stopifnot(exists("fcm_all_size"))

group_cols <- c(
  "Bact" = "#F4A6A6",
  "Pro"  = "#9DBCE3",
  "Syn"  = "#F6C28B",
  "Euk"  = "#A8D5BA",
  "PEuk" = "#CDEB9D",
  "NEuk" = "#7FBF9B"
)

plot_size_df <- fcm_all_size %>%
  mutate(
    Group = factor(str_trim(as.character(Group)), levels = names(group_cols))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Group),
         is.finite(Diameter_um), Diameter_um > 0,
         is.finite(Volume_um3),  Volume_um3 > 0)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Diameter (µm) — log10
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_diam <- ggplot(plot_size_df, aes(x = Group, y = Diameter_um, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.25, size = 1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = scales::label_number()) +
  labs(
    x = "FCM Group",
    y = expression(paste("Estimated diameter (", mu, "m, log scale)")),
    title = "Cell diameter distribution by FCM group"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p_diam

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Volume (µm^3) — log10
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_vol <- ggplot(plot_size_df, aes(x = Group, y = Volume_um3, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.3, linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.25, size = 1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = scales::label_number()) +
  labs(
    x = "FCM Group",
    y = expression(paste("Estimated volume (", mu, "m"^3, ", log scale)")),
    title = "Cell volume distribution by FCM group"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p_vol

# Optional save
out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/FCS files"
out_dir <- normalizePath(out_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "FCM_Size_Diameter_by_Group_boxplot.png"),
       plot = p_diam, width = 18, height = 12, units = "cm", dpi = 300)

ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "FCM_Size_Volume_by_Group_boxplot.png"),
       plot = p_vol, width = 18, height = 12, units = "cm", dpi = 300)

################################################################################
# End
################################################################################

```



### Carbon/Nitrogen content conversion (µmol/L) using literature factors + Scenario builder (fixed Oceanic/Coastal + allometric for photo taxa)
```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=10}

################################################################################
# FCM → Carbon/Nitrogen content conversion (µmol/L)
# using literature conversion factors + scenario builder
#
# PURPOSE
# -------
# Convert FCM cell concentration data into Carbon/Nitrogen content (µmol/L),
# based on literature-derived conversion factors.
#
# INPUTS
# ------
# 1) fcm_all_size
#    Must already exist in the environment.
#    Required columns:
#      - Group            : taxonomic group (e.g., Bact, Pro, Syn, PEuk, NEuk)
#      - Concentration_uL : cell concentration in cells/µL
#      - Volume_um3       : cell volume in µm^3
#      - Unique_code      : sample identifier
#      - InEx             : IN / EX
#
# 2) Excel file: Conversion factors from literature.xlsx
#    Sheet 2 = fixed per-cell content (Oceanic / Coastal)
#    Sheet 3 = allometric relationships (content as function of cell volume)
#
# SCENARIOS
# ---------
# S1: fixed Oceanic (Sheet2 fg/cell)
# S2: fixed Coastal (Sheet2 fg/cell)
# S3: allometric for photo taxa (Sheet3) + Bact fixed Coastal (Sheet2)
# S4: allometric for photo taxa (Sheet3) + Bact fixed Oceanic (Sheet2)
# S5: fixed Coastal Carbon (Sheet2) + Redfield Nitrogen (N = C/6.6 molar)
# S6: allometric only for NEuk (Sheet3) + all other taxa fixed Coastal (Sheet2)
#
# OUTPUTS
# -------
# - fcm_scenarios   : long table with all scenarios and umol_L values
# - summary_by_taxon
# - summary_total
# - QC plots
#
# IMPORTANT ASSUMPTIONS
# ---------------------
# - Concentration_uL is in cells/µL
# - Volume_um3 is cell volume in µm^3
# - Sheet2 values are in fg/cell unless changed below
# - Sheet3 coefficient + exponent define:
#       fg_per_cell = coeff_fg_um3 * (Volume_um3 ^ power)
################################################################################

# ==============================================================================
# 0) Libraries
# ==============================================================================
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)

# ==============================================================================
# 1) SETTINGS
# ==============================================================================
conv_path <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/FCS files/Conversion factors from literature.xlsx"
out_dir   <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/FCS files"

# Taxa used in scenarios
taxa_photo <- c("Pro", "Syn", "PEuk", "NEuk")   # taxa treated allometrically in S3/S4
taxa_bact  <- "Bact"                            # bacteria handled separately in S3/S4
taxa_s6_allometric <- "NEuk"                    # only NEuk is allometric in S6

# Sheet2 unit:
# if Sheet2 values are in fg/cell -> keep "fg"
# if they are in pg/cell          -> use "pg" and convert to fg
sheet2_unit <- "fg"   # choose: "fg" or "pg"

# Redfield molar C:N ratio for S5
redfield_CN <- 6.6

# Convert Sheet2 values to fg if needed
to_fg <- function(x) {
  if (sheet2_unit == "pg") x * 1000 else x
}

# Safety checks
stopifnot(exists("fcm_all_size"))
stopifnot(file.exists(conv_path))

# ==============================================================================
# 2) HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ==============================================================================

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Return atomic weight for Carbon or Nitrogen
# Used to convert mass units into molar units
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
atomic_weight <- function(element) {
  element <- tolower(as.character(element))
  ifelse(grepl("carbon|^c$", element), 12,
         ifelse(grepl("nitrogen|^n$", element), 14, NA_real_))
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Convert fg/cell into µmol/L
#
# INPUT columns required:
#   - fg_per_cell
#   - cells_mL
#   - Element
#
# Logic:
#   fg_per_cell * cells_mL = fg/mL
#   fg/mL -> fg/L          = *1000
#   fg -> g                = *1e-15
#   g -> mol               = / atomic_weight
#   mol -> µmol            = *1e6
#
# Combined conversion simplifies to:
#   umol_L = (fg_per_mL * 1e-6) / atomic_weight
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
fgcell_to_umolL <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      fg_per_mL = fg_per_cell * cells_mL,
      umol_L    = (fg_per_mL * 1e-6) / atomic_weight(Element)
    )
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Detect a column name by regex pattern
# Useful when Excel sheet names are slightly inconsistent
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
detect_col <- function(nms, pattern, label) {
  hit <- nms[str_detect(nms, regex(pattern, ignore_case = TRUE))]
  if (length(hit) == 0) {
    stop(
      "Could not detect ", label, " column. Pattern: ", pattern,
      "\nAvailable columns: ", paste(nms, collapse = " | ")
    )
  }
  hit[1]
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Calculate allometric fg_per_cell from Sheet3 coefficients
# Formula:
#   fg_per_cell = coeff_fg_um3 * Volume_um3^power
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
calc_allometric_fg_cell <- function(df, sheet3_filled) {
  df %>%
    left_join(sheet3_filled, by = c("Taxon", "Element")) %>%
    mutate(
      fg_per_cell = coeff_fg_um3 * (Volume_um3 ^ power)
    )
}

# ==============================================================================
# 3) BUILD BASE FCM TABLE
# ==============================================================================
# Validate required columns
req_base <- c("Group", "Concentration_uL", "Volume_um3", "Unique_code", "InEx")
miss_base <- setdiff(req_base, names(fcm_all_size))

if (length(miss_base) > 0) {
  stop("fcm_all_size is missing required columns: ", paste(miss_base, collapse = ", "))
}

# Keep all useful identifiers that happen to exist in your real table
id_candidates <- c(
  "Project_type", "Project name", "Analysis_Date",
  "Start_DateTime", "End_DateTime",
  "Sample_ID", "Unique_code", "InEx", "Run_duration_min",
  "Total volume (nL)", "Flow Rate (ul/min)",
  "Total_Volume_nL", "FlowRate_uL_min"
)
id_cols <- intersect(names(fcm_all_size), id_candidates)

# Build clean long base table
fcm_base <- fcm_all_size %>%
  mutate(
    Taxon      = str_trim(as.character(Group)),
    InEx       = toupper(str_trim(as.character(InEx))),
    cells_mL   = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(Concentration_uL)) * 1000, # cells/µL -> cells/mL
    Volume_um3 = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(Volume_um3))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    InEx %in% c("IN", "EX"),
    !is.na(Taxon), Taxon != "",
    is.finite(cells_mL), cells_mL >= 0,
    is.finite(Volume_um3), Volume_um3 > 0
  ) %>%
  select(all_of(id_cols), Taxon, cells_mL, Volume_um3) %>%
  tidyr::crossing(Element = c("Carbon", "Nitrogen")) %>%
  mutate(Element = as.character(Element))

# Optional quick QC
cat("\n--- Base FCM table preview ---\n")
print(head(fcm_base))

# ==============================================================================
# 4) READ SHEET 2 = FIXED fg/cell (Oceanic / Coastal)
# ==============================================================================
sheet2_raw <- read_excel(conv_path, sheet = 2) %>%
  rename_with(~ str_squish(.x))

nms2 <- names(sheet2_raw)

taxon_col_s2   <- detect_col(nms2, "^taxon$|^taxa$|group", "Sheet2 taxon")
element_col_s2 <- detect_col(nms2, "^element$|elements",   "Sheet2 element")
oceanic_col    <- detect_col(nms2, "oceanic",              "Sheet2 oceanic")
coastal_col    <- detect_col(nms2, "coastal",              "Sheet2 coastal")

sheet2 <- sheet2_raw %>%
  transmute(
    Taxon   = str_trim(as.character(.data[[taxon_col_s2]])),
    Element = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(.data[[element_col_s2]]))),
    Oceanic_fg_per_cell = to_fg(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[oceanic_col]]))),
    Coastal_fg_per_cell = to_fg(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[coastal_col]])))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Taxon), Taxon != "",
    !is.na(Element), Element != ""
  )

cat("\n--- Sheet2 parsed preview ---\n")
print(sheet2)

# ==============================================================================
# 5) READ SHEET 3 = ALLOMETRIC RELATIONSHIPS
# ==============================================================================
sheet3_raw <- read_excel(conv_path, sheet = 3) %>%
  rename_with(~ str_squish(.x))

nms3 <- names(sheet3_raw)

taxon_col_s3   <- detect_col(nms3, "^taxon$|^taxa$|group", "Sheet3 taxon")
element_col_s3 <- detect_col(nms3, "^element$|elements",   "Sheet3 element")
power_col      <- detect_col(nms3, "^power$|exponent",     "Sheet3 power/exponent")
coeff_col      <- detect_col(nms3, "coeff|content per cell|fg\\s*um\\-?3", "Sheet3 coefficient")

sheet3 <- sheet3_raw %>%
  transmute(
    Taxon        = str_trim(as.character(.data[[taxon_col_s3]])),
    Element      = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(.data[[element_col_s3]]))),
    coeff_fg_um3 = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[coeff_col]])),
    power        = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[power_col]]))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Taxon), Taxon != "",
    !is.na(Element), Element != ""
  )

cat("\n--- Sheet3 parsed preview ---\n")
print(sheet3)

# ==============================================================================
# 5b) FILL Bact FOR ALLOMETRIC TABLE
```
