```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=10}
# ==============================================================================
# In S3/S4 we want photo taxa from allometry, but Bact from fixed values.
# To simplify the join logic, we optionally create Bact rows inside sheet3_filled.
# We store them with power = 0, so:
#   fg_per_cell = coeff * Volume^0 = coeff
bact_source <- "Coastal"  # used only for this filled helper table

bact_fill <- sheet2 %>%
  filter(Taxon == "Bact") %>%
  transmute(
    Taxon = "Bact",
    Element,
    coeff_fg_um3 = if (bact_source == "Coastal") {
      as.numeric(Coastal_fg_per_cell)
    } else {
      as.numeric(Oceanic_fg_per_cell)
    },
    power = 0
  )

sheet3_filled <- bind_rows(sheet3, bact_fill) %>%
  distinct(Taxon, Element, .keep_all = TRUE)

cat("\n--- Sheet3_filled preview ---\n")
print(sheet3_filled)

# ==============================================================================
# 5c) KEY COVERAGE QC
# ==============================================================================
# This checks whether every Taxon × Element combination in the FCM data
# has a corresponding conversion rule in Sheet2 and Sheet3_filled.

keys_base <- fcm_base %>% distinct(Taxon, Element)
keys_s2   <- sheet2 %>% distinct(Taxon, Element)
keys_s3   <- sheet3_filled %>% distinct(Taxon, Element)

cat("\n--- Missing keys from Sheet2 (fixed) ---\n")
print(anti_join(keys_base, keys_s2, by = c("Taxon", "Element")), n = 200)

cat("\n--- Missing keys from Sheet3_filled (allometric + bact fill) ---\n")
print(anti_join(keys_base, keys_s3, by = c("Taxon", "Element")), n = 200)

# ==============================================================================
# 6) BUILD SCENARIOS
# ==============================================================================

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# S1: fixed Oceanic for all taxa
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
scenario1 <- fcm_base %>%
  left_join(
    sheet2 %>% select(Taxon, Element, fg_per_cell = Oceanic_fg_per_cell),
    by = c("Taxon", "Element")
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(fg_per_cell)) %>%
  mutate(Scenario = "S1_fixed_Oceanic") %>%
  fgcell_to_umolL()

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# S2: fixed Coastal for all taxa
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
scenario2 <- fcm_base %>%
  left_join(
    sheet2 %>% select(Taxon, Element, fg_per_cell = Coastal_fg_per_cell),
    by = c("Taxon", "Element")
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(fg_per_cell)) %>%
  mutate(Scenario = "S2_fixed_Coastal") %>%
  fgcell_to_umolL()

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# S3: allometric for photo taxa + Bact fixed Coastal
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
scenario3 <- bind_rows(
  # Photo taxa from allometry
  fcm_base %>%
    filter(Taxon %in% taxa_photo) %>%
    calc_allometric_fg_cell(sheet3_filled = sheet3_filled) %>%
    filter(is.finite(fg_per_cell)) %>%
    mutate(Scenario = "S3_allometric + Bact_Coastal") %>%
    fgcell_to_umolL(),

  # Bacteria from fixed Coastal
  fcm_base %>%
    filter(Taxon == taxa_bact) %>%
    left_join(
      sheet2 %>% select(Taxon, Element, fg_per_cell = Coastal_fg_per_cell),
      by = c("Taxon", "Element")
    ) %>%
    filter(is.finite(fg_per_cell)) %>%
    mutate(Scenario = "S3_allometric + Bact_Coastal") %>%
    fgcell_to_umolL()
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# S4: allometric for photo taxa + Bact fixed Oceanic
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
scenario4 <- bind_rows(
  # Photo taxa from allometry
  fcm_base %>%
    filter(Taxon %in% taxa_photo) %>%
    calc_allometric_fg_cell(sheet3_filled = sheet3_filled) %>%
    filter(is.finite(fg_per_cell)) %>%
    mutate(Scenario = "S4_allometric + Bact_Oceanic") %>%
    fgcell_to_umolL(),

  # Bacteria from fixed Oceanic
  fcm_base %>%
    filter(Taxon == taxa_bact) %>%
    left_join(
      sheet2 %>% select(Taxon, Element, fg_per_cell = Oceanic_fg_per_cell),
      by = c("Taxon", "Element")
    ) %>%
    filter(is.finite(fg_per_cell)) %>%
    mutate(Scenario = "S4_allometric + Bact_Oceanic") %>%
    fgcell_to_umolL()
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# S5: fixed Coastal Carbon + Redfield Nitrogen
#
# Logic:
#   1) Take Carbon from Coastal fixed conversion
#   2) Convert Carbon to µmol/L
#   3) Duplicate rows into Carbon + Nitrogen
#   4) Set Nitrogen = Carbon / 6.6 (molar Redfield C:N)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
scenario5 <- fcm_base %>%
  filter(Element == "Carbon") %>%
  left_join(
    sheet2 %>%
      filter(Element == "Carbon") %>%
      select(Taxon, Element, fg_per_cell = Coastal_fg_per_cell),
    by = c("Taxon", "Element")
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(fg_per_cell)) %>%
  mutate(Scenario = "S5_fixed_Coastal + Redfield_N") %>%
  fgcell_to_umolL() %>%
  transmute(
    across(all_of(intersect(names(.), id_cols))),
    Taxon, cells_mL, Volume_um3,
    Scenario,
    Carbon_umol_L = umol_L
  ) %>%
  tidyr::crossing(Element = c("Carbon", "Nitrogen")) %>%
  mutate(
    umol_L = ifelse(Element == "Carbon",
                    Carbon_umol_L,
                    Carbon_umol_L / redfield_CN)
  ) %>%
  select(-Carbon_umol_L)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# S6: NEuk allometric Carbon + all other taxa fixed Coastal Carbon
#     then Nitrogen is derived from Carbon using Redfield C:N = 6.6
#
# Logic:
#   - NEuk Carbon      -> allometric from Sheet3
#   - all other Carbon -> fixed Coastal from Sheet2
#   - Nitrogen for all taxa is NOT taken directly from literature factors
#     but calculated as:
#         Nitrogen_umol_L = Carbon_umol_L / 6.6
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
scenario6_carbon <- bind_rows(
  # NEuk Carbon from allometry
  fcm_base %>%
    filter(Taxon %in% taxa_s6_allometric, Element == "Carbon") %>%
    calc_allometric_fg_cell(sheet3_filled = sheet3_filled) %>%
    filter(is.finite(fg_per_cell)) %>%
    fgcell_to_umolL(),

  # All remaining taxa Carbon from fixed Coastal
  fcm_base %>%
    filter(!Taxon %in% taxa_s6_allometric, Element == "Carbon") %>%
    left_join(
      sheet2 %>%
        filter(Element == "Carbon") %>%
        select(Taxon, Element, fg_per_cell = Coastal_fg_per_cell),
      by = c("Taxon", "Element")
    ) %>%
    filter(is.finite(fg_per_cell)) %>%
    fgcell_to_umolL()
)

scenario6 <- scenario6_carbon %>%
  mutate(Scenario = "S6_NEuk_allometric_C + Redfield_N") %>%
  transmute(
    across(all_of(intersect(names(.), id_cols))),
    Taxon, cells_mL, Volume_um3,
    Scenario,
    Carbon_umol_L = umol_L
  ) %>%
  tidyr::crossing(Element = c("Carbon", "Nitrogen")) %>%
  mutate(
    umol_L = ifelse(Element == "Carbon",
                    Carbon_umol_L,
                    Carbon_umol_L / redfield_CN)
  ) %>%
  select(-Carbon_umol_L)


# ==============================================================================
# 7) COMBINE ALL SCENARIOS
# ==============================================================================
fcm_scenarios <- bind_rows(
  scenario1,
  scenario2,
  scenario3,
  scenario4,
  scenario5,
  scenario6
)

cat("\n--- Scenarios available ---\n")
print(unique(fcm_scenarios$Scenario))

# ==============================================================================
# 8) SUMMARIES
# ==============================================================================

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Summary by Scenario × Element × Taxon
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
summary_by_taxon <- fcm_scenarios %>%
  group_by(Scenario, Element, Taxon) %>%
  summarise(
    n             = sum(!is.na(umol_L)),
    mean_umol_L   = mean(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_umol_L = median(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_umol_L     = sd(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Total per sample / code / IN-EX / scenario / element
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
id_cols2 <- intersect(
  names(fcm_scenarios),
  c("Sample_ID", "Unique_code", "Start_DateTime", "InEx",
    "Project name", "Analysis_Date", "Project_type")
)

summary_total <- fcm_scenarios %>%
  group_by(across(all_of(id_cols2)), Scenario, Element) %>%
  summarise(
    Total_umol_L = sum(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n--- summary_by_taxon ---\n")
print(summary_by_taxon)

cat("\n--- summary_total ---\n")
print(summary_total)

# ==============================================================================
# 9) QC PLOTS
# ==============================================================================

# Colors for taxa
group_cols <- c(
  "Bact" = "#F4A6A6",
  "Pro"  = "#9DBCE3",
  "Syn"  = "#F6C28B",
  "Euk"  = "#A8D5BA",
  "PEuk" = "#CDEB9D",
  "NEuk" = "#7FBF9B"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot 1: mean ± CI by Taxon within each Scenario and Element
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
summary_by_taxon_plot <- fcm_scenarios %>%
  group_by(Scenario, Element, Taxon) %>%
  summarise(
    n    = sum(!is.na(umol_L)),
    mean = mean(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    conf_int = sd / sqrt(pmax(n, 1)) * qt(0.975, df = pmax(n - 1, 1))
  )

p_scenarios_taxa <- ggplot(summary_by_taxon_plot,
                           aes(x = Taxon, y = mean, fill = Taxon)) +
  geom_col(color = "black", linewidth = 0.3)+
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = mean - conf_int, ymax = mean + conf_int),
    width = 0.25
  ) +
  facet_grid(Element ~ Scenario, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    y = expression(paste("Content (", mu, "mol L"^{-1}, ")")),
    x = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

print(p_scenarios_taxa)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot 2: total mean ± CI by Scenario and Element
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
total_plot <- fcm_scenarios %>%
  group_by(Scenario, Element) %>%
  summarise(
    n    = sum(!is.na(umol_L)),
    mean = mean(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    conf_int = sd / sqrt(pmax(n, 1)) * qt(0.975, df = pmax(n - 1, 1))
  )

p_total <- ggplot(total_plot, aes(x = Scenario, y = mean)) +
  geom_col(fill = "grey40") +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = mean - conf_int, ymax = mean + conf_int),
    width = 0.25
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~Element, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    y = expression(paste("Total content (", mu, "mol L"^{-1}, ")")),
    x = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )

print(p_total)

# ==============================================================================
# 10) OPTIONAL QC FOR S6
# ==============================================================================
cat("\n--- S6 row counts by Taxon and Element ---\n")
print(
  scenario6 %>%
    count(Taxon, Element, Scenario)
)

# ==============================================================================
# 11) SAVE FIGURES
# ==============================================================================
out_dir <- normalizePath(out_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(
  filename = "FCM_Content_by_Taxon_Scenario_Element_QC.png",
  plot     = p_scenarios_taxa,
  path     = out_dir,
  width    = 35,
  height   = 20,
  units    = "cm",
  dpi      = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = "FCM_Total_Content_by_Scenario_QC.png",
  plot     = p_total,
  path     = out_dir,
  width    = 20,
  height   = 14,
  units    = "cm",
  dpi      = 300
)

################################################################################
# END OF CHUNK
################################################################################


```
### QC — Verify C:N ratios from conversion constants (Sheet2+Sheet3) and after multiplication
```{r, fig.width=7, fig.height=5}
################################################################################
# QC — C:N ratios from (A) your EXISTING fcm_scenarios table
# + overlay the ORIGINAL ratios from the conversion constants (Sheet2+Sheet3)
#
# GOAL
#   1) Use your already-built fcm_scenarios (NO re-calculation of umol_L)
#   2) Compute per-row C:N ratio = (umol_C / umol_N) for each Taxon × Sample (Unique_code/InEx…)
#   3) Visualize:
#        (A) Boxplot of C:N by Taxon (colored) + overlay the constant ratio(s)
#        (B) Scatter: C:N vs Volume_um3 (log-x) to show why some taxa vary
#   4) Explain WHY ratios change:
#        CN ∝ V^(power_C - power_N)  (allometry) -> varies if powers differ
#
# INPUTS (must exist):
#   - fcm_scenarios : long table with Taxon, Element (Carbon/Nitrogen), umol_L, Scenario, IDs
#   - sheet2, sheet3 : conversion factor tables already read/cleaned in your pipeline
#       sheet2 has: Taxon, Element, Oceanic_fg_per_cell, Coastal_fg_per_cell
#       sheet3 has: Taxon, Element, coeff_fg_um3, power
#   - fcm_all_size : to get Volume_um3 per Unique_code/InEx/Taxon (for the scatter)
#
# OUTPUT OBJECTS:
#   - cn_by_row : per-row C:N from fcm_scenarios
#   - cn_constants : original constant ratios from sheet2+sheet3
#   - cn_reference_lines : per-taxon reference ratio used in scenario_keep
#   - p_cn_box, p_cn_vs_vol
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# ==============================================================================
# 0) SETTINGS
# ==============================================================================
scenario_keep <- "S6_NEuk_allometric_C + Redfield_N"  # ✅ must match unique(fcm_scenarios$Scenario)

# colors (your palette)
group_cols <- c(
  "Bact" = "#F4A6A6",
  "Pro"  = "#9DBCE3",
  "Syn"  = "#F6C28B",
  "Euk"  = "#A8D5BA",
  "PEuk" = "#CDEB9D",
  "NEuk" = "#7FBF9B"
)

# In S3: Bact uses Sheet2 Coastal; photo taxa use Sheet3 allometry
bact_env_for_S3 <- "Coastal"   # S3 definition

# ==============================================================================
# 1) SAFETY CHECKS
# ==============================================================================
stopifnot(exists("fcm_scenarios"))
stopifnot(exists("sheet2"))
stopifnot(exists("sheet3"))
stopifnot(exists("fcm_all_size"))

# Make sure scenario exists
if (!(scenario_keep %in% unique(fcm_scenarios$Scenario))) {
  stop("scenario_keep not found in fcm_scenarios$Scenario: ", scenario_keep,
       "\nAvailable: ", paste(unique(fcm_scenarios$Scenario), collapse = " | "))
}

# ==============================================================================
# 2) BUILD "CN PER ROW" USING YOUR EXISTING fcm_scenarios
#    (no re-calculation; just pivot Carbon/Nitrogen and divide)
# ==============================================================================

# Which ID columns exist? (we keep whatever is available)
id_cols <- intersect(
  names(fcm_scenarios),
  c("Project_type","Project name","Analysis_Date","Start_DateTime","End_DateTime",
    "Sample_ID","Unique_code","InEx")
)

cn_by_row <- fcm_scenarios %>%
  filter(Scenario == scenario_keep) %>%
  mutate(
    Taxon   = str_trim(as.character(Taxon)),
    Element = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(Element)))
  ) %>%
  filter(Element %in% c("Carbon","Nitrogen")) %>%
  select(all_of(id_cols), Scenario, Taxon, Element, umol_L) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Element, values_from = umol_L) %>%
  mutate(
    CN_ratio = Carbon / Nitrogen
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(CN_ratio), CN_ratio > 0)  # CN must be positive to interpret

# Quick QC
cn_by_row %>%
  count(Taxon, name = "n_rows") %>%
  arrange(desc(n_rows)) %>%
  print(n = 50)

# ==============================================================================
# 3) ORIGINAL CN RATIOS FROM THE CONSTANT TABLES (Sheet2 + Sheet3)
#    These are NOT the results; these are the theoretical ratios implied by constants.
#
# Sheet2 fixed:
#   CN_ratio_Coastal = Coastal_C / Coastal_N
#   CN_ratio_Oceanic = Oceanic_C / Oceanic_N
#
# Sheet3 allometric:
#   CN_constant_ratio = coeff_C / coeff_N
#   CN_power_diff     = power_C - power_N
#   -> CN varies with volume if CN_power_diff != 0
# ==============================================================================

# ---- Sheet3 (allometric) ----
cn_sheet3 <- sheet3 %>%
  mutate(
    Taxon   = str_trim(as.character(Taxon)),
    Element = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(Element)))
  ) %>%
  filter(Element %in% c("Carbon","Nitrogen")) %>%
  select(Taxon, Element, coeff_fg_um3, power) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Element, values_from = c(coeff_fg_um3, power)) %>%
  mutate(
    CN_constant_ratio = coeff_fg_um3_Carbon / coeff_fg_um3_Nitrogen,
    CN_power_diff     = power_Carbon - power_Nitrogen
  ) %>%
  select(Taxon, CN_constant_ratio, CN_power_diff)

# ---- Sheet2 (fixed) ----
cn_sheet2 <- sheet2 %>%
  mutate(
    Taxon   = str_trim(as.character(Taxon)),
    Element = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(Element)))
  ) %>%
  filter(Element %in% c("Carbon","Nitrogen")) %>%
  select(Taxon, Element, Oceanic_fg_per_cell, Coastal_fg_per_cell) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Element, values_from = c(Oceanic_fg_per_cell, Coastal_fg_per_cell)) %>%
  mutate(
    CN_ratio_Coastal = Coastal_fg_per_cell_Carbon / Coastal_fg_per_cell_Nitrogen,
    CN_ratio_Oceanic = Oceanic_fg_per_cell_Carbon / Oceanic_fg_per_cell_Nitrogen
  ) %>%
  select(Taxon, CN_ratio_Coastal, CN_ratio_Oceanic)

cn_constants <- full_join(cn_sheet3, cn_sheet2, by = "Taxon")

print(cn_constants, n = 50)

# ==============================================================================
# 4) PICK THE REFERENCE RATIO USED IN *this* scenario_keep  [UPDATED for S5]
# ==============================================================================

redfield_CN <- 6.6

cn_reference_lines <- cn_constants %>%
  mutate(
    CN_reference = case_when(
      # ---- S5: Forced Redfield for ALL taxa ----
      scenario_keep == "S5_fixed_Coastal + Redfield_N" ~ redfield_CN,

      # ---- fixed scenarios from Sheet2 ----
      scenario_keep == "S1_fixed_Oceanic" ~ CN_ratio_Oceanic,
      scenario_keep == "S2_fixed_Coastal" ~ CN_ratio_Coastal,

      # ---- mixed allometric + fixed Bact ----
      scenario_keep == "S3_allometric + Bact_Coastal" & Taxon == "Bact" ~ CN_ratio_Coastal,
      scenario_keep == "S4_allometric + Bact_Oceanic" & Taxon == "Bact" ~ CN_ratio_Oceanic,

      # In S3/S4 for non-Bact taxa -> Sheet3 baseline coeff ratio
      scenario_keep %in% c("S3_allometric + Bact_Coastal", "S4_allometric + Bact_Oceanic") ~ CN_constant_ratio,

      # fallback
      TRUE ~ CN_constant_ratio
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(CN_reference)) %>%
  select(Taxon, CN_reference) %>%
  distinct()

print(cn_reference_lines, n = 50)

# ==============================================================================
# 5) FIGURE A — BOX PLOT of CN ratio by Taxon + overlay reference CN
# ==============================================================================

# Ensure consistent order (optional)
taxon_order <- intersect(names(group_cols), unique(cn_by_row$Taxon))
cn_by_row <- cn_by_row %>%
  mutate(Taxon = factor(Taxon, levels = taxon_order))

cn_reference_lines <- cn_reference_lines %>%
  mutate(Taxon = factor(Taxon, levels = taxon_order))

# subtitle that matches scenario
subtitle_txt <- dplyr::case_when(
  scenario_keep == "S5_fixed_Coastal + Redfield_N" ~ "Black diamonds = forced Redfield reference (C:N = 6.6)",
  scenario_keep %in% c("S1_fixed_Oceanic","S2_fixed_Coastal") ~ "Black diamonds = ratios implied by Sheet2 fixed constants",
  scenario_keep %in% c("S3_allometric + Bact_Coastal","S4_allometric + Bact_Oceanic") ~ "Black diamonds = Sheet3 coeff ratio (photo taxa) + Sheet2 ratio for Bact",
  TRUE ~ "Black diamonds = reference ratios"
)

p_cn_box <- ggplot(cn_by_row, aes(x = Taxon, y = CN_ratio, fill = Taxon)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.25, linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.20, size = 1, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_point(
    data = cn_reference_lines,
    aes(x = Taxon, y = CN_reference),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    shape = 18, size = 3, color = "black"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = paste0("C:N ratio by taxon — ", scenario_keep),
    subtitle = subtitle_txt,
    x = "FCM Group (Taxon)",
    y = expression("C:N ratio ("*mu*"mol C / "*mu*"mol N)")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p_cn_box

################################################################################
# 6) FIGURE B — CN ratio vs Volume_um3 (FIXED: ensure Taxon exists)
################################################################################

# Prepare volume table (same IDs + Taxon + Volume)
vol_tbl <- fcm_all_size %>%
  transmute(
    Taxon_vol  = str_trim(as.character(Group)),
    Volume_um3 = as.numeric(Volume_um3),
    across(all_of(id_cols), ~ .)
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(Volume_um3), Volume_um3 > 0)

# Join volumes onto CN table
cn_with_vol <- cn_by_row %>%
  mutate(Taxon_chr = as.character(Taxon)) %>%
  left_join(
    vol_tbl %>% mutate(Taxon_chr = as.character(Taxon_vol)),
    by = c(setNames(id_cols, id_cols), "Taxon_chr" = "Taxon_chr")
  )

# If join weak -> fallback join by Unique_code + InEx + Taxon
if (sum(is.na(cn_with_vol$Volume_um3)) > 0.5 * nrow(cn_with_vol)) {
  message("Volume join via full IDs was weak -> using fallback join by Unique_code + InEx + Taxon")

  cn_with_vol <- cn_by_row %>%
    mutate(Taxon_chr = as.character(Taxon)) %>%
    left_join(
      vol_tbl %>%
        select(any_of(c("Unique_code","InEx")), Taxon_vol, Volume_um3) %>%
        mutate(Taxon_chr = as.character(Taxon_vol)),
      by = c("Unique_code","InEx","Taxon_chr")
    )
}

# Build a FINAL Taxon column explicitly (so ggplot always finds it)
cn_with_vol <- cn_with_vol %>%
  mutate(
    Taxon = dplyr::coalesce(as.character(Taxon), Taxon_chr)
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(Volume_um3), is.finite(CN_ratio), !is.na(Taxon), Taxon != "")

# Plot
p_cn_vs_vol <- ggplot(cn_with_vol, aes(x = Volume_um3, y = CN_ratio, color = Taxon)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.35, size = 1.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_cols, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_x_log10(labels = label_number()) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 6.6, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "C:N ratio vs estimated cell volume",
    subtitle = "If power_C != power_N (Sheet3), C:N changes with Volume. Dashed line = Redfield (6.6)",
    x = expression("Estimated volume ("*mu*"m^3, log scale)"),
    y = expression("C:N ratio ( "*mu*"mol C / "*mu*"mol N )"),
    color = "Taxon"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

p_cn_vs_vol
# ==============================================================================
# 7) OPTIONAL: PRINT A SMALL SUMMARY TABLE (median CN + IQR + reference)
# ==============================================================================

cn_summary <- cn_by_row %>%
  group_by(Taxon) %>%
  summarise(
    n = sum(is.finite(CN_ratio)),
    CN_median = median(CN_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    CN_Q1     = quantile(CN_ratio, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    CN_Q3     = quantile(CN_ratio, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(cn_reference_lines %>% mutate(Taxon = as.character(Taxon)), by = c("Taxon" = "Taxon")) %>%
  arrange(Taxon)

print(cn_summary, n = 50)

################################################################################
# INTERPRETATION (important to remember)
#
# - For taxa where Sheet3 has power_C == power_N (e.g., Pro/Syn in your table),
#   CN should be ~constant across sizes (you'll see a "flat band").
#
# - For taxa where power_C != power_N (e.g., PEuk/NEuk: 0.863 vs 1),
#   CN scales with volume:
#       CN ∝ V^(power_C - power_N)
#   so CN will systematically change with Volume_um3 — exactly what your scatter shows.
#
# - The black diamonds in the boxplot are "baseline" ratios from constants:
#   * Sheet2: Coastal/Oceanic fg-per-cell ratios
#   * Sheet3: coeff_C/coeff_N (baseline at V^0), but real CN changes with V if powers differ.
################################################################################

```


## DATABASE
### Sponge_DataBase (Google Sheet) → Clean tibble + short names + FIX unit tokens
```{r}
################################################################################
# Sponge_DataBase (Google Sheet) → Clean tibble + short names + FIX unit tokens
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Core idea:
#   - Keep names() short/ASCII
#   - Preserve units from the original header in a dictionary
#   - Use that dictionary to fix wrong unit tokens in column names
################################################################################

library(googlesheets4)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(tibble)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Google Sheet URL
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
gs_url <- "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1vFp0rbGr9K5niG-0GAgNEMunppRf5tMnMHtRvBUFpis/edit?gid=979247839#gid=979247839"


# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Read header rows (3 rows) and build RAW + machine-friendly names
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
hdr <- read_sheet(
  ss        = gs_url,
  sheet     = "Sponge_DataBase",
  range     = "A1:ZZ3",
  col_names = FALSE,
  col_types = "c"
)

fill_right <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  for (i in seq_along(x)) {
    if (i > 1 && (is.na(x[i]) || x[i] == "")) x[i] <- x[i - 1]
  }
  x
}

h1 <- fill_right(hdr[1, ])
h2 <- fill_right(hdr[2, ])
h3 <- as.character(hdr[3, ])

raw_names <- str_squish(paste(h1, h2, h3, sep = " | "))
empty_field <- is.na(h3) | h3 == ""
raw_names[empty_field] <- str_squish(paste(h1[empty_field], h2[empty_field], sep = " | "))

clean_name <- function(x) {
  x <- tolower(x)
  x <- gsub("\\|", "_", x)
  x <- gsub("[^a-z0-9_]+", "_", x)
  x <- gsub("_+", "_", x)
  x <- gsub("^_|_$", "", x)
  x
}

machine_names <- make.unique(clean_name(raw_names), sep = "__")

name_dict <- tibble(
  col_machine = machine_names,
  col_raw     = raw_names
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Read data (A4:CI) as character and apply machine names
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
dat <- read_sheet(
  ss        = gs_url,
  sheet     = "Sponge_DataBase",
  range     = "A4:CI",
  col_names = FALSE,
  col_types = "c"
)

names(dat) <- machine_names[seq_len(ncol(dat))]
sponge_db2 <- dat

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Clean values + type_convert
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
sponge_db2_clean <- sponge_db2 %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ na_if(.x, ""))) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ na_if(.x, "N/A"))) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ na_if(.x, "#N/A"))) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ str_replace_all(.x, ",", "."))) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ str_squish(.x))) %>%
  type_convert(na = c("", "N/A", "#N/A", "NA"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Drop all-NA columns + keep dictionary aligned
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
sponge_db2_clean2 <- sponge_db2_clean %>%
  select(where(~ sum(!is.na(.x)) > 0))

name_dict2 <- name_dict %>%
  filter(col_machine %in% names(sponge_db2_clean2))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Convert "numbers stored as text" → numeric only when safe
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
never_convert <- c(
  "metadata_na_unique_code",
  "metadata_na_ind_code",
  "metadata_na_sampling_date",
  "loggers_data_oxygen_loggers_start_date_time",
  "loggers_data_oxygen_loggers_end_date_time",
  "loggers_data_ph_loggers_start_date_time",
  "loggers_data_ph_loggers_end_date_time",
  "loggers_data_oxygen_loggers_time_sampled_hr_mm"
)

is_numlike <- function(x) {
  x <- str_trim(x)
  str_detect(x, regex("^[-+]?\\d*\\.?\\d+(e[-+]?\\d+)?$", ignore_case = TRUE))
}

to_numeric_if_mostly_numlike <- function(v, min_prop = 0.8) {
  if (!is.character(v)) return(v)
  v_trim <- str_trim(v)
  ok <- !is.na(v_trim) & v_trim != ""
  if (!any(ok)) return(v)

  prop <- mean(is_numlike(v_trim[ok]))
  if (prop >= min_prop) parse_number(v_trim) else v
}

sponge_db2_typed <- sponge_db2_clean2 %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character) & !any_of(never_convert), to_numeric_if_mostly_numlike))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) Shorten names (drop prefixes) + standardize logger prefixes
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
prefixes_to_drop <- c(
  "^metadata_na_",
  "^size_estimation_na_",
  "^loggers_data_",
  "^fcm_",
  "^nutrients_"
)

clean_short_names <- function(nms) {
  out <- nms
  for (p in prefixes_to_drop) out <- str_replace(out, p, "")
  out <- str_replace(out, "^oxygen_loggers_", "oxy_")
  out <- str_replace(out, "^ph_loggers_",     "ph_")
  out <- str_replace_all(out, "__+", "_")
  make.unique(out, sep = "_")
}

sponge_db2_short <- sponge_db2_typed %>%
  rename_with(clean_short_names) %>%
  rename(ofr_l_hr_1 = any_of("dfs_ex_ofr_l_hr_1"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7) Build final dictionary: short ↔ raw header + extracted unit
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
dict_machine_to_short <- tibble(
  col_machine = names(sponge_db2_typed),
  col_short   = names(sponge_db2_short)
)

name_dict_short <- dict_machine_to_short %>%
  left_join(name_dict2, by = "col_machine") %>%
  mutate(
    col_raw = if_else(is.na(col_raw), "", col_raw),

    # 1) units in (...) at end
    unit_paren = str_match(col_raw, "\\(([^()]*)\\)\\s*$")[,2],

    # 2) units in [...] anywhere (e.g. Syn. [# µl⁻¹])
    unit_brack = str_match(col_raw, "\\[([^\\]]+)\\]")[,2],

    # 3) fallback: if nothing found, keep ""
    unit = coalesce(unit_paren, unit_brack, "")
  ) %>%
  transmute(col_short, col_machine, col_raw, unit)


# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 8) Apply unit-based fixes SAFELY + QC (RUN ONCE)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

library(stringr)
library(tibble)

fix_unit_tokens <- function(col_short, unit_text) {
  col_short <- as.character(col_short)
  unit_text <- tolower(as.character(unit_text))

  if (is.na(unit_text) || unit_text == "") return(col_short)

  # אם היחידה מציינת umol/L ובשם יש mol_l -> מתקנים ל umol_l
  if (str_detect(unit_text, "µmol|umol") && str_detect(col_short, "mol_l")) {
    col_short <- str_replace_all(col_short, "mol_l", "umol_l")
  }

  col_short
}

before_names <- names(sponge_db2_short)

fixed_names <- mapply(
  fix_unit_tokens,
  col_short = before_names,
  unit_text = name_dict_short$unit[match(before_names, name_dict_short$col_short)],
  USE.NAMES = FALSE
)

# אם משום מה יצא NA בשם החדש -> תשאירי את הישן
fixed_names[is.na(fixed_names) | fixed_names == ""] <- before_names[is.na(fixed_names) | fixed_names == ""]

# בדיקת כפילויות לפני החלה
if (anyDuplicated(fixed_names)) {
  dup <- fixed_names[duplicated(fixed_names)]
  stop("Unit-fix would create duplicate column names: ", paste(unique(dup), collapse = ", "))
}

# החלה
names(sponge_db2_short) <- fixed_names

# QC: מה השתנה?
changed <- tibble(
  before = before_names,
  after  = fixed_names,
  unit   = name_dict_short$unit[match(before_names, name_dict_short$col_short)]
) %>% dplyr::filter(before != after)

print(changed, n = 200)
print(grep("mol_l", names(sponge_db2_short), value = TRUE))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9) OPTIONAL QC FILTER — drop whole Unique_code rows (sponges didn't work)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Expectation:
#   - drop_whole_uc exists (tibble/data.frame with column Unique_code)
#   - sponge_db2_short has column unique_code (lowercase, after renaming)

if (exists("drop_whole_uc")) {

  # --- Safety checks
  stopifnot("Unique_code" %in% names(drop_whole_uc))
  stopifnot("unique_code" %in% names(sponge_db2_short))

  # --- Build clean drop list
  drop_list <- drop_whole_uc %>%
    transmute(unique_code = str_trim(as.character(Unique_code))) %>%
    filter(!is.na(unique_code), unique_code != "") %>%
    distinct()

  # --- QC before
  n_before <- nrow(sponge_db2_short)
  n_match  <- sum(str_trim(as.character(sponge_db2_short$unique_code)) %in% drop_list$unique_code)

  message("Dropping whole Unique_code rows (n unique codes) = ", nrow(drop_list))
  message("Rows matched for removal = ", n_match, " out of ", n_before)

  # --- Filter out
  sponge_db2_short <- sponge_db2_short %>%
    mutate(unique_code = str_trim(as.character(unique_code))) %>%
    anti_join(drop_list, by = "unique_code")

  # --- QC after
  message("Rows before: ", n_before, " | after: ", nrow(sponge_db2_short))

} else {
  message("drop_whole_uc not found -> skipping whole-Unique_code removal step.")
}

```

### Update DIC columns BEFORE summary tables
```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=10}
################################################################################
# Update DIC columns BEFORE summary tables
# - Reads cleaned DIC values from CSV
# - Joins by unique_code
# - Overwrites DIC columns only where new values exist
#
# INPUT OBJECT (must exist from previous chunk):
#   sponge_db2_short   (the cleaned GoogleSheet sponge database)
#
# OUTPUT OBJECT:
#   sponge_db2_with_cn (starts as sponge_db2_short, but with DIC columns updated)
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# ==============================================================================
# 0) Safety checks
# ==============================================================================
stopifnot(exists("sponge_db2_short"))
stopifnot("unique_code" %in% names(sponge_db2_short))

# ==============================================================================
# 1) Start from sponge_db2_short (create/refresh sponge_db2_with_cn here)
# ==============================================================================
sponge_db2_with_cn <- sponge_db2_short %>%
  mutate(unique_code = as.character(str_trim(unique_code)))

# ==============================================================================
# 2) Load cleaned DIC table (CSV)
# ==============================================================================
dic_clean_raw <- read_csv(
  "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/Energy_budget/Summary_tables/Statistic Table Between species all data.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

# ==============================================================================
# 3) Build mapping: unique_code + new DIC columns
#    (collapse duplicates per unique_code defensively)
# ==============================================================================
dic_clean <- dic_clean_raw %>%
  transmute(
    unique_code = as.character(str_trim(Unique_Code)),

    # DIC "direct chemistry"
    dic_ex_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`DIC Conc. In (µmol/L)`)),
    dic_ex_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`DIC Conc.Ex (µmol/L)`)),

    # DIC from pH+TA (pH-system)
    ph_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new     = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`DICfromPHandTA Amb`)),
    ph_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new     = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`DICfromPHandTA Ex`))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(unique_code), unique_code != "") %>%
  group_by(unique_code) %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      ends_with("_new"),
      ~ if (all(is.na(.x))) NA_real_ else mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ==============================================================================
# 4) Optional backup (only if columns exist)
# ==============================================================================
backup_if_exists <- function(df, old_nm, new_nm) {
  if (old_nm %in% names(df)) df[[new_nm]] <- df[[old_nm]]
  df
}

sponge_db2_with_cn <- sponge_db2_with_cn %>%
  { backup_if_exists(., "ph_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l",     "ph_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_old") } %>%
  { backup_if_exists(., "ph_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l",     "ph_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_old") } %>%
  { backup_if_exists(., "dic_ex_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l", "dic_ex_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_old") } %>%
  { backup_if_exists(., "dic_ex_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l", "dic_ex_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_old") }

# ==============================================================================
# 5) Join + overwrite ONLY where new values exist
# ==============================================================================
n_before <- nrow(sponge_db2_with_cn)

sponge_db2_with_cn <- sponge_db2_with_cn %>%
  left_join(dic_clean, by = "unique_code") %>%
  mutate(
    ph_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l     = if ("ph_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l" %in% names(.))
      coalesce(ph_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new, ph_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l) else ph_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new,

    ph_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l     = if ("ph_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l" %in% names(.))
      coalesce(ph_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new, ph_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l) else ph_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new,

    dic_ex_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l = if ("dic_ex_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l" %in% names(.))
      coalesce(dic_ex_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new, dic_ex_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l) else dic_ex_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new,

    dic_ex_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l = if ("dic_ex_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l" %in% names(.))
      coalesce(dic_ex_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new, dic_ex_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l) else dic_ex_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new
  ) %>%
  select(-ends_with("_new"))

# Safety check: ensure no row multiplication
stopifnot(nrow(sponge_db2_with_cn) == n_before)

# ==============================================================================
# 6) Coverage report (how many unique_code had new values)
# ==============================================================================
cat("Unique_code with NEW pH DIC IN:     ", sum(!is.na(dic_clean$ph_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new)), "\n")
cat("Unique_code with NEW pH DIC EX:     ", sum(!is.na(dic_clean$ph_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new)), "\n")
cat("Unique_code with NEW dic_ex DIC IN: ", sum(!is.na(dic_clean$dic_ex_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new)), "\n")
cat("Unique_code with NEW dic_ex DIC EX: ", sum(!is.na(dic_clean$dic_ex_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l_new)), "\n")

################################################################################
# End chunk: sponge_db2_with_cn is now ready for the summary-table chunk
################################################################################

```

### Species × Parameter tables (Chemistry + FCM S6 taxon) — RAW + SUMMARY
```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=10}

################################################################################
# Species × Parameter tables (Chemistry + FCM S3 taxon) — RAW + SUMMARY
################################################################################
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)

stopifnot(exists("sponge_db2_with_cn"), exists("fcm_scenarios"))


# Make sure scenario_keep exists exactly
scenario_keep <- "S6_NEuk_allometric_C + Redfield_N"
#scenario_keep <- "S3_allometric + Bact_Coastal"
stopifnot(scenario_keep %in% unique(fcm_scenarios$Scenario))
# ---- SETTINGS ----

digits_conc <- 2
digits_Q    <- 2
digits_flux <- 2

out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/Energy_budget/Summary_tables"
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# ---- HELPERS ----
mean_ci95 <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  n <- length(x)
  if (n == 0) return(tibble(mean = NA_real_, ci95 = NA_real_, n = 0L))
  if (n == 1) return(tibble(mean = x[1], ci95 = NA_real_, n = 1L))
  m  <- mean(x)
  se <- sd(x) / sqrt(n)
  ci <- qt(0.975, df = n - 1) * se
  tibble(mean = m, ci95 = ci, n = as.integer(n))
}
get_col <- function(df, nm) if (nm %in% names(df)) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df[[nm]])) else rep(NA_real_, nrow(df))

# ==============================================================================
# 0) BASE: unique_code × species × Q
# ==============================================================================
base_tbl <- sponge_db2_with_cn %>%
  transmute(
    unique_code = as.character(unique_code),
    species     = as.character(species),
    Q_L_hr      = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(ofr_l_hr_1))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(species), !is.na(unique_code))

# ==============================================================================
# 1) CHEMISTRY -> long (IN/EX/Delta)
# ==============================================================================
chem_direct <- sponge_db2_with_cn %>%
  transmute(
    unique_code = as.character(unique_code),
    species     = as.character(species),

    O2_IN  = get_col(., "oxy_in_mean_oxygen_conc_umol_l"),
    O2_EX  = get_col(., "oxy_ex_mean_oxygen_conc_umol_l"),

    DIC_pH_IN = get_col(., "ph_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l"),
    DIC_pH_EX = get_col(., "ph_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l"),

    DIC_dicex_IN = get_col(., "dic_ex_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l"),
    DIC_dicex_EX = get_col(., "dic_ex_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l"),

    NOx_IN = coalesce(get_col(., "ex_in_nox_conc_umol_l"), get_col(., "ex_in_nox_conc_mol_l")),
    NOx_EX = coalesce(get_col(., "ex_ex_nox_conc_umol_l"), get_col(., "ex_ex_nox_conc_mol_l")),

    NH4_IN = coalesce(get_col(., "ex_in_nh_conc_umol_l"),  get_col(., "ex_in_nh_conc_mol_l")),
    NH4_EX = coalesce(get_col(., "ex_ex_nh_conc_umol_l"),  get_col(., "ex_ex_nh_conc_mol_l")),

    PO4_IN = coalesce(get_col(., "ex_in_po_conc_umol_l"),  get_col(., "ex_in_po_conc_mol_l")),
    PO4_EX = coalesce(get_col(., "ex_ex_po_conc_umol_l"),  get_col(., "ex_ex_po_conc_mol_l")),

    DOC_IN = get_col(., "dom_ex_in_doc_conc_umol_l"),
    DOC_EX = get_col(., "dom_ex_ex_doc_conc_umol_l"),

    TN_IN  = get_col(., "dom_ex_in_tn_conc_umol_l"),
    TN_EX  = get_col(., "dom_ex_ex_tn_conc_umol_l")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(species), !is.na(unique_code)) %>%
  mutate(
    DIN_IN = ifelse(is.na(NOx_IN) & is.na(NH4_IN), NA_real_, coalesce(NOx_IN, 0) + coalesce(NH4_IN, 0)),
    DIN_EX = ifelse(is.na(NOx_EX) & is.na(NH4_EX), NA_real_, coalesce(NOx_EX, 0) + coalesce(NH4_EX, 0)),
    DON_IN = ifelse(!is.na(TN_IN) & !is.na(DIN_IN), TN_IN - DIN_IN, NA_real_),
    DON_EX = ifelse(!is.na(TN_EX) & !is.na(DIN_EX), TN_EX - DIN_EX, NA_real_)
  )

chem_long <- chem_direct %>%
  select(unique_code, species,
         O2_IN, O2_EX,
         DIC_pH_IN, DIC_pH_EX,
         DIC_dicex_IN, DIC_dicex_EX,
         NOx_IN, NOx_EX,
         NH4_IN, NH4_EX,
         DIN_IN, DIN_EX,
         PO4_IN, PO4_EX,
         DOC_IN, DOC_EX,
         TN_IN, TN_EX,
         DON_IN, DON_EX) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(unique_code, species), names_to = "name", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    side = ifelse(str_detect(name, "_IN$"), "IN", "EX"),
    parameter = str_remove(name, "_(IN|EX)$")
  ) %>%
  select(unique_code, species, parameter, side, value) %>%
  group_by(unique_code, species, parameter, side) %>%
  summarise(value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = side, values_from = value) %>%
  mutate(Delta = IN - EX)

# ==============================================================================
# 2) FCM TAXON C/N -> long (IN/EX/Delta) for S3 only  [FIXED]
# ==============================================================================
species_map <- sponge_db2_with_cn %>%
  transmute(
    unique_code = as.character(str_trim(unique_code)),
    species     = as.character(species)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(unique_code), unique_code != "", !is.na(species)) %>%
  distinct(unique_code, species) %>%
  # אם משום מה יש כמה species לאותו unique_code (לא אמור לקרות) – בוחרים את הראשון
  group_by(unique_code) %>%
  summarise(species = dplyr::first(species), .groups = "drop")

fcm_long <- fcm_scenarios %>%
  filter(Scenario == scenario_keep) %>%
  transmute(
    unique_code = as.character(str_trim(Unique_code)),
    InEx        = str_to_upper(str_trim(as.character(InEx))),
    Taxon       = as.character(Taxon),
    Element     = as.character(Element),
    value       = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(umol_L))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    InEx %in% c("IN", "EX"),
    Element %in% c("Carbon", "Nitrogen"),
    !is.na(unique_code), unique_code != ""
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    parameter = paste0(Taxon, "_", ifelse(Element == "Carbon", "C", "N"))
  ) %>%
  left_join(species_map, by = "unique_code") %>%
  filter(!is.na(species)) %>%
  # ✅ קריטי: לסכם כפילויות לפני pivot
  group_by(unique_code, species, parameter, InEx) %>%
  summarise(value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = InEx,
    values_from = value,
    values_fill = NA_real_,
    values_fn   = mean   # extra safety (shouldn't be needed after summarise)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    IN = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(IN)),
    EX = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(EX)),
    Delta = IN - EX
  )

# ==============================================================================
# 3) ONE unified RAW table + Flux
# ==============================================================================
raw_individual_tbl_num <- bind_rows(
  chem_long %>% select(unique_code, species, parameter, IN, EX, Delta),
  fcm_long  %>% select(unique_code, species, parameter, IN, EX, Delta)
) %>%
  left_join(base_tbl, by = c("unique_code", "species")) %>%
  mutate(Flux_umol_hr = Q_L_hr * Delta) %>%
  filter(!(is.na(IN) & is.na(EX) & is.na(Delta))) %>%
  arrange(species, parameter, unique_code)

raw_individual_tbl <- raw_individual_tbl_num %>%
  mutate(
    IN           = round(IN, digits_conc),
    EX           = round(EX, digits_conc),
    Delta        = round(Delta, digits_conc),
    Q_L_hr       = round(Q_L_hr, digits_Q),
    Flux_umol_hr = round(Flux_umol_hr, digits_flux)
  )

# ==============================================================================
# 4) SUMMARY  (עם N, %I/%E, paired test + *)
# ==============================================================================
summary_species_tbl <- raw_individual_tbl_num %>%
  mutate(pair_ok = is.finite(IN) & is.finite(EX),
         delta_ok = is.finite(Delta)) %>%
  group_by(species, parameter) %>%
  summarise(
    # --- means + CI for concentrations/fluxes (as before)
    IN_mean    = mean_ci95(IN)$mean,          IN_ci95    = mean_ci95(IN)$ci95,
    EX_mean    = mean_ci95(EX)$mean,          EX_ci95    = mean_ci95(EX)$ci95,
    Delta_mean = mean_ci95(Delta)$mean,       Delta_ci95 = mean_ci95(Delta)$ci95,
    Q_mean     = mean_ci95(Q_L_hr)$mean,      Q_ci95     = mean_ci95(Q_L_hr)$ci95,
    Flux_mean  = mean_ci95(Flux_umol_hr)$mean,Flux_ci95  = mean_ci95(Flux_umol_hr)$ci95,

    # --- N exactly like the paper: number of paired In-Ex
    N = sum(pair_ok),

    # --- %I / %E based on paired deltas
    pct_I = ifelse(N == 0, NA_real_, 100 * sum(pair_ok & Delta > 0, na.rm = TRUE) / N),
    pct_E = ifelse(N == 0, NA_real_, 100 * sum(pair_ok & Delta < 0, na.rm = TRUE) / N),

    # --- paired test (t-test if deltas ~ normal, else Wilcoxon)
    p_value = {
      d <- Delta[pair_ok]
      d <- d[is.finite(d)]
      if (length(d) < 2 || length(unique(d)) < 2) {
        NA_real_
      } else {
        # Shapiro only when n>=3 (otherwise meaningless)
        sh_p <- if (length(d) >= 3) {
          tryCatch(shapiro.test(d)$p.value, error = function(e) NA_real_)
        } else {
          NA_real_
        }

        if (!is.na(sh_p) && sh_p > 0.05) {
          tryCatch(t.test(IN[pair_ok], EX[pair_ok], paired = TRUE)$p.value,
                   error = function(e) NA_real_)
        } else {
          tryCatch(wilcox.test(IN[pair_ok], EX[pair_ok], paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE)$p.value,
                   error = function(e) NA_real_)
        }
      }
    },

    test_used = {
      d <- Delta[pair_ok]
      d <- d[is.finite(d)]
      if (length(d) < 2 || length(unique(d)) < 2) {
        NA_character_
      } else {
        sh_p <- if (length(d) >= 3) {
          tryCatch(shapiro.test(d)$p.value, error = function(e) NA_real_)
        } else {
          NA_real_
        }
        if (!is.na(sh_p) && sh_p > 0.05) "paired_t" else "wilcoxon_paired"
      }
    },

    sig_star = ifelse(!is.na(p_value) & p_value < 0.05, "*", ""),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    IN_mean    = round(IN_mean, digits_conc),    IN_ci95    = round(IN_ci95, digits_conc),
    EX_mean    = round(EX_mean, digits_conc),    EX_ci95    = round(EX_ci95, digits_conc),
    Delta_mean = round(Delta_mean, digits_conc), Delta_ci95 = round(Delta_ci95, digits_conc),
    Q_mean     = round(Q_mean, digits_Q),        Q_ci95     = round(Q_ci95, digits_Q),
    Flux_mean  = round(Flux_mean, digits_flux),  Flux_ci95  = round(Flux_ci95, digits_flux),
    pct_I      = round(pct_I, 1),                pct_E      = round(pct_E, 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(species, parameter)
# ==============================================================================
# 5) SAVE
# ==============================================================================
scenario_filetag <- scenario_keep %>%
  str_replace_all("\\s+", "_") %>%
  str_replace_all("\\+", "plus") %>%
  str_replace_all("[^A-Za-z0-9_\\-]", "")

raw_path     <- file.path(out_dir, paste0("RAW_individual_values_", scenario_filetag, ".csv"))
summary_path <- file.path(out_dir, paste0("SUMMARY_species_means_", scenario_filetag, ".csv"))

write_csv(raw_individual_tbl, raw_path, na = "")
write_csv(summary_species_tbl, summary_path, na = "")

cat("Saved:\n", raw_path, "\n", summary_path, "\n")

```

### QC TABLE — parameter ranges and dispersion
```{r, results='asis', fig.width=12, fig.height=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

# ---- helper: table of extremes ----
qc_extremes_tbl <- function(param_name, n_show = 8) {
  raw_individual_tbl_num %>%
    filter(parameter == param_name, is.finite(Delta)) %>%
    group_by(species) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(Delta))) %>%
    slice_head(n = n_show) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    select(species, parameter, unique_code, IN, EX, Delta, Q_L_hr, Flux_umol_hr)
}

# ---- main: plots for one parameter ----
qc_plots_for_param <- function(param_name, iqr_k = 1.5, label_n = 8) {

  df <- raw_individual_tbl_num %>%
    filter(parameter == param_name) %>%
    mutate(pair_ok = is.finite(IN) & is.finite(EX))

  if (nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)

  df2 <- df %>%
    group_by(species) %>%
    mutate(
      Q1 = quantile(Delta, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
      Q3 = quantile(Delta, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
      IQR = Q3 - Q1,
      lower = Q1 - iqr_k * IQR,
      upper = Q3 + iqr_k * IQR,
      outlier = ifelse(is.finite(Delta) & (Delta < lower | Delta > upper), TRUE, FALSE)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()

  label_df <- df2 %>%
    filter(is.finite(Delta)) %>%
    arrange(desc(outlier), desc(abs(Delta))) %>%
    slice_head(n = label_n)

  p_delta <- ggplot(df2, aes(x = Delta, y = reorder(unique_code, Delta))) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_point(aes(shape = outlier), size = 2.5) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c(`FALSE` = 16, `TRUE` = 17)) +
    facet_wrap(~ species, scales = "free_y") +
    labs(title = paste("Delta (IN−EX) —", param_name),
         x = "Delta (IN − EX)", y = "unique_code") +
    theme_bw() +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data = label_df, aes(label = unique_code),
      size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf, segment.alpha = 0.4
    )

  p_hist <- ggplot(df2, aes(x = Delta)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 18) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    facet_wrap(~ species, scales = "free") +
    labs(title = paste("Delta histogram —", param_name),
         x = "Delta (IN − EX)", y = "count") +
    theme_bw()

  p_flux <- ggplot(df2, aes(x = Q_L_hr, y = Flux_umol_hr)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_point(aes(shape = outlier), size = 2.5) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c(`FALSE` = 16, `TRUE` = 17)) +
    facet_wrap(~ species, scales = "free") +
    labs(title = paste("Flux vs Q —", param_name),
         x = "Q (L hr⁻¹)", y = "Flux (umol hr⁻¹)") +
    theme_bw() +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data = label_df, aes(label = unique_code),
      size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf, segment.alpha = 0.4
    )

  list(p_delta = p_delta, p_hist = p_hist, p_flux = p_flux)
}

# ---- run for ALL parameters ----
params <- raw_individual_tbl_num %>%
  distinct(parameter) %>%
  arrange(parameter) %>%
  pull(parameter)

for (p in params) {
  cat("\n\n# QC — ", p, "\n\n")

  plots <- qc_plots_for_param(p)
  if (is.null(plots)) next

  print(plots$p_delta)
  print(plots$p_hist)
  print(plots$p_flux)

  cat("\n\n**Top extremes (by |Delta|) — ", p, "**\n\n")
  print(qc_extremes_tbl(p, n_show = 6), n = 200)
}
```


### REMOVE specific bad rows (parameter × unique_code)
```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=10}
# ==============================================================================
# REMOVE specific bad rows (parameter × unique_code)
# ==============================================================================

exclusions <- tibble::tribble(
  ~parameter, ~unique_code,
  "DOC",   "10.42.1",
  "O2",    "10.23.1",
  "O2",    "10.7.1",
  "Pro_C", "11.53C.1",
  "TN",    "10.16.1",
  "DON",   "10.16.1",
  "PO4",   "10.21.1",
  "Pro_N", "11.53C.1"
)

raw_individual_tbl_num <- raw_individual_tbl_num %>%
  mutate(
    parameter   = stringr::str_trim(as.character(parameter)),
    unique_code = stringr::str_trim(as.character(unique_code))
  ) %>%
  anti_join(exclusions, by = c("parameter", "unique_code"))

cat("Rows removed. Remaining rows:", nrow(raw_individual_tbl_num), "\n")

raw_individual_tbl <- raw_individual_tbl_num %>%
  mutate(
    IN           = round(IN, digits_conc),
    EX           = round(EX, digits_conc),
    Delta        = round(Delta, digits_conc),
    Q_L_hr       = round(Q_L_hr, digits_Q),
    Flux_umol_hr = round(Flux_umol_hr, digits_flux)
  )

summary_species_tbl <- raw_individual_tbl_num %>%
  mutate(pair_ok = is.finite(IN) & is.finite(EX)) %>%
  group_by(species, parameter) %>%
  summarise(
    IN_mean    = mean_ci95(IN)$mean,    IN_ci95    = mean_ci95(IN)$ci95,
    EX_mean    = mean_ci95(EX)$mean,    EX_ci95    = mean_ci95(EX)$ci95,
    Delta_mean = mean_ci95(Delta)$mean, Delta_ci95 = mean_ci95(Delta)$ci95,
    Q_mean     = mean_ci95(Q_L_hr)$mean, Q_ci95    = mean_ci95(Q_L_hr)$ci95,
    Flux_mean  = mean_ci95(Flux_umol_hr)$mean, Flux_ci95 = mean_ci95(Flux_umol_hr)$ci95,
    n          = mean_ci95(Delta)$n,
    n_pairs    = sum(pair_ok),
    pct_I      = ifelse(n_pairs == 0, NA_real_, 100 * sum(pair_ok & Delta > 0, na.rm = TRUE) / n_pairs),
    pct_E      = ifelse(n_pairs == 0, NA_real_, 100 * sum(pair_ok & Delta < 0, na.rm = TRUE) / n_pairs),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    IN_mean    = round(IN_mean, digits_conc),
    EX_mean    = round(EX_mean, digits_conc),
    Delta_mean = round(Delta_mean, digits_conc),
    Q_mean     = round(Q_mean, digits_Q),
    Flux_mean  = round(Flux_mean, digits_flux),
    pct_I      = round(pct_I, 1),
    pct_E      = round(pct_E, 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(species, parameter)

write_csv(raw_individual_tbl, raw_path, na = "")
write_csv(summary_species_tbl, summary_path, na = "")

cat("Files overwritten:\n", raw_path, "\n", summary_path, "\n")

```


### Agelas-only tables + Volume-normalized fluxes

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=10}


################################################################################
# Agelas-only tables + Volume-normalized fluxes + model-ready parameter ranges
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PURPOSE
#   Build clean Agelas-only tables from your existing objects:
#     1) RAW_Agelas_...csv
#     2) SUMMARY_Agelas_...csv
#     3) RATIO_RANGES_Agelas_...csv      (CN_Bac, CN_DOM, RQ from raw-level ratios)
#     4) MODEL_RANGES_Agelas_...csv      (initial min/max table for model input)
#
# IMPORTANT FIXES INCLUDED
#   - Avoid many-to-many join by forcing ONE volume per unique_code
#   - Add robust summary helper with mean / ci95 / min / max / q05 / q95 / sd
#   - Keep a numeric version for calculations and a pretty version for export
#   - Compute CN and RQ from raw/individual-level values when possible
#
# NORMALIZATION
#   Flux_umol_hr_ml   = Flux_umol_hr / Volume_ml
#                     = µmol hr^-1 ml^-1 = µmol hr^-1 cm^-3
#
#   Flux_umol_day_cm3 = Flux_umol_hr * 24 / Volume_ml
#                     = µmol day^-1 cm^-3
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0) CHECK REQUIRED OBJECTS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
stopifnot(exists("raw_individual_tbl_num"))
stopifnot(exists("sponge_db2_with_cn"))
stopifnot(exists("scenario_filetag"))
stopifnot(exists("out_dir"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) SETTINGS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
digits_conc <- 2
digits_flux <- 2
digits_vol  <- 1

species_keep <- "Agelas oroides"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Summary helper:
# returns mean, CI95, n, min, max, q05, q95, sd
mean_ci95_range <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  n <- length(x)

  if (n == 0) {
    return(tibble(
      mean = NA_real_,
      ci95 = NA_real_,
      n    = 0L,
      min  = NA_real_,
      max  = NA_real_,
      q05  = NA_real_,
      q95  = NA_real_,
      sd   = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  if (n == 1) {
    return(tibble(
      mean = x[1],
      ci95 = NA_real_,
      n    = 1L,
      min  = x[1],
      max  = x[1],
      q05  = x[1],
      q95  = x[1],
      sd   = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  m  <- mean(x)
  s  <- sd(x)
  se <- s / sqrt(n)
  ci <- qt(0.975, df = n - 1) * se

  tibble(
    mean = m,
    ci95 = ci,
    n    = as.integer(n),
    min  = min(x),
    max  = max(x),
    q05  = unname(quantile(x, probs = 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)),
    q95  = unname(quantile(x, probs = 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)),
    sd   = s
  )
}

# Safe first finite value
first_finite_or_na <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  if (length(x) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  x[1]
}

# Safe ratio
safe_ratio <- function(num, den) {
  if (!is.finite(num) || !is.finite(den) || den == 0) return(NA_real_)
  num / den
}

# Extract one value from summary table by parameter + column
get_summary_value <- function(df, param, colname) {
  x <- df %>%
    filter(parameter == param) %>%
    pull(all_of(colname))
  if (length(x) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x[1]))
}

# Small helper to summarise one vector into named values
vec_stats <- function(x) {
  s <- mean_ci95_range(x)
  tibble(
    mean = s$mean,
    ci95 = s$ci95,
    min  = s$min,
    max  = s$max,
    q05  = s$q05,
    q95  = s$q95,
    sd   = s$sd,
    n    = s$n
  )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) BUILD CLEAN VOLUME TABLE (ONE VOLUME PER unique_code)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Why:
#   Previously distinct(unique_code, volume_ml) could still leave >1 row per
#   unique_code if the same sponge appeared with different volume values.
#   That causes many-to-many joins and duplicates downstream.

agelas_vol_long <- sponge_db2_with_cn %>%
  transmute(
    unique_code = str_trim(as.character(unique_code)),
    species     = as.character(species),
    volume_ml   = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(estimated_sponge_volume_ml))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    species == species_keep,
    !is.na(unique_code),
    unique_code != ""
  )

# QC table: which unique_code has >1 distinct finite volume?
agelas_vol_qc <- agelas_vol_long %>%
  filter(is.finite(volume_ml)) %>%
  distinct(unique_code, volume_ml) %>%
  count(unique_code, name = "n_distinct_volumes") %>%
  filter(n_distinct_volumes > 1)

# Collapse to one volume per unique_code
# Rule used here:
#   take first finite volume found per unique_code
# If you prefer median instead, replace first_finite_or_na(volume_ml) with median(...)
agelas_vol <- agelas_vol_long %>%
  group_by(unique_code) %>%
  summarise(
    volume_ml = first_finite_or_na(volume_ml),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) BUILD RAW AGELAS TABLE + VOLUME-NORMALIZED FLUXES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
agelas_raw_num <- raw_individual_tbl_num %>%
  mutate(unique_code = str_trim(as.character(unique_code))) %>%
  filter(species == species_keep) %>%
  left_join(agelas_vol, by = "unique_code") %>%
  mutate(
    Flux_umol_hr_ml = ifelse(
      is.finite(Flux_umol_hr) & is.finite(volume_ml) & volume_ml > 0,
      Flux_umol_hr / volume_ml,
      NA_real_
    ),
    Flux_umol_day_cm3 = ifelse(
      is.finite(Flux_umol_hr) & is.finite(volume_ml) & volume_ml > 0,
      Flux_umol_hr * 24 / volume_ml,
      NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(parameter, unique_code)

# Pretty export version
agelas_raw <- agelas_raw_num %>%
  mutate(
    IN               = round(IN, digits_conc),
    EX               = round(EX, digits_conc),
    Delta            = round(Delta, digits_conc),
    Q_L_hr           = round(Q_L_hr, digits_flux),
    Flux_umol_hr     = round(Flux_umol_hr, digits_flux),
    volume_ml        = round(volume_ml, digits_vol),
    Flux_umol_hr_ml  = round(Flux_umol_hr_ml, digits_flux),
    Flux_umol_day_cm3 = round(Flux_umol_day_cm3, digits_flux)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) SUMMARY TABLE (AGELAS ONLY) WITH EXTENDED STATS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
agelas_summary_num <- agelas_raw_num %>%
  mutate(pair_ok = is.finite(IN) & is.finite(EX)) %>%
  group_by(species, parameter) %>%
  summarise(

    # ------------------------------
    # Concentrations / Delta
    # ------------------------------
    IN_mean    = mean_ci95_range(IN)$mean,
    IN_ci95    = mean_ci95_range(IN)$ci95,
    IN_min     = mean_ci95_range(IN)$min,
    IN_max     = mean_ci95_range(IN)$max,
    IN_q05     = mean_ci95_range(IN)$q05,
    IN_q95     = mean_ci95_range(IN)$q95,

    EX_mean    = mean_ci95_range(EX)$mean,
    EX_ci95    = mean_ci95_range(EX)$ci95,
    EX_min     = mean_ci95_range(EX)$min,
    EX_max     = mean_ci95_range(EX)$max,
    EX_q05     = mean_ci95_range(EX)$q05,
    EX_q95     = mean_ci95_range(EX)$q95,

    Delta_mean = mean_ci95_range(Delta)$mean,
    Delta_ci95 = mean_ci95_range(Delta)$ci95,
    Delta_min  = mean_ci95_range(Delta)$min,
    Delta_max  = mean_ci95_range(Delta)$max,
    Delta_q05  = mean_ci95_range(Delta)$q05,
    Delta_q95  = mean_ci95_range(Delta)$q95,

    # ------------------------------
    # Flow / Flux
    # ------------------------------
    Q_mean     = mean_ci95_range(Q_L_hr)$mean,
    Q_ci95     = mean_ci95_range(Q_L_hr)$ci95,
    Q_min      = mean_ci95_range(Q_L_hr)$min,
    Q_max      = mean_ci95_range(Q_L_hr)$max,

    Flux_mean  = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_hr)$mean,
    Flux_ci95  = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_hr)$ci95,
    Flux_min   = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_hr)$min,
    Flux_max   = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_hr)$max,
    Flux_q05   = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_hr)$q05,
    Flux_q95   = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_hr)$q95,

    # ------------------------------
    # Volume + normalized flux
    # ------------------------------
    Vol_mean   = mean_ci95_range(volume_ml)$mean,
    Vol_ci95   = mean_ci95_range(volume_ml)$ci95,
    Vol_min    = mean_ci95_range(volume_ml)$min,
    Vol_max    = mean_ci95_range(volume_ml)$max,

    FluxVol_hr_mean  = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_hr_ml)$mean,
    FluxVol_hr_ci95  = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_hr_ml)$ci95,
    FluxVol_hr_min   = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_hr_ml)$min,
    FluxVol_hr_max   = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_hr_ml)$max,
    FluxVol_hr_q05   = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_hr_ml)$q05,
    FluxVol_hr_q95   = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_hr_ml)$q95,

    FluxVol_day_mean = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_day_cm3)$mean,
    FluxVol_day_ci95 = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_day_cm3)$ci95,
    FluxVol_day_min  = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_day_cm3)$min,
    FluxVol_day_max  = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_day_cm3)$max,
    FluxVol_day_q05  = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_day_cm3)$q05,
    FluxVol_day_q95  = mean_ci95_range(Flux_umol_day_cm3)$q95,

    # ------------------------------
    # Counts and sign frequencies
    # ------------------------------
    n       = mean_ci95_range(Delta)$n,
    n_pairs = sum(pair_ok),

    pct_I = ifelse(
      sum(pair_ok) == 0,
      NA_real_,
      100 * sum(pair_ok & Delta > 0, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(pair_ok)
    ),

    pct_E = ifelse(
      sum(pair_ok) == 0,
      NA_real_,
      100 * sum(pair_ok & Delta < 0, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(pair_ok)
    ),

    # ------------------------------
    # Paired test IN vs EX
    # ------------------------------
    p_value = {
      d <- Delta[pair_ok]
      if (length(d) < 2 || length(unique(d[is.finite(d)])) < 2) {
        NA_real_
      } else {
        sh_p <- tryCatch(shapiro.test(d)$p.value, error = function(e) NA_real_)
        if (!is.na(sh_p) && sh_p > 0.05) {
          tryCatch(
            t.test(IN[pair_ok], EX[pair_ok], paired = TRUE)$p.value,
            error = function(e) NA_real_
          )
        } else {
          tryCatch(
            wilcox.test(IN[pair_ok], EX[pair_ok], paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE)$p.value,
            error = function(e) NA_real_
          )
        }
      }
    },

    test_used = {
      d <- Delta[pair_ok]
      if (length(d) < 2 || length(unique(d[is.finite(d)])) < 2) {
        NA_character_
      } else {
        sh_p <- tryCatch(shapiro.test(d)$p.value, error = function(e) NA_real_)
        if (!is.na(sh_p) && sh_p > 0.05) "paired_t" else "wilcoxon_paired"
      }
    },

    sig_star = ifelse(!is.na(p_value) & p_value < 0.05, "*", ""),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      c(IN_mean, IN_ci95, IN_min, IN_max, IN_q05, IN_q95,
        EX_mean, EX_ci95, EX_min, EX_max, EX_q05, EX_q95,
        Delta_mean, Delta_ci95, Delta_min, Delta_max, Delta_q05, Delta_q95),
      ~ round(.x, digits_conc)
    ),
    across(
      c(Q_mean, Q_ci95, Q_min, Q_max,
        Flux_mean, Flux_ci95, Flux_min, Flux_max, Flux_q05, Flux_q95,
        FluxVol_hr_mean, FluxVol_hr_ci95, FluxVol_hr_min, FluxVol_hr_max, FluxVol_hr_q05, FluxVol_hr_q95,
        FluxVol_day_mean, FluxVol_day_ci95, FluxVol_day_min, FluxVol_day_max, FluxVol_day_q05, FluxVol_day_q95),
      ~ round(.x, digits_flux)
    ),
    across(c(Vol_mean, Vol_ci95, Vol_min, Vol_max), ~ round(.x, digits_vol)),
    pct_I = round(pct_I, 1),
    pct_E = round(pct_E, 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(parameter)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) ADD PUBLICATION-STYLE UNIT LABELS TO EXPORT TABLES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
agelas_raw_units <- agelas_raw %>%
  rename(
    `IN (µmol L^-1)`                 = IN,
    `EX (µmol L^-1)`                 = EX,
    `Delta (µmol L^-1)`              = Delta,
    `Q (L hr^-1)`                    = Q_L_hr,
    `Flux (µmol hr^-1)`              = Flux_umol_hr,
    `Volume (cm^3)`                  = volume_ml,
    `FluxVol_hr (µmol hr^-1 cm^-3)`  = Flux_umol_hr_ml,
    `FluxVol_day (µmol day^-1 cm^-3)`= Flux_umol_day_cm3
  )

agelas_summary_units <- agelas_summary_num %>%
  rename(
    `IN_mean (µmol L^-1)`         = IN_mean,
    `IN_ci95 (µmol L^-1)`         = IN_ci95,
    `IN_min (µmol L^-1)`          = IN_min,
    `IN_max (µmol L^-1)`          = IN_max,
    `IN_q05 (µmol L^-1)`          = IN_q05,
    `IN_q95 (µmol L^-1)`          = IN_q95,

    `EX_mean (µmol L^-1)`         = EX_mean,
    `EX_ci95 (µmol L^-1)`         = EX_ci95,
    `EX_min (µmol L^-1)`          = EX_min,
    `EX_max (µmol L^-1)`          = EX_max,
    `EX_q05 (µmol L^-1)`          = EX_q05,
    `EX_q95 (µmol L^-1)`          = EX_q95,

    `Delta_mean (µmol L^-1)`      = Delta_mean,
    `Delta_ci95 (µmol L^-1)`      = Delta_ci95,
    `Delta_min (µmol L^-1)`       = Delta_min,
    `Delta_max (µmol L^-1)`       = Delta_max,
    `Delta_q05 (µmol L^-1)`       = Delta_q05,
    `Delta_q95 (µmol L^-1)`       = Delta_q95,

    `Q_mean (L hr^-1)`            = Q_mean,
    `Q_ci95 (L hr^-1)`            = Q_ci95,
    `Q_min (L hr^-1)`             = Q_min,
    `Q_max (L hr^-1)`             = Q_max,

    `Flux_mean (µmol hr^-1)`      = Flux_mean,
    `Flux_ci95 (µmol hr^-1)`      = Flux_ci95,
    `Flux_min (µmol hr^-1)`       = Flux_min,
    `Flux_max (µmol hr^-1)`       = Flux_max,
    `Flux_q05 (µmol hr^-1)`       = Flux_q05,
    `Flux_q95 (µmol hr^-1)`       = Flux_q95,

    `Volume_mean (cm^3)`          = Vol_mean,
    `Volume_ci95 (cm^3)`          = Vol_ci95,
    `Volume_min (cm^3)`           = Vol_min,
    `Volume_max (cm^3)`           = Vol_max,

    `FluxVol_hr_mean (µmol hr^-1 cm^-3)`   = FluxVol_hr_mean,
    `FluxVol_hr_ci95 (µmol hr^-1 cm^-3)`   = FluxVol_hr_ci95,
    `FluxVol_hr_min (µmol hr^-1 cm^-3)`    = FluxVol_hr_min,
    `FluxVol_hr_max (µmol hr^-1 cm^-3)`    = FluxVol_hr_max,
    `FluxVol_hr_q05 (µmol hr^-1 cm^-3)`    = FluxVol_hr_q05,
    `FluxVol_hr_q95 (µmol hr^-1 cm^-3)`    = FluxVol_hr_q95,

    `FluxVol_day_mean (µmol day^-1 cm^-3)` = FluxVol_day_mean,
    `FluxVol_day_ci95 (µmol day^-1 cm^-3)` = FluxVol_day_ci95,
    `FluxVol_day_min (µmol day^-1 cm^-3)`  = FluxVol_day_min,
    `FluxVol_day_max (µmol day^-1 cm^-3)`  = FluxVol_day_max,
    `FluxVol_day_q05 (µmol day^-1 cm^-3)`  = FluxVol_day_q05,
    `FluxVol_day_q95 (µmol day^-1 cm^-3)`  = FluxVol_day_q95
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7) CN_Bac AND CN_DOM FROM RAW-LEVEL RATIOS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Why this fix is needed:
#   Some unique_code × parameter combinations appear more than once.
#   So before pivot_wider(), we collapse them to one value per sponge and parameter.
#   Here we use mean(IN), ignoring NA/non-finite values.

cn_input_collapsed <- agelas_raw_num %>%
  filter(parameter %in% c("Bact_C", "Bact_N", "DOC", "DON")) %>%
  group_by(unique_code, parameter) %>%
  summarise(
    IN_value = {
      x <- IN[is.finite(IN)]
      if (length(x) == 0) NA_real_ else mean(x)
    },
    n_rows = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Optional QC: duplicates that were collapsed
cn_input_qc <- cn_input_collapsed %>%
  filter(n_rows > 1)

cn_ratio_raw <- cn_input_collapsed %>%
  select(unique_code, parameter, IN_value) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = parameter, values_from = IN_value) %>%
  mutate(
    CN_Bac = ifelse(
      is.finite(Bact_C) & is.finite(Bact_N) & Bact_N != 0,
      Bact_C / Bact_N,
      NA_real_
    ),
    CN_DOM = ifelse(
      is.finite(DOC) & is.finite(DON) & DON != 0,
      DOC / DON,
      NA_real_
    )
  )

cn_ratio_summary <- bind_rows(
  vec_stats(cn_ratio_raw$CN_Bac) %>% mutate(parameter = "CN_Bac", .before = 1),
  vec_stats(cn_ratio_raw$CN_DOM) %>% mutate(parameter = "CN_DOM", .before = 1)
) %>%
  mutate(across(c(mean, ci95, min, max, q05, q95, sd), ~ round(.x, 3)))


# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 8) RQ FROM RAW-LEVEL VOLUME-NORMALIZED FLUXES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# We first collapse duplicate unique_code × parameter rows to one mean value.

rq_input_collapsed <- agelas_raw_num %>%
  filter(parameter %in% c("O2", "DIC_pH", "DIC_dicex")) %>%
  group_by(unique_code, parameter) %>%
  summarise(
    Flux_day_value = {
      x <- Flux_umol_day_cm3[is.finite(Flux_umol_day_cm3)]
      if (length(x) == 0) NA_real_ else mean(x)
    },
    n_rows = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Optional QC: duplicates that were collapsed
rq_input_qc <- rq_input_collapsed %>%
  filter(n_rows > 1)

rq_raw <- rq_input_collapsed %>%
  select(unique_code, parameter, Flux_day_value) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = parameter, values_from = Flux_day_value) %>%
  mutate(
    CO2prod_pH    = ifelse(is.finite(DIC_pH),    abs(DIC_pH),    NA_real_),
    CO2prod_dicex = ifelse(is.finite(DIC_dicex), abs(DIC_dicex), NA_real_),

    RQ_classic_pH = ifelse(
      is.finite(O2) & is.finite(CO2prod_pH) & O2 > 0 & CO2prod_pH > 0,
      CO2prod_pH / O2,
      NA_real_
    ),
    RQ_model_pH = ifelse(
      is.finite(O2) & is.finite(CO2prod_pH) & O2 > 0 & CO2prod_pH > 0,
      O2 / CO2prod_pH,
      NA_real_
    ),

    RQ_classic_dicex = ifelse(
      is.finite(O2) & is.finite(CO2prod_dicex) & O2 > 0 & CO2prod_dicex > 0,
      CO2prod_dicex / O2,
      NA_real_
    ),
    RQ_model_dicex = ifelse(
      is.finite(O2) & is.finite(CO2prod_dicex) & O2 > 0 & CO2prod_dicex > 0,
      O2 / CO2prod_dicex,
      NA_real_
    )
  )

rq_summary <- bind_rows(
  vec_stats(rq_raw$RQ_classic_pH)    %>% mutate(parameter = "RQ_classic_pH",    .before = 1),
  vec_stats(rq_raw$RQ_model_pH)      %>% mutate(parameter = "RQ_model_pH",      .before = 1),
  vec_stats(rq_raw$RQ_classic_dicex) %>% mutate(parameter = "RQ_classic_dicex", .before = 1),
  vec_stats(rq_raw$RQ_model_dicex)   %>% mutate(parameter = "RQ_model_dicex",   .before = 1)
) %>%
  mutate(across(c(mean, ci95, min, max, q05, q95, sd), ~ round(.x, 3)))

rq_out <- rq_summary
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9) BUILD INITIAL MODEL RANGE TABLE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# This is the table we will later use to feed the Monte Carlo model.
# For measured fluxes we use FluxVol_day summary ranges.
# For CN and RQ we use ratio summaries above.
#
# IMPORTANT:
#   Check the PARAMETER names below against your model naming conventions.
#   Example:
#     O2 in the summary table may correspond to Oxyupt in the model.
#     DOC may correspond to DOCupt, etc.
#
# Also IMPORTANT:
#   NH4 / NO2 / NO3 sign conventions must match the model exactly.
#   Here we only extract your observed min/max as stored in your table.

model_ranges <- tibble(
  parameter = c(
    "CN_Bac",
    "CN_DOM",
    "RQ_model_pH",
    "RQ_model_dicex",
    "O2",
    "DOC",
    "Bact_C",
    "NH4",
    "NO2",
    "NO3"
  ),
  min = c(
    cn_ratio_summary %>% filter(parameter == "CN_Bac") %>% pull(min),
    cn_ratio_summary %>% filter(parameter == "CN_DOM") %>% pull(min),
    rq_summary %>% filter(parameter == "RQ_model_pH") %>% pull(min),
    rq_summary %>% filter(parameter == "RQ_model_dicex") %>% pull(min),
    get_summary_value(agelas_summary_num, "O2",     "FluxVol_day_min"),
    get_summary_value(agelas_summary_num, "DOC",    "FluxVol_day_min"),
    get_summary_value(agelas_summary_num, "Bact_C", "FluxVol_day_min"),
    get_summary_value(agelas_summary_num, "NH4",    "FluxVol_day_min"),
    get_summary_value(agelas_summary_num, "NO2",    "FluxVol_day_min"),
    get_summary_value(agelas_summary_num, "NO3",    "FluxVol_day_min")
  ),
  max = c(
    cn_ratio_summary %>% filter(parameter == "CN_Bac") %>% pull(max),
    cn_ratio_summary %>% filter(parameter == "CN_DOM") %>% pull(max),
    rq_summary %>% filter(parameter == "RQ_model_pH") %>% pull(max),
    rq_summary %>% filter(parameter == "RQ_model_dicex") %>% pull(max),
    get_summary_value(agelas_summary_num, "O2",     "FluxVol_day_max"),
    get_summary_value(agelas_summary_num, "DOC",    "FluxVol_day_max"),
    get_summary_value(agelas_summary_num, "Bact_C", "FluxVol_day_max"),
    get_summary_value(agelas_summary_num, "NH4",    "FluxVol_day_max"),
    get_summary_value(agelas_summary_num, "NO2",    "FluxVol_day_max"),
    get_summary_value(agelas_summary_num, "NO3",    "FluxVol_day_max")
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    min = round(min, 3),
    max = round(max, 3)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 10) SAVE ALL OUTPUTS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
agelas_dir <- file.path(out_dir, "Agelas_oroides")
dir.create(agelas_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

raw_path_agelas    <- file.path(agelas_dir, paste0("RAW_Agelas_", scenario_filetag, "_volnorm.csv"))
```
