```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
    TN_in  = mean(TN_in,  na.rm = TRUE),
    TN_ex  = mean(TN_ex,  na.rm = TRUE),

    NOx_in = mean(NOx_in, na.rm = TRUE),
    NOx_ex = mean(NOx_ex, na.rm = TRUE),

    NH4_in = mean(NH4_in, na.rm = TRUE),
    NH4_ex = mean(NH4_ex, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Deltas (Δ = IN - EX)
    dDOC = DOC_in - DOC_ex,
    dTN  = TN_in  - TN_ex,
    dNOx = NOx_in - NOx_ex,
    dNH4 = NH4_in - NH4_ex,

    # ΔDIN robust NA handling:
    # - if both NOx and NH4 missing -> ΔDIN NA
    # - else treat missing component as 0
    dDIN = if_else(is.na(dNOx) & is.na(dNH4), NA_real_, coalesce(dNOx, 0) + coalesce(dNH4, 0)),

    # ΔDON = ΔTN - ΔDIN (only if both exist)
    dDON = if_else(!is.na(dTN) & !is.na(dDIN), dTN - dDIN, NA_real_),

    # Classify sign patterns (very useful in Results/Discussion)
    dom_mode = case_when(
      is.na(dDOC) | is.na(dDON)              ~ "missing",
      dDOC > 0 & dDON > 0                    ~ "uptake DOC + uptake DON",
      dDOC > 0 & dDON < 0                    ~ "uptake DOC + release DON",
      dDOC < 0 & dDON > 0                    ~ "release DOC + uptake DON",
      dDOC < 0 & dDON < 0                    ~ "release DOC + release DON",
      dDOC == 0 | dDON == 0                  ~ "zero (one component)",
      TRUE                                   ~ "other"
    ),

    # Ratio ONLY meaningful for assimilated DOM (both uptake)
    CN_assimilated = if_else(dDOC > 0 & dDON > 0, dDOC / dDON, NA_real_)
  )

# Quick QC summary
qc <- dom_delta_uc %>%
  summarise(
    n_total = n(),
    n_have_dDOC = sum(is.finite(dDOC)),
    n_have_dDON = sum(is.finite(dDON)),
    n_uptake_both = sum(is.finite(CN_assimilated)),
    frac_dDON_negative = mean(is.finite(dDON) & dDON < 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
print(qc)

# -----------------------------
# 2) Species summaries: ΔDOC and ΔDON + mode fractions
# -----------------------------
species_summary <- dom_delta_uc %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_ci95(dDOC) %>% setNames(c("dDOC_mean","dDOC_lo","dDOC_hi","n_dDOC")),
    mean_ci95(dDON) %>% setNames(c("dDON_mean","dDON_lo","dDON_hi","n_dDON")),
    mean_ci95(CN_assimilated) %>% setNames(c("CN_mean","CN_lo","CN_hi","n_CN")),
    n_total = n(),
    frac_uptake_both = mean(dom_mode == "uptake DOC + uptake DON", na.rm = TRUE),
    frac_release_DON = mean(dom_mode == "uptake DOC + release DON", na.rm = TRUE),
    frac_missing = mean(dom_mode == "missing", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(Species)

print(species_summary)

# Factor order: by dDOC_mean (or choose dDON_mean)
sp_levels <- species_summary %>%
  arrange(dDOC_mean) %>%
  pull(Species)

dom_delta_uc <- dom_delta_uc %>%
  mutate(Species = factor(Species, levels = sp_levels))

species_summary <- species_summary %>%
  mutate(Species = factor(Species, levels = sp_levels))

# -----------------------------
# 3) Plot A: ΔDOC by species
# -----------------------------
p_dDOC <- ggplot(dom_delta_uc, aes(x = Species, y = dDOC)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.12, height = 0), size = 2, alpha = 0.75) +
geom_point(
  data = species_summary,
  aes(x = Species, y = dDOC_mean),
  inherit.aes = FALSE,
  size = 3.3
) +
geom_errorbar(
  data = species_summary,
  aes(x = Species, ymin = dDOC_lo, ymax = dDOC_hi),
  inherit.aes = FALSE,
  width = 0.18,
  linewidth = 0.6
) +
  labs(
    title = expression(paste(Delta, "DOC by individual and species")),
    subtitle = expression(paste(Delta, " = IN − EX. Positive = DOC removal from water (uptake).")),
    x = NULL,
    y = expression(paste(Delta, "DOC (", mu, "mol L"^{-1}, ")"))
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))

print(p_dDOC)

# -----------------------------
# 4) Plot B: ΔDON by species
# -----------------------------
p_dDON <- ggplot(dom_delta_uc, aes(x = Species, y = dDON)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.12, height = 0), size = 2, alpha = 0.75) +
geom_point(
  data = species_summary,
  aes(x = Species, y = dDON_mean),
  inherit.aes = FALSE,
  size = 3.3
) +
geom_errorbar(
  data = species_summary,
  aes(x = Species, ymin = dDON_lo, ymax = dDON_hi),
  inherit.aes = FALSE,
  width = 0.18,
  linewidth = 0.6
) +
  labs(
    title = expression(paste(Delta, "DON by individual and species")),
    subtitle = expression(paste(Delta, "DON = ", Delta, "TN − (", Delta, "NOx + ", Delta, "NH"[4]^"+", "). Positive = DON uptake; Negative = DON release.")),
    x = NULL,
    y = expression(paste(Delta, "DON (", mu, "mol L"^{-1}, ")"))
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))

print(p_dDON)

# -----------------------------
# 5) Plot C: ΔDOC vs ΔDON (quadrants)
# -----------------------------
p_scatter <- ggplot(dom_delta_uc, aes(x = dDOC, y = dDON)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Species), size = 2.2, alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = expression(paste(Delta, "DOC vs ", Delta, "DON (quadrant view)")),
    subtitle = "Each point is one individual (Unique_code mean). Quadrants separate uptake vs release.",
    x = expression(paste(Delta, "DOC (IN − EX)")),
    y = expression(paste(Delta, "DON (IN − EX)"))
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)

print(p_scatter)

# -----------------------------
# 6) Optional: Plot D — C:N of ASSIMILATED DOM only (ΔDOC>0 & ΔDON>0)
# -----------------------------
dom_uptake_only <- dom_delta_uc %>%
  filter(is.finite(CN_assimilated))

if (nrow(dom_uptake_only) > 0) {

  cn_species <- dom_uptake_only %>%
    group_by(Species) %>%
    summarise(mean_ci95(CN_assimilated), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(Species = factor(Species, levels = sp_levels))

  p_cn <- ggplot(dom_uptake_only %>% mutate(Species = factor(Species, levels = sp_levels)),
                 aes(x = Species, y = CN_assimilated)) +
    geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.12, height = 0), size = 2, alpha = 0.75) +
    geom_point(data = cn_species, aes(y = mean), inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 3.3) +
    geom_errorbar(data = cn_species, aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), inherit.aes = FALSE,
                  width = 0.18, linewidth = 0.6) +
    labs(
      title = expression(paste("C:N of assimilated DOM (", Delta, "DOC/", Delta, "DON)")),
      subtitle = expression(paste("Only individuals with ", Delta, "DOC > 0 and ", Delta, "DON > 0 are included.")),
      x = NULL,
      y = expression(paste(Delta, "DOC / ", Delta, "DON"))
    ) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))

  print(p_cn)

} else {
  message("No individuals with ΔDOC>0 and ΔDON>0. CN_assimilated cannot be computed meaningfully.")
}

################################################################################
# Optional saving (uncomment + set out_dir)
################################################################################
# out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/Energy_budget/by_species"
# dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
#
# write.csv(dom_delta_uc, file.path(out_dir, "DOM_deltas_per_unique_code.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
# write.csv(species_summary, file.path(out_dir, "DOM_deltas_species_summary.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
#
# ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "DeltaDOC_by_species.png"), p_dDOC, width = 22, height = 14, units = "cm", dpi = 300)
# ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "DeltaDON_by_species.png"), p_dDON, width = 22, height = 14, units = "cm", dpi = 300)
# ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "DeltaDOC_vs_DeltaDON_scatter.png"), p_scatter, width = 18, height = 14, units = "cm", dpi = 300)
# if (exists("p_cn")) ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "CN_assimilated_uptake_only.png"), p_cn, width = 22, height = 14, units = "cm", dpi = 300)
################################################################################
# END
################################################################################

```

### Sankey: Species-level flux schema (your data) — FIXED joins + no many-to-many
```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
################################################################################
# Sankey: Species-level flux schema (your data) — FIXED joins + no many-to-many
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(networkD3)

stopifnot(exists("fcm_scenarios"))
stopifnot(exists("sponge_db2_with_cn"))

scenario_use <- "S2_fixed_Coastal"
min_n_unique <- 2

to_num <- function(x) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))

make_sankey <- function(links) {
  nodes <- data.frame(name = unique(c(links$from, links$to)))
  links_d3 <- links %>%
    mutate(
      source = match(from, nodes$name) - 1,
      target = match(to, nodes$name) - 1
    )

  sankeyNetwork(
    Links = links_d3,
    Nodes = nodes,
    Source = "source",
    Target = "target",
    Value  = "value",
    NodeID = "name",
    LinkGroup = "group",
    fontSize = 12,
    nodeWidth = 24
  )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0) Build ONE ROW per Unique_code from sponge_db2_with_cn (prevents many-to-many)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

uc_meta <- sponge_db2_with_cn %>%
  transmute(
    Unique_code = unique_code,
    species = species,
    ofr_l_hr = to_num(ofr_l_hr_1),

    # O2 (umol/L)
    o2_in  = to_num(oxy_in_mean_oxygen_conc_umol_l),
    o2_ex  = to_num(oxy_ex_mean_oxygen_conc_umol_l),

    # DIC from pH system (umol/L)
    dic_in = to_num(ph_in_mean_dic_conc_umol_l),
    dic_ex = to_num(ph_ex_mean_dic_conc_umol_l),

    # NOx (umol/L)
    nox_in = to_num(ex_in_nox_conc_umol_l),
    nox_ex = to_num(ex_ex_nox_conc_umol_l),

    # TN (umol/L) (proxy)
    tn_in  = to_num(dom_ex_in_tn_conc_umol_l),
    tn_ex  = to_num(dom_ex_ex_tn_conc_umol_l)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Unique_code) %>%
  summarise(
    species = dplyr::first(na.omit(species)),
    ofr_l_hr = mean(ofr_l_hr, na.rm = TRUE),

    o2_in  = mean(o2_in,  na.rm = TRUE),
    o2_ex  = mean(o2_ex,  na.rm = TRUE),

    dic_in = mean(dic_in, na.rm = TRUE),
    dic_ex = mean(dic_ex, na.rm = TRUE),

    nox_in = mean(nox_in, na.rm = TRUE),
    nox_ex = mean(nox_ex, na.rm = TRUE),

    tn_in  = mean(tn_in,  na.rm = TRUE),
    tn_ex  = mean(tn_ex,  na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # deltas (umol/L)
    O2_uptake_umol_L   = o2_in - o2_ex,     # uptake positive
    DIC_release_umol_L = dic_ex - dic_in,   # release positive
    NOx_release_umol_L = nox_ex - nox_in,   # release positive
    TN_release_umol_L  = tn_ex - tn_in,     # release positive (proxy)

    # flux (umol/hr)
    O2_uptake_umol_hr   = O2_uptake_umol_L   * ofr_l_hr,
    DIC_release_umol_hr = DIC_release_umol_L * ofr_l_hr,
    NOx_release_umol_hr = NOx_release_umol_L * ofr_l_hr,
    TN_release_umol_hr  = TN_release_umol_L  * ofr_l_hr
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Plankton C/N uptake from FCM (Scenario)
#    Compute Uptake = IN - EX per Unique_code × Taxon × Element
#    Then sum across taxa to get total plankton C and N uptake per Unique_code
#    Finally convert to flux via ofr_l_hr from uc_meta
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

fcm_uptake_by_uc <- fcm_scenarios %>%
  filter(Scenario == scenario_use) %>%
  mutate(
    InEx = toupper(InEx),
    Element = str_to_title(Element),
    umol_L = to_num(umol_L)
  ) %>%
  filter(InEx %in% c("IN", "EX"), Element %in% c("Carbon", "Nitrogen")) %>%
  group_by(Unique_code, Taxon, Element, InEx) %>%
  summarise(umol_L_mean = mean(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = InEx, values_from = umol_L_mean) %>%
  mutate(
    Uptake_umol_L = IN - EX
  ) %>%
  group_by(Unique_code, Element) %>%
  summarise(
    Uptake_umol_L = sum(Uptake_umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Element, values_from = Uptake_umol_L) %>%
  mutate(
    plankton_C_upt_umol_L = coalesce(Carbon, 0),
    plankton_N_upt_umol_L = coalesce(Nitrogen, 0)
  ) %>%
  select(Unique_code, plankton_C_upt_umol_L, plankton_N_upt_umol_L) %>%
  left_join(uc_meta %>% select(Unique_code, ofr_l_hr, species), by = "Unique_code") %>%
  mutate(
    plankton_C_upt_umol_hr = plankton_C_upt_umol_L * ofr_l_hr,
    plankton_N_upt_umol_hr = plankton_N_upt_umol_L * ofr_l_hr
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Combine per Unique_code (now safe: 1 row per Unique_code everywhere)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

uc_all <- uc_meta %>%
  left_join(
    fcm_uptake_by_uc %>%
      select(Unique_code, plankton_C_upt_umol_hr, plankton_N_upt_umol_hr),
    by = "Unique_code"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    plankton_C_upt_umol_hr = coalesce(plankton_C_upt_umol_hr, 0),
    plankton_N_upt_umol_hr = coalesce(plankton_N_upt_umol_hr, 0)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Species means
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

species_sum <- uc_all %>%
  group_by(species) %>%
  summarise(
    n_unique = n_distinct(Unique_code),

    plankton_C_upt = mean(plankton_C_upt_umol_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    plankton_N_upt = mean(plankton_N_upt_umol_hr, na.rm = TRUE),

    O2_uptake = mean(O2_uptake_umol_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    DIC_rel   = mean(DIC_release_umol_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    NOx_rel   = mean(NOx_release_umol_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    TN_rel    = mean(TN_release_umol_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(species), n_unique >= min_n_unique)

print(species_sum)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Plot Sankey for ONE species (set sp manually)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

pos <- function(x) ifelse(is.na(x) | x < 0, 0, x)

sp <- species_sum$species[1]  # change to "Agelas oroides" etc.
sp_row <- species_sum %>% filter(species == sp)

links_sp <- bind_rows(
  tibble(from="Seawater", to="Plankton C uptake", value=pos(sp_row$plankton_C_upt), group="C_in"),
  tibble(from="Seawater", to="Plankton N uptake", value=pos(sp_row$plankton_N_upt), group="N_in"),
  tibble(from="Seawater", to="O2 uptake",         value=pos(sp_row$O2_uptake),      group="O_in"),

  tibble(from="Plankton C uptake", to="Holobiont", value=pos(sp_row$plankton_C_upt), group="C_in"),
  tibble(from="Plankton N uptake", to="Holobiont", value=pos(sp_row$plankton_N_upt), group="N_in"),
  tibble(from="O2 uptake",         to="Holobiont", value=pos(sp_row$O2_uptake),      group="O_in"),

  tibble(from="Holobiont", to="DIC (≈ CO2) out",   value=pos(sp_row$DIC_rel), group="C_out"),
  tibble(from="Holobiont", to="NOx out",           value=pos(sp_row$NOx_rel), group="N_out"),
  tibble(from="Holobiont", to="TN out (proxy)",    value=pos(sp_row$TN_rel),  group="N_out")
)

make_sankey(links_sp)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) QC: show NEGATIVE means (important for DON/TN weirdness)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

qc_signs <- species_sum %>%
  transmute(
    species, n_unique,
    plankton_C_upt, plankton_N_upt,
    O2_uptake, DIC_rel, NOx_rel, TN_rel,
    any_negative = (plankton_C_upt < 0) | (plankton_N_upt < 0) | (O2_uptake < 0) |
                   (DIC_rel < 0) | (NOx_rel < 0) | (TN_rel < 0)
  )

print(qc_signs)


```

## Teres
### Teres_data — Load + reshape to long format (Param × In/Ex/Diff)
```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
################################################################################
# Teres_data — Load + reshape to long format (Param × In/Ex/Diff)
#
# GOAL
#   1) Load the sheet "Teres_data" from the Excel file
#   2) Standardize column names (lowercase, spaces -> underscores)
#   3) Identify all columns that represent measured parameters in the format:
#        <param>-in / <param>-ex / <param>-diff
#      (and also allow underscore: <param>_in etc.)
#   4) Convert the wide table (many columns) into a tidy long table:
#        one row per sample × parameter, with columns In / Ex / Diff
#
# IMPORTANT NOTE
#   - In this dataset, some columns (e.g., pico-in / pico-ex) are character codes
#     and cannot be pivoted together with numeric concentration columns.
#   - Therefore, we pivot only numeric parameter columns.
################################################################################

# ==============================================================================
# 0) Libraries
# ==============================================================================
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)

# ==============================================================================
# 1) Paths + safety checks
# ==============================================================================
path_teres  <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/Energy_budget/Uptake final data set.xlsx"
sheet_teres <- "Teres_data"
stopifnot(file.exists(path_teres))

# ==============================================================================
# 2) Load Teres_data and normalize column names
#   - replace spaces with "_"
#   - convert to lowercase
# ==============================================================================
teres_raw <- read_excel(path_teres, sheet = sheet_teres)

teres <- teres_raw %>%
  rename_with(~ str_replace_all(.x, "\\s+", "_")) %>%
  rename_with(tolower)

# ==============================================================================
# 3) Identify parameter columns to reshape
#   We want parameters like:
#     doc, nh4, don, pon, poc, sio4, noxtt, po4, pico, pro, syn, euk, bact
#   And for each parameter we look for suffixes:
#     -in / -ex / -diff  (or _in / _ex / _diff)
# ==============================================================================
params_keep <- c("doc","nh4","don","pon","poc","sio4","noxtt","po4","pico","pro","syn","euk","bact")

# All candidate columns matching "<param>-in/ex/diff" or "<param>_in/ex/diff"
val_cols <- names(teres) %>%
  keep(~ str_detect(.x, paste0("^(", paste(params_keep, collapse="|"), ")(-|_)(in|ex|diff)$")))

# Keep only numeric columns (avoid pico codes etc.)
val_cols_num <- val_cols[sapply(teres[val_cols], is.numeric)]

# Optional debug: see which columns were excluded (usually pico-in/pico-ex codes)
setdiff(val_cols, val_cols_num)

# ==============================================================================
# 4) Pivot to long format: Param + Type (In/Ex/Diff) + Value
#   Result: one row per original row × parameter × type
# ==============================================================================
teres_long <- teres %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(val_cols_num),
    names_to = c("Param", "Type"),
    names_pattern = "^(.+?)[-_](in|ex|diff)$",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Normalize type labels
    Type = case_when(
      Type == "in"   ~ "In",
      Type == "ex"   ~ "Ex",
      Type == "diff" ~ "Diff",
      TRUE ~ Type
    ),
    # Ensure Param is clean lowercase
    Param = tolower(Param)
  )

# ==============================================================================
# 5) Pivot back to a tidy wide: In / Ex / Diff as separate columns
#   Result: one row per sample × Param with columns In, Ex, Diff
# ==============================================================================
teres_long_wide <- teres_long %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Type, values_from = Value) %>%
  arrange(sn, Param)

# ==============================================================================
# 6) Optional: if Diff is missing but In+Ex exist, compute Diff = In - Ex
#   (useful when some params have only In & Ex columns but no explicit diff column)
# ==============================================================================
teres_long_wide <- teres_long_wide %>%
  mutate(
    Diff = ifelse(is.na(Diff) & !is.na(In) & !is.na(Ex), In - Ex, Diff)
  )

# Output object
teres_long_wide

```

### Seasonal sampling (2019) — Build ONE combined table from ALL species sheets
```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
################################################################################
# Seasonal sampling (2019) — Build ONE combined table from ALL species sheets
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Goal:
#   1) Read an Excel file where EACH SHEET is a different species
#   2) Keep only the selected columns (cols_keep)
#   3) Add a new column "Species" = sheet name
#   4) Bind (stack) all sheets into ONE tidy table: seasonal_all_species
#
# Why this custom reading?
#   - Some sheets contain a note line above the real headers
#   - Column types may differ between sheets (numbers stored as text, etc.)
#   - We therefore:
#       * read without headers (col_names = FALSE)
#       * detect the header row (the row that contains "Nº SAMPLING")
#       * set that row as column names
#       * read everything as text, then convert numeric columns robustly
################################################################################

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0) Libraries
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)   # for parse_number()

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) File path
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
file_path <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/Energy_budget/Seasonal sampling final data set_2019.xlsx"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) List all sheets (each sheet = one species)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
sheets <- excel_sheets(file_path)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Columns we want to keep from each sheet
#    (We will select these by name AFTER we set real column headers)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cols_keep <- c(
  "Nº SAMPLING",
  "DATE",
  "Temperature Categories (ºC)",
  "Field Temperatures (ºC)",
  "VIDEO",
  "NºIND.",
  "NºIND Resp",
  "Osculum Area (cm2)",
  "Osculum Area (mm2)",
  "OSCULUM JET SPEED (cm/s)",
  "OSCULUM FLOW RATE (ml/min)",
  "AREA (cm2)"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Helper function: robust numeric parsing
#    - Works even if cells contain "3*", spaces, commas, etc.
#    - Returns numeric (NA if nothing parsable)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
parse_num <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  x <- str_replace_all(x, ",", ".")  # if someone used comma as decimal separator
  readr::parse_number(x, locale = readr::locale(decimal_mark = "."))
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Main function: read ONE species sheet into a clean tibble
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
read_species_sheet <- function(sheet_name) {

  # (A) Read the whole sheet as raw text with NO headers
  #     -> prevents readxl from guessing wrong column names/types
  raw <- read_excel(
    path      = file_path,
    sheet     = sheet_name,
    col_names = FALSE,
    col_types = "text"
  )

  # (B) Detect which row is the header row:
  #     Find the first row that contains "Nº SAMPLING"
  header_row <- which(
    apply(raw, 1, function(r) {
      any(str_detect(str_to_lower(r), "nº sampling|no sampling|n sampling"))
    })
  )[1]

  # If we can't find a header row, return empty tibble but keep pipeline running
  if (is.na(header_row)) {
    warning(paste("No header row found in sheet:", sheet_name))
    return(tibble())
  }

  # (C) Extract the header row and clean it into column names
  new_names <- raw[header_row, ] |>
    unlist(use.names = FALSE) |>
    as.character() |>
    str_squish()

  # Handle blank/NA header cells + duplicated names
  blanks <- (new_names == "" | is.na(new_names))
  if (any(blanks)) {
    new_names[blanks] <- paste0("X", seq_len(sum(blanks)))
  }
  new_names <- make.unique(new_names)

  # (D) Data starts AFTER the header row
  df <- raw[(header_row + 1):nrow(raw), , drop = FALSE]
  names(df) <- new_names

  # (E) Keep only the required columns, and add Species = sheet name
  df_out <- df %>%
    mutate(Species = sheet_name) %>%
    relocate(Species, .before = 1) %>%
    select(Species, any_of(cols_keep))

  # (F) Convert numeric columns (after safe selection)
  df_out %>%
    mutate(
      `NºIND.`                     = parse_num(`NºIND.`),
      `NºIND Resp`                 = parse_num(`NºIND Resp`),
      `Osculum Area (cm2)`         = parse_num(`Osculum Area (cm2)`),
      `Osculum Area (mm2)`         = parse_num(`Osculum Area (mm2)`),
      `OSCULUM JET SPEED (cm/s)`   = parse_num(`OSCULUM JET SPEED (cm/s)`),
      `OSCULUM FLOW RATE (ml/min)` = parse_num(`OSCULUM FLOW RATE (ml/min)`),
      `AREA (cm2)`                 = parse_num(`AREA (cm2)`)
    )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) Read ALL sheets and bind into one table
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
seasonal_all_species <- map_dfr(sheets, read_species_sheet)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7) Quick check
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
glimpse(seasonal_all_species)

```


### Osculum Flow Rate (OFR) — Summary Statistics per Species × Date × Temperature
```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
################################################################################
# Osculum Flow Rate (OFR) — Summary Statistics per Species × Date × Temperature
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Goal:
#   1) Clean and standardize variables (Temperature, DATE)
#   2) Rename OSCULUM FLOW RATE (ml/min) → OFR_ml_min
#   3) Calculate full descriptive statistics per:
#         Species × DATE × Temperature
#
# Statistics calculated:
#   n        = number of observations
#   mean     = arithmetic mean
#   median   = median
#   SD       = standard deviation
#   SE       = standard error
#   CI95     = 95% confidence interval (half-width)
#   MIN/MAX  = range
#   CV (%)   = coefficient of variation
################################################################################

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0) Load required libraries
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Clean variables and compute summary statistics
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
flow_summary_full <- seasonal_all_species %>%
  
  # (A) Clean and standardize variables
  mutate(
    # Convert temperature column to numeric
    Temperature = as.numeric(`Temperature Categories (ºC)`),
    
    # Convert DATE to proper Date format
    # Original format example: "13-july-12"
    DATE = dmy(str_replace_all(str_to_lower(DATE), "-", " ")),
    
    # Rename for clarity and easier usage
    OFR_ml_min = `OSCULUM FLOW RATE (ml/min)`
  ) %>%
  
  # (B) Group by biological and environmental structure
  group_by(Species, DATE, Temperature) %>%
  
  # (C) Calculate descriptive statistics
  summarise(
    n      = sum(!is.na(OFR_ml_min)),
    mean   = mean(OFR_ml_min, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(OFR_ml_min, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD     = sd(OFR_ml_min, na.rm = TRUE),
    SE     = SD / sqrt(pmax(n, 1)),
    
    # 95% confidence interval (t-distribution)
    CI95   = qt(0.975, df = pmax(n - 1, 1)) * SE,
    
    MIN    = min(OFR_ml_min, na.rm = TRUE),
    MAX    = max(OFR_ml_min, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Coefficient of variation (%)
    CV     = (SD / mean) * 100,
    
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  
  # Sort output
  arrange(Species, DATE, Temperature)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Inspect summary table
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
flow_summary_full

################################################################################
# Boxplot of Mean OFR per Species
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# This plot shows the distribution of daily/temperature means
# (NOT raw measurements).
#
# Each point = one Species × Date × Temperature mean value
################################################################################

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(flow_summary_full,
       aes(x = Species, y = mean)) +
  
  # Boxplot of mean values
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  
  # Overlay individual mean values
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  
  labs(
    x = "Species",
    y = "Mean OFR (ml/min)",
    title = "Distribution of Mean Osculum Flow Rate per Species"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)



```



### Fill pump_rate in teres_long_wide using Species × Year matching
```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}

################################################################################
# Fill pump_rate in teres_long_wide using Species × Year matching
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rationale:
#   - Species names are identical between datasets
#   - Exact sampling dates barely overlap
#   - Therefore, we assign pump_rate using the mean OFR per Species × Year
#
# What this chunk does:
#   1) Build yearly mean OFR per Species from flow_summary_full
#   2) Extract Year from Teresa date column
#   3) Join both tables by Species × Year
#   4) Fill pump_rate with the yearly mean OFR
#   5) Clean temporary helper columns
################################################################################

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0) Load required libraries
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Create OFR lookup table per Species × Year
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# flow_summary_full contains mean OFR per Species × DATE × Temperature
# Here we collapse it to one mean per Species × Year

ofr_by_species_year <- flow_summary_full %>%
  mutate(
    species_key = str_squish(str_to_lower(Species)),  # standardize species names
    year        = year(DATE)                          # extract year from Date
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(year)) %>%                            # remove rows with missing dates
  group_by(species_key, year) %>%
  summarise(
    pump_rate_year = mean(mean, na.rm = TRUE),        # yearly mean OFR (ml/min)
    n_year = n(),                                     # number of means contributing
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Optional: inspect
print(ofr_by_species_year, n = 50)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Extract Year from Teresa date column
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Teresa dates are stored as character (e.g., "4-July-12")
# Convert to Date and extract year

teres_long_wide <- teres_long_wide %>%
  mutate(
    species_key = str_squish(str_to_lower(species)),
    date_parsed = dmy(str_replace_all(str_to_lower(date), "-", " ")),
    year        = year(date_parsed)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Join by Species × Year and fill pump_rate
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
teres_long_wide <- teres_long_wide %>%
  left_join(ofr_by_species_year,
            by = c("species_key", "year")) %>%
  mutate(
    pump_rate = pump_rate_year   # assign yearly OFR mean
  ) %>%
  select(-species_key, -date_parsed, -pump_rate_year)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Quality check
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
teres_long_wide %>%
  summarise(
    n_total   = n(),
    n_filled  = sum(!is.na(pump_rate)),
    n_missing = sum(is.na(pump_rate))
  ) %>%
  print()

# Optional: inspect sample rows
teres_long_wide %>%
  select(species, date, year, pump_rate) %>%
  arrange(species, year, date) %>%
  head(20)



################################################################################
# QC: Did every row get a pump_rate value?
################################################################################

library(dplyr)

# 1) כמה NA יש ב-pump_rate?
teres_long_wide %>%
  summarise(
    n_total = n(),
    n_na_pump_rate = sum(is.na(pump_rate)),
    n_non_na_pump_rate = sum(!is.na(pump_rate))
  ) %>%
  print()

# 2) אם יש NA — תראי בדיוק אילו שורות חסרות
teres_long_wide %>%
  filter(is.na(pump_rate)) %>%
  select(species, date, year, period, team, temp) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  arrange(species, year, date) %>%
  print(n = 50)

# 3) sanity check: לכל Species×Year צריך להיות pump_rate יחיד (כי זה ממוצע שנתי)
teres_long_wide %>%
  group_by(species, year) %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    n_unique_pump_rate = n_distinct(pump_rate),
    pump_rate_values = paste(sort(unique(pump_rate)), collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(species, year) %>%
  print(n = 50)

```




### Teres → Convert plankton counts to Carbon/Nitrogen (µmol/L) using Sheet2 factors
```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
################################################################################
# Teres → Convert plankton counts to Carbon/Nitrogen (µmol/L) using Sheet2 factors
# FIXED VERSION (no crossing Element, no many-to-many join explosion)
################################################################################

library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)

# ----------------------------
# Settings + safety
# ----------------------------
conv_path <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/FCS files/Conversion factors from literature.xlsx"
stopifnot(file.exists(conv_path))
stopifnot(exists("teres_long_wide"))

counts_unit <- "cells_mL"             # "cells_mL" or "cells_uL"
taxa_keep   <- c("Pro","Syn","Euk","Bact")

sheet2_unit <- "fg"                   # "fg" or "pg"
to_fg <- function(x) if (sheet2_unit == "pg") x * 1000 else x

atomic_weight <- function(element) {
  element <- tolower(as.character(element))
  ifelse(grepl("carbon|^c$", element), 12,
         ifelse(grepl("nitrogen|^n$", element), 14, NA_real_))
}

fgcell_to_umolL <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      fg_per_mL = fg_per_cell * cells_mL,
      umol_L    = (fg_per_mL * 1e-6) / atomic_weight(Element)
    )
}

# ==============================================================================
# 2) Extract IN/EX plankton counts from teres_long_wide  (long: meta × Taxon × InEx)
# ==============================================================================
id_cols_teres <- setdiff(names(teres_long_wide), c("Param","In","Ex","Diff"))

teres_counts <- teres_long_wide %>%
  mutate(Param = str_to_lower(str_squish(as.character(Param)))) %>%
  filter(Param %in% str_to_lower(taxa_keep)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Standardize Teres taxa to Sheet2-like names
    Taxon = case_when(
      Param == "pro"  ~ "Pro",
      Param == "syn"  ~ "Syn",
      Param == "euk"  ~ "Euk",
      Param == "bact" ~ "Bact",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    In = as.numeric(In),
    Ex = as.numeric(Ex)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Taxon)) %>%
  select(all_of(id_cols_teres), Taxon, In, Ex) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(In, Ex),
    names_to = "InEx",
    values_to = "cells_value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    InEx     = toupper(InEx),
    cells_mL = if (counts_unit == "cells_uL") cells_value * 1000 else cells_value
  ) %>%
  select(-cells_value)

# ==============================================================================
# 3) Read Sheet2 conversion factors (robust)
# ==============================================================================
sheet2_raw <- read_excel(conv_path, sheet = 2) %>%
  rename_with(~ str_squish(.x))

detect_col <- function(nms, pattern, label) {
  hit <- nms[str_detect(nms, regex(pattern, ignore_case = TRUE))]
  if (length(hit) == 0) {
    stop("Could not detect ", label, " column. Pattern: ", pattern,
         "\nAvailable columns: ", paste(nms, collapse = " | "))
  }
  hit[1]
}

nms2 <- names(sheet2_raw)
taxon_col_s2   <- detect_col(nms2, "^taxon$|^taxa$|group", "Sheet2 taxon")
element_col_s2 <- detect_col(nms2, "^element$|elements|cn", "Sheet2 element")
oceanic_col    <- detect_col(nms2, "oceanic|offshore|open", "Sheet2 oceanic")
coastal_col    <- detect_col(nms2, "coastal|inshore|near",  "Sheet2 coastal")

sheet2 <- sheet2_raw %>%
  transmute(
    Taxon   = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(.data[[taxon_col_s2]]))),
    Element = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(.data[[element_col_s2]]))),
    Oceanic_fg_per_cell = to_fg(as.numeric(parse_number(as.character(.data[[oceanic_col]])))),
    Coastal_fg_per_cell = to_fg(as.numeric(parse_number(as.character(.data[[coastal_col]]))))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Taxon), Taxon != "", !is.na(Element), Element != "") %>%
  mutate(
    Element = case_when(
      str_detect(str_to_lower(Element), "carbon|^c$")   ~ "Carbon",
      str_detect(str_to_lower(Element), "nitrogen|^n$") ~ "Nitrogen",
      TRUE ~ Element
    ),
    # OPTIONAL (helps when Sheet2 calls euk differently):
    Taxon = case_when(
      Taxon %in% c("Peuk","Neuk","Picoeuk","Nanoeuk","Eukaryotes","Eukaryote") ~ "Euk",
      TRUE ~ Taxon
    )
  )

# QC: what taxa are actually available in Sheet2?
missing_taxa <- setdiff(taxa_keep, unique(sheet2$Taxon))
if (length(missing_taxa) > 0) {
  message("WARNING: These taxa are missing from Sheet2 and will be dropped: ",
          paste(missing_taxa, collapse = ", "))
}

################################################################################
# Teres → Convert plankton counts to C/N (µmol/L) using Sheet2 (FIXED, FINAL)
# Fixes:
#   - eliminates many-to-many warning (Scenario×Taxon unique)
#   - prevents list-cols in pivot_wider by collapsing duplicates
#   - guarantees IN/EX are numeric before Diff calculation
################################################################################

# ==============================================================================
# 4) Build factors table (Scenario × Taxon × Element → fg_per_cell)
#    + build a unique Scenario×Taxon table for safe replication
# ==============================================================================
sheet2_factors <- sheet2 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Oceanic_fg_per_cell, Coastal_fg_per_cell),
    names_to  = "Scenario",
    values_to = "fg_per_cell"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Scenario = recode(
      Scenario,
      "Oceanic_fg_per_cell" = "S1_fixed_Oceanic",
      "Coastal_fg_per_cell" = "S2_fixed_Coastal"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(fg_per_cell)) %>%
  select(Scenario, Taxon, Element, fg_per_cell) %>%
  distinct()

# This table is UNIQUE per Scenario×Taxon (no Element here!)
sheet2_scen_taxon <- sheet2_factors %>%
  distinct(Scenario, Taxon)

# ==============================================================================
# 5) Replicate counts across scenarios (NO warning now) + attach element factors
# ==============================================================================
# replicate each Teres count row across all scenarios available for that taxon
teres_counts_scen <- teres_counts %>%
  inner_join(sheet2_scen_taxon, by = "Taxon")

# attach fg_per_cell per Scenario×Taxon×Element
teres_scenarios_raw <- teres_counts_scen %>%
  inner_join(sheet2_factors, by = c("Scenario", "Taxon")) %>%
  fgcell_to_umolL() %>%
  select(all_of(id_cols_teres), Scenario, Element, Taxon, InEx, cells_mL, fg_per_cell, umol_L)

# ==============================================================================
# 5.1) CRITICAL: Collapse duplicates BEFORE pivot_wider
#     Why duplicates exist?
#       - Sometimes Teres has repeated rows with identical metadata
#       - Or you joined pump_rate by year and created duplicates in meta keys
#     Strategy:
#       - For each unique sample-meta × Scenario × Element × Taxon × InEx,
#         take median(umol_L) (robust) and track n_rep
# ==============================================================================
key_cols <- c(id_cols_teres, "Scenario", "Element", "Taxon", "InEx")

teres_scenarios <- teres_scenarios_raw %>%
  group_by(across(all_of(key_cols))) %>%
  summarise(
    umol_L = median(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_rep  = sum(!is.na(umol_L)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ==============================================================================
# 6) Compute Δ(IN−EX) in µmol/L (wide)
# ==============================================================================
teres_cn_wide <- teres_scenarios %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = InEx,
    values_from = umol_L,
    values_fn   = median,      # extra safety (shouldn't be needed after collapse)
    values_fill = NA_real_
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    IN = as.numeric(IN),
    EX = as.numeric(EX),
    Diff_umol_L = ifelse(!is.na(IN) & !is.na(EX), IN - EX, NA_real_)
  )

# Quick QC
print(
  teres_cn_wide %>%
    summarise(
      n_rows = n(),
      n_complete_pairs = sum(!is.na(IN) & !is.na(EX)),
      n_missing_pairs  = sum(is.na(IN) | is.na(EX))
    )
)

# Outputs
teres_scenarios
teres_cn_wide
################################################################################
# End
################################################################################

```

### Teres — Scenario summaries + plots (same structure as FCM)

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
################################################################################
# Teres — Scenario summaries + plots (same structure as FCM)
#
# Requires:
#   - teres_scenarios (from previous chunk)
# Outputs:
#   - teres_summary_by_taxon, teres_summary_total
#   - p_teres_scenarios_taxa, p_teres_total
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

stopifnot(exists("teres_scenarios"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Summary by taxon (Scenario × Element × Taxon)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
teres_summary_by_taxon <- teres_scenarios %>%
  group_by(Scenario, Element, Taxon) %>%
  summarise(
    n            = sum(!is.na(umol_L)),
    mean_umol_L   = mean(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_umol_L = median(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_umol_L     = sd(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Total per sample/event (uses available ID columns)
#    NOTE: keep InEx separate by default (In vs Ex). You can filter if needed.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
id_cols_teres2 <- intersect(
  names(teres_scenarios),
  c("sn","date","plaque","species","season","period","team","temp")
)

teres_summary_total <- teres_scenarios %>%
  group_by(across(all_of(id_cols_teres2)), Scenario, Element, InEx) %>%
  summarise(
    Total_umol_L = sum(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# If you want totals only for inlet water:
# teres_summary_total <- teres_summary_total %>% filter(InEx == "In")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Plot A: Mean by Taxon per Scenario/Element (with ~95% CI)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
group_cols_teres <- c(
  "Bact" = "#F4A6A6",
  "Pro"  = "#9DBCE3",
  "Syn"  = "#F6C28B",
  "Euk"  = "#A8D5BA"
)

teres_summary_by_taxon_plot <- teres_scenarios %>%
  group_by(Scenario, Element, Taxon) %>%
  summarise(
    n    = sum(!is.na(umol_L)),
    mean = mean(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    conf_int = sd / sqrt(pmax(n, 1)) * qt(0.975, df = pmax(n - 1, 1))
  )

p_teres_scenarios_taxa <- ggplot(
  teres_summary_by_taxon_plot,
  aes(x = Taxon, y = mean, fill = Taxon)
) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - conf_int, ymax = mean + conf_int), width = 0.25) +
  facet_grid(Element ~ Scenario, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols_teres, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(y = expression(paste("Content (", mu, "mol L"^{-1}, ")")), x = NULL) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

p_teres_scenarios_taxa

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Plot B: Total (mean) per Scenario (facet Element) — like your p_total
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
teres_total_plot <- teres_summary_total %>%
  group_by(Scenario, Element) %>%
  summarise(
    n    = sum(!is.na(Total_umol_L)),
    mean = mean(Total_umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(Total_umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    conf_int = sd / sqrt(pmax(n, 1)) * qt(0.975, df = pmax(n - 1, 1))
  )

p_teres_total <- ggplot(teres_total_plot, aes(x = Scenario, y = mean)) +
  geom_col(fill = "grey40") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - conf_int, ymax = mean + conf_int), width = 0.25) +
  facet_wrap(~Element, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    y = expression(paste("Total content (", mu, "mol L"^{-1}, ")")),
    x = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )

p_teres_total

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Print summary tables (optional)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
print(teres_summary_by_taxon)
print(teres_summary_total)

################################################################################
# Teres — Stacked Δ(IN−EX) (µmol/L) by plaque
#
# GOAL
#   Show contribution of each plankton taxon (Bact/Syn/Pro/Euk)
#   to Δ(IN−EX) in µmol/L
#   grouped by plaque (individual sponge unit)
#
# INPUT
#   - teres_cn_wide  (In, Ex, Diff_umol_L already calculated)
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

stopifnot(exists("teres_cn_wide"))

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Prepare stacked data (sum per plaque × Taxon × Element)
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
stack_df_teres <- teres_cn_wide %>%
  group_by(plaque, Taxon, Element) %>%
  summarise(
    Delta_sum = sum(Diff_umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Optional: order plaques numerically if possible
stack_df_teres <- stack_df_teres %>%
  mutate(plaque = factor(plaque, levels = unique(plaque)))

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Color palette (same logic as your FCM plot)
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
group_cols_teres <- c(
  "Bact" = "#F4A6A6",
  "Pro"  = "#9DBCE3",
  "Syn"  = "#F6C28B",
  "Euk"  = "#A8D5BA"
)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Plot
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_stack_teres <- ggplot(stack_df_teres,
                        aes(x = plaque, y = Delta_sum, fill = Taxon)) +
  geom_col(color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~Element, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_cols_teres, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    x = "Plaque",
    y = expression(Delta*"IN - EX ("*mu*"mol L"^{-1}*")"),
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

p_stack_teres



```


### Teres — Delta vs IN (Ambient) — HMA/LMA split + BH(FDR)
```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=10}


################################################################################
# Teres — Delta vs IN (Ambient) — HMA/LMA split + BH(FDR)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PURPOSE
#   Plot Δ(IN−EX) vs Ambient concentration (IN) for multiple solutes, faceted.
#
# FIXES IN THIS VERSION
#   ✅ Fixes the .data / lapply() crash by using select(any_of(meta_keep))
#   ✅ No dependency on teres_base (uses teres_long_wide only)
#   ✅ Delta uses Diff when numeric; otherwise fallback to (In - Ex)
#   ✅ Robust Param matching (NOx accepts noxt/noxtt/etc.)
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(broom)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
library(forcats)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0) Settings + safety checks
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
stopifnot(exists("teres_long_wide"))

out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/Energy_budget/by_species"
out_dir <- normalizePath(out_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

fig_prefix <- "Teres_Delta_vs_IN_HMA_LMA_sigOnly_BH"
alpha <- 0.05

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Base table + HMA/LMA assignment
#    Priority:
#      (A) use existing HMA/LMA column if present
#      (B) else join by species using fallback mapping table
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
hma_col <- intersect(
  names(teres_long_wide),
  c("hma_lma", "hma-lma", "HMA_LMA", "hmaLma")
)

df0 <- teres_long_wide %>%
  mutate(
    Param   = str_to_lower(str_squish(as.character(Param))),
    species = if ("species" %in% names(.)) as.character(species) else NA_character_
  )

if (length(hma_col) > 0) {
  hma_col_use <- hma_col[1]
  df0 <- df0 %>%
    mutate(
      HMA_LMA = toupper(str_trim(as.character(.data[[hma_col_use]]))),
      HMA_LMA = case_when(
        HMA_LMA %in% c("HMA","LMA") ~ HMA_LMA,
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    )
} else {
  hma_map <- tribble(
    ~species,      ~HMA_LMA,
    "Agelas",      "HMA",
    "Chondrosia",  "HMA",
    "Dysidea",     "LMA"
  )
  df0 <- df0 %>%
    left_join(hma_map, by = "species")
}

df0 <- df0 %>%
  mutate(HMA_LMA = factor(HMA_LMA, levels = c("LMA","HMA")))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Solute mapping (Param -> Solute) using regex
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
same_map <- tibble(
  Solute = c("DOC","DON","NH4","NOx","PO4","SiO4"),
  Param_regex = c(
    "^doc$",
    "^don$",
    "^nh4$",
    "^(noxt|noxtt|nox|no3no2)$",
    "^po4$",
    "^(sio4|si)$"
  )
)

# Metadata columns to keep IF THEY EXIST
meta_keep <- intersect(
  names(df0),
  c("sn","date","plaque","species","temp","team","season","period","year","pump_rate","n_year")
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Build plotting table safely (NO .data inside lapply)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_plot <- purrr::pmap_dfr(same_map, function(Solute, Param_regex) {

  df0 %>%
    filter(str_detect(Param, regex(Param_regex, ignore_case = TRUE))) %>%
    # keep metadata safely
    select(any_of(meta_keep), HMA_LMA, In, Ex, Diff) %>%
    transmute(
      across(any_of(meta_keep), ~ .x),

      HMA_LMA = HMA_LMA,
      Solute  = Solute,

      IN_umol_L = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(In)),

      Delta_umol_L = {
        d    <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(Diff))
        in_v <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(In))
        ex_v <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(Ex))
        ifelse(!is.na(d), d, ifelse(!is.na(in_v) & !is.na(ex_v), in_v - ex_v, NA_real_))
      }
    )
}) %>%
  filter(!is.na(IN_umol_L), !is.na(Delta_umol_L), !is.na(HMA_LMA)) %>%
  mutate(Solute = factor(Solute, levels = same_map$Solute))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Facet labels (math)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
facet_labels <- c(
  DOC  = expression(Delta*DOC~"(IN-EX)"),
  DON  = expression(Delta*DON~"(IN-EX)"),
  NH4  = expression(Delta*NH[4]~"(IN-EX)"),
  NOx  = expression(Delta*NO[x]~"(IN-EX)"),
  PO4  = expression(Delta*PO[4]~"(IN-EX)"),
  SiO4 = expression(Delta*SiO[4]~"(IN-EX)")
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Fit lm per Solute × HMA/LMA, BH(FDR) across ALL slopes
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_models <- df_plot %>%
  group_by(Solute, HMA_LMA) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    n = map_int(data, nrow),
    fit = map(data, ~ if (nrow(.x) >= 3) lm(Delta_umol_L ~ IN_umol_L, data = .x) else NULL),
    p_slope = map_dbl(fit, ~ {
      if (is.null(.x)) return(NA_real_)
      broom::tidy(.x) %>% filter(term == "IN_umol_L") %>% pull(p.value) %>% .[1]
    })
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    p_adj = p.adjust(p_slope, method = "BH"),
    sig   = !is.na(p_adj) & p_adj < alpha
  )

stats_tbl <- df_models %>%
  transmute(
    Solute, HMA_LMA, n,
    slope = map_dbl(fit, ~ if (is.null(.x)) NA_real_ else unname(coef(.x)[["IN_umol_L"]])),
    intercept = map_dbl(fit, ~ if (is.null(.x)) NA_real_ else unname(coef(.x)[["(Intercept)"]])),
    r2 = map_dbl(fit, ~ if (is.null(.x)) NA_real_ else summary(.x)$r.squared),
    p_slope, p_adj, sig
  ) %>%
  arrange(Solute, HMA_LMA)

print(stats_tbl)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) Prediction lines ONLY for significant groups
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
pred_df <- df_models %>%
  filter(sig, !is.null(fit)) %>%
  mutate(
    x_min = map_dbl(data, ~ min(.x$IN_umol_L, na.rm = TRUE)),
    x_max = map_dbl(data, ~ max(.x$IN_umol_L, na.rm = TRUE)),
    grid  = map2(x_min, x_max, ~ seq(.x, .y, length.out = 80)),
    yhat  = map2(fit, grid, ~ predict(.x, newdata = data.frame(IN_umol_L = .y))),
    pred  = map2(grid, yhat, ~ tibble(IN_umol_L = .x, Delta_umol_L = .y))
  ) %>%
  select(Solute, HMA_LMA, pred) %>%
  unnest(pred)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7) Equation labels ONLY for significant lines
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
eq_df <- df_models %>%
  filter(sig, !is.null(fit)) %>%
  mutate(
    cf = map(fit, coef),
    m  = map_dbl(cf, ~ .x[["IN_umol_L"]]),
    b  = map_dbl(cf, ~ .x[["(Intercept)"]]),
    r2 = map_dbl(fit, ~ summary(.x)$r.squared),
    label = paste0(
      "y = ", signif(m, 3), "x + ", signif(b, 3),
      "\nR² = ", signif(r2, 3),
      "\nBH p = ", signif(p_adj, 3)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(Solute, HMA_LMA, label)

pos_df <- df_plot %>%
  group_by(Solute) %>%
  summarise(
    x_min = min(IN_umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    x_max = max(IN_umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    y_min = min(Delta_umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    y_max = max(Delta_umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    x      = x_min + 0.03 * (x_max - x_min),
    y_top  = y_max - 0.03 * (y_max - y_min),
    y_step = 0.18 * (y_max - y_min)
  )

eq_df2 <- eq_df %>%
  left_join(pos_df, by = "Solute") %>%
  arrange(Solute, HMA_LMA) %>%
  group_by(Solute) %>%
  mutate(
    idx = row_number(),
    y   = y_top - (idx - 1) * y_step
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 8) Plot
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
pal_hma <- c("LMA" = "#8da0cb", "HMA" = "#fc8d62")

p_delta_in_teres <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = IN_umol_L, y = Delta_umol_L, color = HMA_LMA)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.70, size = 2) +
  geom_line(
    data = pred_df,
    aes(x = IN_umol_L, y = Delta_umol_L, color = HMA_LMA),
    linewidth = 1.0,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = eq_df2,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = HMA_LMA),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = 0, vjust = 1,
    size = 3.4
  ) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~Solute, scales = "free",
    labeller = as_labeller(facet_labels, default = label_parsed)
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal_hma, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Teres: Δ(IN−EX) vs Ambient concentration (IN)",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Lines + equations shown ONLY when slope is significant after BH(FDR) (alpha=", alpha, "). ",
      "Model: lm(Δ ~ IN) per Solute×HMA/LMA; BH correction across all slopes."
    ),
    x = expression(IN~concentration~(mu*mol~L^-1)),
    y = expression(Delta~(IN-EX)~(mu*mol~L^-1)),
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11)
  )

p_delta_in_teres

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9) Save
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, paste0(fig_prefix, ".png")),
  plot = p_delta_in_teres,
  width = 34, height = 22, units = "cm", dpi = 300
)

write.csv(
  stats_tbl,
  file.path(out_dir, paste0(fig_prefix, "_stats_table.csv")),
  row.names = FALSE
)

################################################################################
# End
################################################################################

```


### Teres — Energy budget: stacked "Total Energy" (plankton C/N + DOC/DON) + Overlay OUR ΔO2 reference (mean + 95% CI) by:
```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=10}
################################################################################
# Teres — Energy budget: stacked "Total Energy" (plankton C/N + DOC/DON)
# + Overlay OUR ΔO2 reference (mean + 95% CI)
#
# FIX in this version:
#   ✅ No scales::lighten() (not exported in some versions)
#   ✅ Uses tint_col() (mix color with white) => stable everywhere
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpattern)
library(readr)
library(scales)      # alpha()
library(ggnewscale)  # new_scale_fill()

stopifnot(exists("teres_cn_wide"))
stopifnot(exists("teres_long_wide"))
stopifnot(exists("sponge_db2_with_cn"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SETTINGS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
scenario_keep   <- "S2_fixed_Coastal"
scale_N_by      <- 3

all_taxa <- c("Bact","Euk","Pro","Syn","DOC","DON")
fill_cols <- c(
  "Bact" = "#F4A6A6",
  "Euk"  = "#A8D5BA",
  "Pro"  = "#9DBCE3",
  "Syn"  = "#F6C28B",
  "DOC"  = "#B9B9B9",
  "DON"  = "#8F8F8F"
)

# OUR reference line colors
ref_cols <- c("HMA" = "#E66101", "LMA" = "#1F78B4")

# CI band color style:
ci_band_tint  <- 0.55   # 0..1 : 0=original ; 1=white
ci_alpha      <- 0.35   # transparency of CI band

band_half_width <- 0.42

legend_text_size  <- 9
legend_title_size <- 10
legend_key_size   <- 0.42

out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/Energy_budget/Teres"
out_dir <- normalizePath(out_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# HELPERS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
to_num <- function(x) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(parse_number(as.character(x))))

make_uid <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      plaque = as.character(plaque),
      sn     = as.character(sn),
      date   = as.character(date),
      Unique_code = paste0(plaque, "_sn", sn, "_", date)
    )
}

# stable "lighten": mix with white (works in any R version)
tint_col <- function(col, tint = 0.55) {
  # tint=0 -> original color ; tint=1 -> white
  rgb_col <- grDevices::col2rgb(col) / 255
  rgb_out <- rgb_col + (1 - rgb_col) * tint
  grDevices::rgb(rgb_out[1], rgb_out[2], rgb_out[3])
}

mean_ci95 <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  n <- length(x)
  if (n < 2) return(tibble(n=n, mean_ref=NA_real_, ci_low=NA_real_, ci_high=NA_real_))
  m <- mean(x)
  s <- sd(x)
  se <- s / sqrt(n)
  tcrit <- qt(0.975, df = n - 1)
  tibble(n=n, mean_ref=m, ci_low=m - tcrit*se, ci_high=m + tcrit*se)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART 1 — TERES energy components (scenario fixed)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
plankton_CN <- teres_cn_wide %>%
  filter(Scenario == scenario_keep) %>%
  make_uid() %>%
  transmute(
    Species      = str_trim(as.character(species)),
    Unique_code  = str_trim(as.character(Unique_code)),
    Taxon        = str_trim(as.character(Taxon)),
    ElementGroup = str_trim(as.character(Element)),
    Delta_umol_L = to_num(Diff_umol_L)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Species), Species != "",
    !is.na(Unique_code), Unique_code != "",
    !is.na(Taxon), Taxon != "",
    !is.na(ElementGroup), ElementGroup != "",
    is.finite(Delta_umol_L)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ElementGroup = case_when(
      str_detect(str_to_lower(ElementGroup), "carbon|^c$")   ~ "Carbon",
      str_detect(str_to_lower(ElementGroup), "nitrogen|^n$") ~ "Nitrogen",
      TRUE ~ ElementGroup
    ),
    Delta_umol_L = if_else(ElementGroup == "Nitrogen", Delta_umol_L * scale_N_by, Delta_umol_L)
  )

teres_long_wide2 <- teres_long_wide %>%
  mutate(
    Param    = str_to_lower(str_squish(as.character(Param))),
    Diff_num = to_num(Diff),
    In_num   = to_num(In),
    Ex_num   = to_num(Ex),
    Diff_use = ifelse(!is.na(Diff_num), Diff_num,
                      ifelse(!is.na(In_num) & !is.na(Ex_num), In_num - Ex_num, NA_real_))
  )

chem_DOC_DON <- teres_long_wide2 %>%
  filter(Param %in% c("doc","don")) %>%
  make_uid() %>%
  transmute(
    Species      = str_trim(as.character(species)),
    Unique_code  = str_trim(as.character(Unique_code)),
    Taxon        = if_else(Param == "doc", "DOC", "DON"),
    ElementGroup = if_else(Param == "doc", "Carbon", "Nitrogen"),
    Delta_umol_L = Diff_use
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Species), Species != "",
    !is.na(Unique_code), Unique_code != "",
    is.finite(Delta_umol_L)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Delta_umol_L = if_else(ElementGroup == "Nitrogen", Delta_umol_L * scale_N_by, Delta_umol_L)
  )

energy_df_teres <- bind_rows(plankton_CN, chem_DOC_DON) %>%
  mutate(
    Taxon        = factor(Taxon, levels = all_taxa),
    ElementGroup = factor(ElementGroup, levels = c("Carbon","Nitrogen")),
    pattern_key  = if_else(ElementGroup == "Nitrogen", "stripe", "none"),
    pattern_key  = factor(pattern_key, levels = c("none","stripe"))
  )

energy_species_stack <- energy_df_teres %>%
  group_by(Species, Taxon, ElementGroup, pattern_key) %>%
  summarise(Mean = mean(Delta_umol_L, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

species_levels <- energy_species_stack %>% distinct(Species) %>% pull(Species)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART 2 — OUR ΔO2 reference: genus-specific if n>=2 else HMA/LMA fallback
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
delta_candidates <- intersect(names(sponge_db2_with_cn), c("delta_o2","delta_oxygen","delta","Delta_O2"))
has_delta <- length(delta_candidates) > 0

our_base <- sponge_db2_with_cn %>%
  mutate(
    species_chr = as.character(species),
    genus = str_extract(str_to_lower(species_chr), "^[a-z]+"),
    Delta_O2 = if (has_delta) {
      to_num(.data[[delta_candidates[1]]])
    } else {
      to_num(oxy_in_mean_oxygen_conc_umol_l) - to_num(oxy_ex_mean_oxygen_conc_umol_l)
    }
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(Delta_O2), !is.na(genus))

hma_col <- intersect(names(our_base), c("HMA_LMA","hma_lma","hma-lma","hmaLma"))
if (length(hma_col) > 0) {
  hma_use <- hma_col[1]
  our_base <- our_base %>%
    mutate(
      HMA_LMA = toupper(str_trim(as.character(.data[[hma_use]]))),
      HMA_LMA = ifelse(HMA_LMA %in% c("HMA","LMA"), HMA_LMA, NA_character_)
    )
} else {
  our_base <- our_base %>%
    mutate(
      HMA_LMA = case_when(
        genus %in% c("agelas","chondrosia","petrosia") ~ "HMA",
        genus %in% c("dysidea","crambe")              ~ "LMA",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    )
}
our_base <- our_base %>% filter(!is.na(HMA_LMA))

ref_genus_tbl <- our_base %>%
  group_by(genus, HMA_LMA) %>%
  summarise(mean_ci95(Delta_O2), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(RefType = "Genus-specific")

ref_group_tbl <- our_base %>%
  group_by(HMA_LMA) %>%
  summarise(mean_ci95(Delta_O2), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(RefType = "Group HMA/LMA")

teres_species_map <- tibble(Species = species_levels) %>%
  mutate(
    genus = case_when(
      str_detect(str_to_lower(Species), "agelas")      ~ "agelas",
      str_detect(str_to_lower(Species), "chondrosia")  ~ "chondrosia",
      str_detect(str_to_lower(Species), "dysidea")     ~ "dysidea",
      str_detect(str_to_lower(Species), "crambe")      ~ "crambe",
      str_detect(str_to_lower(Species), "petrosia")    ~ "petrosia",
      TRUE ~ str_extract(str_to_lower(Species), "^[a-z]+")
    ),
    HMA_LMA = case_when(
      genus %in% c("agelas","chondrosia","petrosia") ~ "HMA",
      genus %in% c("dysidea","crambe")              ~ "LMA",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

overlay_df <- teres_species_map %>%
  left_join(ref_genus_tbl, by = c("genus","HMA_LMA")) %>%
  mutate(has_genus_specific = is.finite(mean_ref) & n >= 2) %>%
  left_join(
    ref_group_tbl %>% select(HMA_LMA, mean_ref_g = mean_ref, ci_low_g = ci_low, ci_high_g = ci_high, n_g = n),
    by = "HMA_LMA"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    mean_ref = ifelse(has_genus_specific, mean_ref, mean_ref_g),
    ci_low   = ifelse(has_genus_specific, ci_low,   ci_low_g),
    ci_high  = ifelse(has_genus_specific, ci_high,  ci_high_g),
    n_use    = ifelse(has_genus_specific, n,        n_g),
    RefType  = ifelse(has_genus_specific, "Genus-specific", "Group HMA/LMA")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(HMA_LMA), is.finite(mean_ref), is.finite(ci_low), is.finite(ci_high)) %>%
  mutate(
    HMA_LMA = factor(HMA_LMA, levels = c("HMA","LMA")),
    x    = as.numeric(factor(Species, levels = species_levels)),
    xmin = x - band_half_width,
    xmax = x + band_half_width
  )

cat("\n[QC] OUR reference type used per Teres species:\n")
print(overlay_df %>% select(Species, genus, HMA_LMA, RefType, n_use) %>% arrange(HMA_LMA, RefType, Species))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART 3 — PLOT
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ref_cols_ci <- c(
  "HMA" = scales::alpha(tint_col(ref_cols["HMA"], ci_band_tint), ci_alpha),
  "LMA" = scales::alpha(tint_col(ref_cols["LMA"], ci_band_tint), ci_alpha)
)

p_energy_teres_species <- ggplot(
  energy_species_stack,
  aes(
    x = factor(Species, levels = species_levels),
    y = Mean,
    fill = Taxon,
    pattern = pattern_key
  )
) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.25, colour = "grey60") +

  geom_col_pattern(
    width = 0.85,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.25,
    pattern_fill = NA,
    pattern_colour = "black",
    pattern_angle = 45,
    pattern_density = 0.12,
    pattern_spacing = 0.02
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols, drop = FALSE, name = "Taxon") +

  scale_pattern_manual(
    values = c("none" = "none", "stripe" = "stripe"),
    breaks = c("none","stripe"),
    labels = c("Carbon","Nitrogen"),
    name   = "Element"
  ) +

  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(order = 1, nrow = 1, override.aes = list(pattern = "none")),
    pattern = guide_legend(order = 2, nrow = 1),
    colour  = guide_legend(order = 3, nrow = 1)
  ) +

  ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +

  geom_rect(
    data = overlay_df,
    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high, fill = HMA_LMA),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    colour = NA
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ref_cols_ci, drop = FALSE, name = "Our ΔO2 reference\n(95% CI)") +

  geom_segment(
    data = overlay_df,
    aes(x = xmin, xend = xmax, y = mean_ref, yend = mean_ref, color = HMA_LMA),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 2.2
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = ref_cols, drop = FALSE, name = "Our ΔO2 reference\n(mean)") +

  labs(
    title = "Teres — Species mean energy components (Δ IN−EX)",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Scenario: ", scenario_keep,
      " | Nitrogen scaled ×", scale_N_by,
      " | ΔO2 reference: genus-specific when available (n≥2), otherwise HMA/LMA fallback"
    ),
    x = NULL,
    y = expression(
      paste("Mean ", Delta, " (", mu, "mol L"^{-1}, ")")
    )
  ) +

  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position      = "top",
    legend.direction     = "horizontal",
    legend.box           = "horizontal",
    legend.box.background = element_blank(),
    legend.title         = element_text(size = legend_title_size),
    legend.text          = element_text(size = legend_text_size),
    legend.key.size      = unit(legend_key_size, "cm"),
    legend.spacing.x     = unit(0.18, "cm"),
    legend.margin        = margin(2, 2, 2, 2),
    axis.text.x          = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1)
  )

print(p_energy_teres_species)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SAVE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Teres_EnergyBudget_SpeciesMean_Stacked_refCI95_tinted.png"),
  plot     = p_energy_teres_species,
  width    = 30, height = 15, units = "cm", dpi = 300
)
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Teres_EnergyBudget_SpeciesMean_Stacked_refCI95_tinted.pdf"),
  plot     = p_energy_teres_species,
  width    = 30, height = 15, units = "cm"
)
################################################################################
# END
################################################################################
```


### Teres — Energy budget: stacked "Total Energy" (plankton C/N + DOC/DON + NOx) — NO ΔO2
```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}

### Teres — Energy budget: stacked "Total Energy" (plankton C/N + DOC/DON + NOx) — NO ΔO2

################################################################################
# Teres — Two bars per species (with 95% CI):
#
#   (1) Total Energy (stacked):
#       - Plankton C/N by Taxon (Bact/Pro/Syn/Euk) from teres_cn_wide
#       - Plus DOC + DON from teres_long_wide (Param == doc/don)
#       - Nitrogen parts are: striped + multiplied by ×3
#         (i.e., Taxa-N ×3 + DON ×3)
#
#   (2) NOx (single bar):
#       - Shown as REMOVAL: (EX − IN) ×3
#       - Always striped (Nitrogen) + multiplied by ×3
#
# 95% CI (IMPORTANT, statistically correct):
#   - We compute EVENT-LEVEL totals first (EventID = Species + sn + date + plaque),
#     then compute mean ± 95% t-CI across events within each species.
#   - We do NOT sum component CIs (not valid). We sum components at the event level.
#
# Plot fixes:
#   ✅ Taxon legend smooth; ONLY Element Group legend uses stripes
#   ✅ Species labels italic, shown once per pair (under Total Energy only)
#   ✅ Vertical separator lines between species pairs
#   ✅ Stripes show multiple diagonal lines (tight spacing)
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpattern)
library(readr)
library(grid)  # unit()

stopifnot(exists("teres_cn_wide"))
stopifnot(exists("teres_long_wide"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SETTINGS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
scenario_keep <- "S2_fixed_Coastal"
scale_N_by    <- 3

# species order + the exact binomial labels you want to see
species_order <- c(
  "Agelas oroides",
  "Crambe crambe",
  "Chondrosia reniformis",
  "Dysidea avara",
  "Petrosia ficiformis"
)

# plankton taxa that appear in teres_cn_wide$Taxon
taxa_plankton     <- c("Bact","Euk","Pro","Syn")
components_total  <- c(taxa_plankton, "DOC", "DON")
components_all    <- c(components_total, "NOx")

# colors (as you set)
fill_cols <- c(
  "Bact" = "#F4A6A6",
  "Euk"  = "#A8D5BA",
  "Pro"  = "#9DBCE3",
  "Syn"  = "#F6C28B",
  "DOC"  = "#B9B9B9",
  "DON"  = "#8F8F8F",
  "NOx"  = "#6C91BF"
)

  # stripe look (more than one line)
stripe_angle   <- 45
stripe_density <- 0.25
stripe_spacing <- 0.015   # smaller => more stripes

# output
out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/Energy_budget/Teres"
out_dir <- normalizePath(out_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# HELPERS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
to_num <- function(x) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(parse_number(as.character(x))))

# robust species mapping: Teresa short names -> full binomial
map_species <- function(x) {
  x <- str_squish(as.character(x))
  x_low <- str_to_lower(x)
  case_when(
    x_low %in% c("agelas","agelas oroides") ~ "Agelas oroides",
    x_low %in% c("crambe","crambe crambe") ~ "Crambe crambe",
    x_low %in% c("chondrosia","chondrosia reniformis") ~ "Chondrosia reniformis",
    x_low %in% c("dysidea","dysidea avara","dysidea avaea") ~ "Dysidea avara",
    x_low %in% c("petrosia","petrosia ficiformis") ~ "Petrosia ficiformis",
    TRUE ~ x
  )
}

# x-axis labels: italic species only under "Total Energy", blank under "NOx"
x_lab_fun <- function(x) {
  out <- vector("list", length(x))
  for (i in seq_along(x)) {
    parts <- strsplit(as.character(x[i]), " \\| ")[[1]]
    sp <- str_trim(parts[1])
    bt <- str_trim(parts[2])
    if (bt == "Total Energy") {
      out[[i]] <- parse(text = sprintf("italic('%s')", sp))[[1]]
    } else {
      out[[i]] <- ""
    }
  }
  as.expression(out)
}

# mean ± 95% CI (t interval). Returns: mean, lower, upper, n
mean_ci95 <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  n <- length(x)
  if (n < 2) {
    return(tibble(mean = mean(x), lower = NA_real_, upper = NA_real_, n = n))
  }
  m <- mean(x)
  se <- sd(x) / sqrt(n)
  tval <- qt(0.975, df = n - 1)
  tibble(mean = m, lower = m - tval * se, upper = m + tval * se, n = n)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART A — Build component-level data for the STACKED "Total Energy" bar
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# A1) plankton C/N by Taxon (teres_cn_wide has Taxon + Element + Diff_umol_L)
plankton_CN <- teres_cn_wide %>%
  filter(Scenario == scenario_keep) %>%
  transmute(
    Species      = map_species(species),
    Component    = str_squish(as.character(Taxon)),
    ElementGroup = str_squish(as.character(Element)),
    Delta_umol_L = to_num(Diff_umol_L)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Species), Species != "",
    Component %in% taxa_plankton,
    is.finite(Delta_umol_L),
    !is.na(ElementGroup), ElementGroup != ""
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ElementGroup = case_when(
      str_detect(str_to_lower(ElementGroup), "carbon|^c$")   ~ "Carbon",
      str_detect(str_to_lower(ElementGroup), "nitrogen|^n$") ~ "Nitrogen",
      TRUE ~ ElementGroup
    ),
    # multiply ONLY nitrogen parts by 3
    Delta_umol_L = if_else(ElementGroup == "Nitrogen", Delta_umol_L * scale_N_by, Delta_umol_L),
    BarType      = "Total Energy"
  )

# A2) DOC + DON from teres_long_wide (Param column)
teres_long_wide2 <- teres_long_wide %>%
  mutate(
    Species  = map_species(species),
    Param    = str_to_lower(str_squish(as.character(Param))),
    Diff_num = to_num(Diff),
    In_num   = to_num(In),
    Ex_num   = to_num(Ex),
    # use Diff if present, else compute (IN−EX)
    Diff_use = ifelse(!is.na(Diff_num), Diff_num,
                      ifelse(!is.na(In_num) & !is.na(Ex_num), In_num - Ex_num, NA_real_))
  )

chem_DOC_DON <- teres_long_wide2 %>%
  filter(Param %in% c("doc","don")) %>%
  transmute(
    Species      = Species,
    Component    = if_else(Param == "doc", "DOC", "DON"),
    ElementGroup = if_else(Param == "doc", "Carbon", "Nitrogen"),
    Delta_umol_L = Diff_use
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Species), Species != "", is.finite(Delta_umol_L)) %>%
  mutate(
    Delta_umol_L = if_else(ElementGroup == "Nitrogen", Delta_umol_L * scale_N_by, Delta_umol_L),
    BarType      = "Total Energy"
  )
```
