```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# A3) collapse to SPECIES MEANS per component (what we stack in the bar)
total_energy_stack <- bind_rows(plankton_CN, chem_DOC_DON) %>%
  mutate(
    Species      = factor(Species, levels = species_order),
    Component    = factor(Component, levels = components_all),
    ElementGroup = factor(ElementGroup, levels = c("Carbon","Nitrogen")),
    pattern_key  = if_else(ElementGroup == "Nitrogen", "stripe", "none")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Species)) %>%
  group_by(Species, BarType, Component, ElementGroup, pattern_key) %>%
  summarise(Mean = mean(Delta_umol_L, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART B — NOx bar (single component) as REMOVAL (EX−IN) ×3
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

stopifnot(all(c("noxt-in","noxt-ex") %in% names(teres_long_wide)))

nox_bar <- teres_long_wide %>%
  transmute(
    Species = factor(map_species(species), levels = species_order),
    NOx_in  = to_num(.data[["noxt-in"]]),
    NOx_ex  = to_num(.data[["noxt-ex"]])
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # REMOVAL = EX − IN  (already the sign convention you wanted)
    Delta_NOx_plot = (NOx_ex - NOx_in) * scale_N_by
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Species), is.finite(Delta_NOx_plot)) %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  summarise(Mean = mean(Delta_NOx_plot, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    BarType      = "NOx",
    Component    = factor("NOx", levels = components_all),
    ElementGroup = factor("Nitrogen", levels = c("Carbon","Nitrogen")),
    pattern_key  = factor("stripe", levels = c("none","stripe"))
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART C — combine data for plotting: two bars per species (x_id)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

plot_df <- bind_rows(total_energy_stack, nox_bar) %>%
  mutate(
    BarType = factor(BarType, levels = c("Total Energy","NOx")),
    pattern_key = factor(pattern_key, levels = c("none","stripe"))
  )

# build x_id levels (Species | BarType) in the order we want
x_levels <- plot_df %>%
  distinct(Species, BarType) %>%
  arrange(Species, BarType) %>%
  mutate(x_id = paste0(as.character(Species), " | ", as.character(BarType))) %>%
  pull(x_id)

plot_df <- plot_df %>%
  mutate(
    x_id = factor(paste0(as.character(Species), " | ", as.character(BarType)),
                  levels = x_levels)
  )

# separator lines between species groups (after each pair)
n_species <- length(species_order)
sep_x <- seq(2.5, by = 2, length.out = max(0, n_species - 1))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUBTITLE (explicit computations)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
subtitle_txt <- paste0(
  "Total Energy = mean Δ(IN−EX) per species from plankton C/N (Bact/Pro/Syn/Euk) + DOC/DON. ",
  "Nitrogen components (Taxa-N + DON) scaled ×", scale_N_by, " and shown as striped. ",
  "NOx shown as REMOVAL (EX−IN) and scaled ×", scale_N_by, ". ",
  "95% CI computed from EVENT-LEVEL totals (Species×sn×date×plaque), not by summing component CIs."
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART D — 95% CI (EVENT-LEVEL) for Total and NOx
#   Key principle: sum components at EVENT level first, then CI on the totals.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# D1) Event-level plankton sum (with N×3 already applied)
event_plankton <- teres_cn_wide %>%
  filter(Scenario == scenario_keep) %>%
  mutate(
    Species = map_species(species),
    Species = factor(Species, levels = species_order),
    EventID = paste(as.character(Species), sn, date, plaque, sep = "_"),
    Delta   = to_num(Diff_umol_L),
    Delta   = if_else(Element == "Nitrogen", Delta * scale_N_by, Delta)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Species), is.finite(Delta)) %>%
  group_by(Species, EventID) %>%
  summarise(Plankton_sum = sum(Delta, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# D2) Event-level DOC+DON sum (DON×3)
event_DOC_DON <- teres_long_wide2 %>%
  filter(Param %in% c("doc","don")) %>%
  mutate(
    Species = factor(Species, levels = species_order),
    EventID = paste(as.character(Species), sn, date, plaque, sep = "_"),
    Delta   = Diff_use,
    Delta   = if_else(Param == "don", Delta * scale_N_by, Delta)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Species), is.finite(Delta)) %>%
  group_by(Species, EventID) %>%
  summarise(DOC_DON_sum = sum(Delta, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# D3) Merge to get EVENT-LEVEL Total Energy
event_total <- full_join(event_plankton, event_DOC_DON, by = c("Species","EventID")) %>%
  mutate(
    Total = coalesce(Plankton_sum, 0) + coalesce(DOC_DON_sum, 0)
  )

# D4) Species-level CI for Total Energy
total_CI <- event_total %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  summarise(mean_ci95(Total), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(BarType = factor("Total Energy", levels = c("Total Energy","NOx")))

# D5) Event-level NOx and CI
event_nox <- teres_long_wide %>%
  mutate(
    Species = factor(map_species(species), levels = species_order),
    EventID = paste(as.character(Species), sn, date, plaque, sep = "_"),
    NOx_in  = to_num(.data[["noxt-in"]]),
    NOx_ex  = to_num(.data[["noxt-ex"]]),
    NOx     = (NOx_ex - NOx_in) * scale_N_by
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Species), is.finite(NOx)) %>%
  group_by(Species, EventID) %>%
  summarise(NOx = mean(NOx), .groups = "drop")

nox_CI <- event_nox %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  summarise(mean_ci95(NOx), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(BarType = factor("NOx", levels = c("Total Energy","NOx")))

# D6) Combine CIs and build x_id (MUST match plot_df x_id)
CI_df <- bind_rows(total_CI, nox_CI) %>%
  mutate(
    x_id = factor(paste0(as.character(Species), " | ", as.character(BarType)),
                  levels = x_levels)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(x_id))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLOT
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_teres <- ggplot(
  plot_df,
  aes(x = x_id, y = Mean, fill = Component, pattern = pattern_key)
) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.25, colour = "grey60") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = sep_x, linewidth = 0.35, colour = "grey70") +

  geom_col_pattern(
    width = 0.85,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.25,
    pattern_fill    = NA,
    pattern_colour  = "black",
    pattern_angle   = stripe_angle,
    pattern_density = stripe_density,
    pattern_spacing = stripe_spacing
  ) +

  # 95% CI error bars (do NOT inherit aes from main plot!)
  geom_errorbar(
    data = CI_df,
    aes(x = x_id, ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    width = 0.25,
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols, drop = FALSE, name = "Taxon") +

  scale_pattern_manual(
    values = c("none" = "none", "stripe" = "stripe"),
    breaks = c("none","stripe"),
    labels = c("Carbon", paste0("Nitrogen (scaled ×", scale_N_by, ")")),
    name   = "Element Group"
  ) +

  # Taxon legend: smooth; Element Group legend: Carbon vs Nitrogen pattern
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      order = 1, nrow = 2,
      override.aes = list(pattern = "none")
    ),
    pattern = guide_legend(
      order = 2, nrow = 1,
      override.aes = list(
        # don't force stripe; just make sure the key uses the same params
        fill            = "white",
        colour          = "black",
        pattern_fill    = NA,
        pattern_colour  = "black",
        pattern_angle   = stripe_angle,
        pattern_density = stripe_density,
        pattern_spacing = stripe_spacing
      )
    )
  ) +

  scale_x_discrete(labels = x_lab_fun_titles) +

  labs(
    title    = "Overall species means: Total Energy (stacked) | NOx (removal)",
    subtitle = subtitle_txt,
    x = NULL,
    y = expression(paste("Mean ", Delta, " (", mu, "mol L"^{-1}, ")"))
  ) +

  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "top",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.box       = "horizontal",
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.key.size  = unit(0.42, "cm"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    plot.title.position = "plot"
  )

print(p_teres)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SAVE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Teres_TwoBars_TotalEnergy_vs_NOx_Removal_scaled3_CI95.png"),
  plot     = p_teres,
  width    = 34, height = 16, units = "cm", dpi = 300
)
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Teres_TwoBars_TotalEnergy_vs_NOx_Removal_scaled3_CI95.pdf"),
  plot     = p_teres,
  width    = 34, height = 16, units = "cm"
)

################################################################################
# END
################################################################################

```


### Teres — Directional mean flux (Option B): Release vs Uptake (NO ΔO2)

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}

### Teres — Directional mean flux (Option B): Release vs Uptake (NO ΔO2)


################################################################################
# Teres — Two bars per species (Directional mean flux; NO duplicates):
#
# LEFT  = Release  : components with net_mean < 0  (plotted as abs(net_mean))
# RIGHT = Uptake   : components with net_mean > 0  (plotted as abs(net_mean))
#
# Data sources:
#   1) Plankton C/N by Taxon (Bact/Euk/Pro/Syn): teres_cn_wide, Diff_umol_L = (IN−EX)
#      - Nitrogen rows (Element == Nitrogen) are scaled ×3 and striped
#   2) DOC/DON: teres_long_wide, Diff if present else (IN−EX)
#      - DON scaled ×3 and striped
#   3) NOx: teres_long_wide columns noxt-in / noxt-ex
#      - NOx defined as (EX−IN) and scaled ×3 (nitrogen -> striped)
#
# Key visualization rules:
#   ✅ Taxon legend smooth (no stripes)
#   ✅ ONLY Element legend shows stripes (Carbon vs Nitrogen×3)
#   ✅ Species labels italic, shown once per species (under Uptake only)
#   ✅ Vertical separators between species
#   ✅ More stripes (tight spacing)
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpattern)
library(readr)
library(grid)  # unit()

stopifnot(exists("teres_cn_wide"))
stopifnot(exists("teres_long_wide"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SETTINGS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
scenario_keep <- "S2_fixed_Coastal"
scale_N_by    <- 3

species_order <- c(
  "Agelas oroides",
  "Crambe crambe",
  "Chondrosia reniformis",
  "Dysidea avara",
  "Petrosia ficiformis"
)

taxa_plankton  <- c("Bact","Euk","Pro","Syn")
components_all <- c(taxa_plankton, "DOC", "DON", "NOx")

fill_cols <- c(
  "Bact" = "#F4A6A6",
  "Euk"  = "#A8D5BA",
  "Pro"  = "#9DBCE3",
  "Syn"  = "#F6C28B",
  "DOC"  = "#B9B9B9",
  "DON"  = "#8F8F8F",
  "NOx"  = "#6C91BF"
)

# Stripe look (more diagonal lines)
stripe_angle   <- 45
stripe_density <- 0.25
stripe_spacing <- 0.015

out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/Energy_budget/Teres"
out_dir <- normalizePath(out_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# HELPERS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
to_num <- function(x) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(parse_number(as.character(x))))

# map Teresa short names -> full binomial (robust)
map_species <- function(x) {
  x <- str_squish(as.character(x))
  x_low <- str_to_lower(x)
  case_when(
    x_low %in% c("agelas","agelas oroides") ~ "Agelas oroides",
    x_low %in% c("crambe","crambe crambe") ~ "Crambe crambe",
    x_low %in% c("chondrosia","chondrosia reniformis") ~ "Chondrosia reniformis",
    x_low %in% c("dysidea","dysidea avara","dysidea avaea") ~ "Dysidea avara",
    x_low %in% c("petrosia","petrosia ficiformis") ~ "Petrosia ficiformis",
    TRUE ~ x
  )
}

# x-axis labels: italic species only under Uptake bar
x_lab_fun <- function(x) {
  out <- vector("list", length(x))
  for (i in seq_along(x)) {
    parts <- strsplit(as.character(x[i]), " \\| ")[[1]]
    sp <- str_trim(parts[1]); bt <- str_trim(parts[2])
    if (bt == "Uptake") out[[i]] <- parse(text = sprintf("italic('%s')", sp))[[1]] else out[[i]] <- ""
  }
  as.expression(out)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART 0 — Build consistent EventID = Species + sn + date + plaque
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# We compute deltas at EVENT level first, then compute species-level net means.

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART 1 — EVENT-LEVEL plankton components (Bact/Euk/Pro/Syn) from teres_cn_wide
#   Delta = Diff_umol_L (IN−EX); Nitrogen rows ×3
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
event_plankton <- teres_cn_wide %>%
  filter(Scenario == scenario_keep) %>%
  transmute(
    Species      = map_species(species),
    EventID      = paste(map_species(species), sn, date, plaque, sep = "_"),
    Component    = str_squish(as.character(Taxon)),
    ElementGroup = str_squish(as.character(Element)),
    Delta_raw    = to_num(Diff_umol_L)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Species), Species != "",
    Component %in% taxa_plankton,
    is.finite(Delta_raw),
    !is.na(ElementGroup), ElementGroup != ""
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ElementGroup = case_when(
      str_detect(str_to_lower(ElementGroup), "carbon|^c$")   ~ "Carbon",
      str_detect(str_to_lower(ElementGroup), "nitrogen|^n$") ~ "Nitrogen",
      TRUE ~ ElementGroup
    ),
    Delta = if_else(ElementGroup == "Nitrogen", Delta_raw * scale_N_by, Delta_raw)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART 2 — EVENT-LEVEL DOC/DON from teres_long_wide
#   Delta = Diff if present else (IN−EX); DON ×3
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
teres_long_wide2 <- teres_long_wide %>%
  mutate(
    Species  = map_species(species),
    Param    = str_to_lower(str_squish(as.character(Param))),
    Diff_num = to_num(Diff),
    In_num   = to_num(In),
    Ex_num   = to_num(Ex),
    Delta0   = ifelse(!is.na(Diff_num), Diff_num,
                      ifelse(!is.na(In_num) & !is.na(Ex_num), In_num - Ex_num, NA_real_)),
    EventID  = paste(map_species(species), sn, date, plaque, sep = "_")
  )

event_docdon <- teres_long_wide2 %>%
  filter(Param %in% c("doc","don")) %>%
  transmute(
    Species      = Species,
    EventID      = EventID,
    Component    = if_else(Param == "doc", "DOC", "DON"),
    ElementGroup = if_else(Param == "doc", "Carbon", "Nitrogen"),
    Delta_raw    = Delta0
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Species), Species != "", is.finite(Delta_raw)) %>%
  mutate(
    Delta = if_else(Component == "DON", Delta_raw * scale_N_by, Delta_raw)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART 3 — EVENT-LEVEL NOx from teres_long_wide (noxt-ex - noxt-in), ×3
#   Convention: NOx = (EX−IN) ×3
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
stopifnot(all(c("noxt-in","noxt-ex") %in% names(teres_long_wide)))

event_nox <- teres_long_wide %>%
  transmute(
    Species = map_species(species),
    EventID = paste(map_species(species), sn, date, plaque, sep = "_"),
    NOx_in  = to_num(.data[["noxt-in"]]),
    NOx_ex  = to_num(.data[["noxt-ex"]])
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Delta = (NOx_in -  NOx_ex ) * scale_N_by
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Species), Species != "", is.finite(Delta)) %>%
  transmute(
    Species      = Species,
    EventID      = EventID,
    Component    = "NOx",
    ElementGroup = "Nitrogen",
    Delta        = Delta
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART 4 — Combine all EVENT-LEVEL deltas, then compute species net mean
#   Directional rule (Option B):
#     net_mean > 0  -> Uptake
#     net_mean < 0  -> Release
#   Plot magnitude = abs(net_mean) so both bars are positive heights.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
event_all <- bind_rows(
  event_plankton %>% transmute(Species, EventID, Component, ElementGroup, Delta),
  event_docdon   %>% transmute(Species, EventID, Component, ElementGroup, Delta),
  event_nox
) %>%
  mutate(
    Species      = factor(Species, levels = species_order),
    Component    = factor(Component, levels = components_all),
    ElementGroup = factor(ElementGroup, levels = c("Carbon","Nitrogen"))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Species), !is.na(Component), is.finite(Delta))

species_comp_net <- event_all %>%
  group_by(Species, Component, ElementGroup) %>%
  summarise(
    net_mean = mean(Delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_events = dplyr::n_distinct(EventID),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(net_mean), net_mean != 0) %>%
  mutate(
    BarType     = if_else(net_mean > 0, "Uptake", "Release"),
    Mean        = abs(net_mean),
    pattern_key = if_else(ElementGroup == "Nitrogen", "stripe", "none")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    BarType     = factor(BarType, levels = c("Release","Uptake")),
    pattern_key = factor(pattern_key, levels = c("none","stripe"))
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART 4B — 95% CI for BAR TOTALS (Release/Uptake), matching the plot definition
#   We bootstrap across EventID within each species:
#     1) sample EventIDs with replacement
#     2) compute mean Delta per Component
#     3) take abs(mean) (same as plot)
#     4) sum abs(means) into Release/Uptake using the ORIGINAL assignment table
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

set.seed(1)
B <- 1000  # number of bootstrap resamples (increase if you want smoother CI)

# Component -> BarType assignment fixed from the ORIGINAL species_comp_net
assign_tbl <- species_comp_net %>%
  select(Species, Component, BarType) %>%
  distinct()

# Helper: bootstrap CI for one species
boot_one_species <- function(sp_df, sp_name, assign_sp, B = 1000) {
  ev_ids <- unique(sp_df$EventID)
  n_ev   <- length(ev_ids)

  if (n_ev < 2) {
    return(tibble(
      Species = sp_name,
      BarType = factor(c("Release","Uptake"), levels = c("Release","Uptake")),
      mean = NA_real_, lower = NA_real_, upper = NA_real_, n_events = n_ev
    ))
  }

  # store bootstrap bar totals
  out <- vector("list", B)

  for (b in seq_len(B)) {
    samp_ids <- sample(ev_ids, size = n_ev, replace = TRUE)

    # means per component for this bootstrap sample
    m_comp <- sp_df %>%
      filter(EventID %in% samp_ids) %>%
      group_by(Component) %>%
      summarise(m = mean(Delta, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
      right_join(assign_sp, by = "Component") %>%  # keep only assigned components
      mutate(m_abs = abs(m))

    # bar totals = sum abs(means) per BarType (matches your plot)
    bar_tot <- m_comp %>%
      group_by(BarType) %>%
      summarise(bar_total = sum(m_abs, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

    out[[b]] <- bar_tot
  }

  boot_df <- bind_rows(out)

  boot_df %>%
    group_by(BarType) %>%
    summarise(
      mean  = mean(bar_total, na.rm = TRUE),
      lower = quantile(bar_total, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
      upper = quantile(bar_total, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      Species = sp_name,
      n_events = n_ev,
      BarType = factor(as.character(BarType), levels = c("Release","Uptake"))
    ) %>%
    select(Species, BarType, mean, lower, upper, n_events)
}

# Build CI table for all species
total_CI_df <- event_all %>%
  mutate(
    Species = factor(Species, levels = species_order),
    Component = factor(Component, levels = components_all)
  ) %>%
  group_split(Species) %>%
  lapply(function(sp_df) {
    sp_name <- unique(sp_df$Species)[1]
    assign_sp <- assign_tbl %>%
      filter(Species == sp_name) %>%
      select(Component, BarType)
    boot_one_species(sp_df, sp_name, assign_sp, B = B)
  }) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  mutate(
    Species = factor(Species, levels = species_order),
    BarType = factor(BarType, levels = c("Release","Uptake"))
  )
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PART 5 — Create x_id = Species | BarType  (Release left, Uptake right)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
x_levels <- species_comp_net %>%
  distinct(Species, BarType) %>%
  arrange(Species, BarType) %>%
  mutate(x_id = paste0(as.character(Species), " | ", as.character(BarType))) %>%
  pull(x_id)

plot_stack <- species_comp_net %>%
  mutate(
    x_id = factor(paste0(as.character(Species), " | ", as.character(BarType)),
                  levels = x_levels)
  )


# add x_id to CI table (must match x_levels)
total_CI_df <- total_CI_df %>%
  mutate(
    x_id = factor(paste0(as.character(Species), " | ", as.character(BarType)),
                  levels = x_levels)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(x_id))

# vertical separators after each species pair (2 bars)
sep_x <- seq(2.5, by = 2, length.out = max(0, length(species_order) - 1))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUBTITLE (clear definitions)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
subtitle_txt <- paste0(
  "Directional mean flux per component (species means). ",
  "Plankton: Δ(IN−EX) by Taxon; Nitrogen rows ×", scale_N_by, " (striped). ",
  "DOC/DON: Diff if present else (IN−EX); DON ×", scale_N_by, " (striped). ",
  "NOx: (EX−IN) ×", scale_N_by, " (striped). ",
  "Assignment: net_mean>0 → Uptake; net_mean<0 → Release. Bars show |net_mean|."
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLOT
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_dir <- ggplot(
  plot_stack,
  aes(x = x_id, y = Mean, fill = Component, pattern = pattern_key)
) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.25, colour = "grey60") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = sep_x, linewidth = 0.35, colour = "grey70") +

  geom_col_pattern(
    width = 0.85,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.25,
    pattern_fill    = NA,
    pattern_colour  = "black",
    pattern_angle   = stripe_angle,
    pattern_density = stripe_density,
    pattern_spacing = stripe_spacing
  ) +
    # TOTAL 95% CI (one per bar: Release/Uptake)
  geom_errorbar(
    data = total_CI_df,
    aes(x = x_id, ymin = lower, ymax = upper),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    width = 0.22,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
geom_text(
  data = species_label_df,
  aes(x = x_triplet, y = y, label = Species),
  inherit.aes = FALSE,
  fontface = "italic",
  size = 4
) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols, drop = FALSE, name = "Component") +

  scale_pattern_manual(
    values = c("none" = "none", "stripe" = "stripe"),
    breaks = c("none","stripe"),
    labels = c("Carbon", paste0("Nitrogen (×", scale_N_by, ")")),
    name   = "Element"
  ) +

  # KEY: component legend smooth; element legend shows stripes only for Nitrogen
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(order = 1, nrow = 2, override.aes = list(pattern = "none")),
    pattern = guide_legend(order = 2, nrow = 1)
  ) +

  scale_x_discrete(labels = x_lab_fun) +

  labs(
    title    = "Teres — Uptake vs Release (Directional mean flux; species means)",
    subtitle = subtitle_txt,
    x = NULL,
    y = expression(paste("|", Delta, "| (", mu, "mol L"^{-1}, ")"))
  ) +

  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "top",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.box       = "horizontal",
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.key.size  = unit(0.42, "cm"),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.title.position = "plot"
  )

print(p_dir)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SAVE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(out_dir, "Teres_DirectionalMean_Uptake_vs_Release_scaled3.png"),
  plot     = p_dir,
  width    = 34, height = 16, units = "cm", dpi = 300
)


################################################################################
# END
################################################################################
```


### TERES Flux Engine (event level) — regular solutes + C/N scenarios

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
################################################################################
# TERES — Flux engine (event level)  ✅ FIXED to keep BOTH scenarios
#
# GOAL
#   1) Convert pump_rate (ml/min) → Q (L/hr)
#   2) Compute Δ (IN−EX) for regular solutes
#   3) Compute Flux = Q × Δ
#   4) Add C/N scenario fluxes (KEEP S1 + S2)
#   5) Produce ONE clean long table for plotting
#
# MAIN FIX
#   ✅ cn_keep_scenario is set to NULL by default → keeps ALL scenarios (S1+S2)
#   ✅ Added QC prints so you can SEE which scenarios exist at each stage
#   ✅ More robust Δ: uses Diff if available, else (In - Ex) safely
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)

stopifnot(exists("teres_long_wide"))
stopifnot(exists("teres_cn_wide"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SETTINGS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/Energy_budget/Teres_Flux"
out_dir <- normalizePath(out_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

fig_prefix <- "Teres_Flux"

# IMPORTANT:
#   - To see BOTH scenarios in the scenario plot, this MUST be NULL
#   - If later you want only one scenario, change to "S1_fixed_Oceanic" or "S2_fixed_Coastal"
cn_keep_scenario <- NULL

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
to_num <- function(x) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(parse_number(as.character(x))))

make_uid <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      plaque = as.character(plaque),
      sn     = as.character(sn),
      date   = as.character(date),
      Unique_code = paste0(plaque, "_sn", sn, "_", date)
    )
}

################################################################################
# 1) Build Q (flow rate in L/hr)
################################################################################
# pump_rate is in ml/min → convert to L/hr
# Q_L_hr = pump_rate * 60 / 1000

teres_q <- teres_long_wide %>%
  make_uid() %>%
  mutate(
    pump_rate = to_num(pump_rate),
    Q_L_hr    = pump_rate * 60 / 1000
  ) %>%
  select(Unique_code, Q_L_hr) %>%
  group_by(Unique_code) %>%
  summarise(
    # If duplicates exist per Unique_code, take median Q (robust)
    Q_L_hr = median(Q_L_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n[QC] teres_q:\n")
print(teres_q %>% summarise(n_rows = n(), n_Q = sum(is.finite(Q_L_hr))))

################################################################################
# 2) Regular solutes (DOC, DON, NH4, NOx, PO4, SiO4, POC, PON)
################################################################################

# ---- Robust mapping Param → clean Parameter names ----
map_param <- function(p) {
  p <- str_to_lower(str_squish(p))
  case_when(
    p == "doc" ~ "DOC",
    p == "don" ~ "DON",
    p == "nh4" ~ "NH4",
    str_detect(p, "^(noxt|noxtt|nox|no3no2)$") ~ "NOx",
    p == "po4" ~ "PO4",
    str_detect(p, "^(sio4|si)$") ~ "SiO4",
    p == "poc" ~ "POC",
    p == "pon" ~ "PON",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
}

teres_solutes <- teres_long_wide %>%
  make_uid() %>%
  mutate(
    Parameter = map_param(Param),

    In_num   = to_num(In),
    Ex_num   = to_num(Ex),
    Diff_num = to_num(Diff),

    # Prefer explicit Diff; otherwise compute IN−EX (safely)
    Delta_umol_L = ifelse(
      !is.na(Diff_num),
      Diff_num,
      ifelse(!is.na(In_num) & !is.na(Ex_num), In_num - Ex_num, NA_real_)
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Parameter), is.finite(Delta_umol_L)) %>%
  select(Unique_code, Parameter, Delta_umol_L) %>%
  group_by(Unique_code, Parameter) %>%
  summarise(
    Delta_umol_L = mean(Delta_umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ---- Add DIN (NH4 + NOx) ----
teres_solutes_wide <- teres_solutes %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Parameter, values_from = Delta_umol_L)

teres_solutes_wide <- teres_solutes_wide %>%
  mutate(
    DIN = ifelse(is.na(NH4) & is.na(NOx), NA_real_, coalesce(NH4, 0) + coalesce(NOx, 0))
  )

teres_solutes_long <- teres_solutes_wide %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Unique_code,
    names_to = "Parameter",
    values_to = "Delta_umol_L"
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(Delta_umol_L))

################################################################################
# 3) Attach Q and compute solute flux
################################################################################
teres_flux_solutes <- teres_solutes_long %>%
  left_join(teres_q, by = "Unique_code") %>%
  mutate(
    Flux_umol_hr = Q_L_hr * Delta_umol_L,
    Source = "Solutes"
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(Flux_umol_hr))

cat("\n[QC] solutes flux:\n")
print(teres_flux_solutes %>% summarise(n_rows = n(), n_flux = sum(is.finite(Flux_umol_hr))))

################################################################################
# 4) C/N scenario fluxes  ✅ KEEP BOTH SCENARIOS
################################################################################

# Step 4.1: collapse to event means (Unique_code × Scenario × Taxon × Element)
teres_cn_event <- teres_cn_wide %>%
  make_uid() %>%
  mutate(
    Scenario   = as.character(Scenario),
    Taxon      = as.character(Taxon),
    Element    = as.character(Element),
    Diff_umol_L = to_num(Diff_umol_L)
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(Diff_umol_L)) %>%
  group_by(Unique_code, Scenario, Taxon, Element) %>%
  summarise(
    Diff_umol_L = mean(Diff_umol_L, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n[QC] Scenarios in teres_cn_event BEFORE filtering:\n")
print(teres_cn_event %>% count(Scenario) %>% arrange(Scenario))

# Step 4.2: OPTIONAL scenario filter (keep NULL to keep both)
if (!is.null(cn_keep_scenario)) {
  teres_cn_event <- teres_cn_event %>% filter(Scenario == cn_keep_scenario)
}

cat("\n[QC] Scenarios in teres_cn_event AFTER filtering:\n")
print(teres_cn_event %>% count(Scenario) %>% arrange(Scenario))

# Step 4.3: join Q and compute flux
teres_flux_cn <- teres_cn_event %>%
  left_join(teres_q, by = "Unique_code") %>%
  mutate(
    Parameter   = paste0(Scenario, "_", Taxon, "_", Element),
    Delta_umol_L = Diff_umol_L,
    Flux_umol_hr = Q_L_hr * Diff_umol_L,
    Source = "C/N scenarios"
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(Flux_umol_hr)) %>%
  select(Unique_code, Parameter, Delta_umol_L, Flux_umol_hr, Source)

cat("\n[QC] CN flux rows per Scenario (from Parameter):\n")
print(
  teres_flux_cn %>%
    mutate(Scenario = str_extract(Parameter, "^S\\d+_fixed_(Oceanic|Coastal)")) %>%
    count(Scenario) %>%
    arrange(Scenario)
)

################################################################################
# 5) ONE combined table for plotting
################################################################################
flux_long_teres <- bind_rows(
  teres_flux_solutes %>%
    select(Unique_code, Parameter, Delta_umol_L, Flux_umol_hr, Source),
  teres_flux_cn
)

# QC overview
flux_qc <- flux_long_teres %>%
  count(Source, Parameter, name = "n") %>%
  arrange(desc(n))

cat("\n[QC] flux_long_teres counts:\n")
print(flux_qc, n = 200)

write.csv(
  flux_qc,
  file.path(out_dir, paste0(fig_prefix, "_QC_counts.csv")),
  row.names = FALSE
)

write.csv(
  flux_long_teres,
  file.path(out_dir, paste0(fig_prefix, "_flux_long_teres.csv")),
  row.names = FALSE
)

################################################################################
# OUTPUT:
#   teres_flux_solutes
#   teres_flux_cn
#   flux_long_teres
################################################################################
```


### TERES PLOTS: (A) All fluxes full+zoom ONE legend, (B) CN-only
```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
################################################################################
# CHUNK 2 — TERES PLOTS:
# (A) All fluxes full+zoom ONE legend
# (B) CN-only "Across scenarios" — X = Scenario (S1 + S2), facet = Element
#
# FIXES IN THIS VERSION:
#   ✅ B uses Scenario on X axis (so you can compare scenarios directly)
#   ✅ Totals across taxa (like your 2nd reference figure)
#   ✅ Shows BOTH scenarios: S1_fixed_Oceanic + S2_fixed_Coastal
#   ✅ QC print: tells you if one scenario is missing upstream
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

stopifnot(exists("flux_long_teres"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Settings
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
out_dir <- "C:/Users/ASUS/Google Drive/PHD/Chapter_4/Energy_budget/Teres_Flux"
out_dir <- normalizePath(out_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

fig_prefix <- "Teres_Flux"
zoom_lim <- 2.5  # zoom panel range (change if needed)

pal <- c(
  "C/N scenarios"    = "#66c2a5",
  "Metabolism / DOM" = "#fc8d62",
  "Nutrients"        = "#8da0cb",
  "Other"            = "grey60"
)

################################################################################
# A) All fluxes — BUT show ONLY S2 TOTAL for C/N
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

stopifnot(exists("flux_long_teres"))

# -----------------------------
# 1) Regular solutes (unchanged)
# -----------------------------
flux_solutes_only <- flux_long_teres %>%
  filter(Source == "Solutes")

# -----------------------------
# 2) S2 TOTAL across taxa (C/N)
# -----------------------------
flux_cn_s2_total <- flux_long_teres %>%
  filter(Source == "C/N scenarios") %>%
  mutate(
    Scenario = str_extract(Parameter, "^S\\d+_fixed_(Oceanic|Coastal)"),
    Element  = case_when(
      str_detect(Parameter, "Carbon$")   ~ "Carbon",
      str_detect(Parameter, "Nitrogen$") ~ "Nitrogen",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Scenario == "S2_fixed_Coastal") %>%   # 🔴 only S2
  group_by(Unique_code, Element) %>%
  summarise(
    Flux_umol_hr = sum(Flux_umol_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Parameter = paste0("S2_total_", Element),
    Source = "C/N scenarios"
  )

# -----------------------------
# 3) Combine solutes + S2 total
# -----------------------------
flux_plotA <- bind_rows(
  flux_solutes_only,
  flux_cn_s2_total
)

# -----------------------------
# 4) Define groups (color logic)
# -----------------------------
pal <- c(
  "C/N scenarios"    = "#66c2a5",
  "Metabolism / DOM" = "#fc8d62",
  "Nutrients"        = "#8da0cb",
  "Other"            = "grey60"
)

flux_plotA <- flux_plotA %>%
  mutate(
    ParamGroup = case_when(
      Source == "C/N scenarios" ~ "C/N scenarios",
      Parameter %in% c("DOC","DON") ~ "Metabolism / DOM",
      Parameter %in% c("NH4","NOx","DIN","PO4","SiO4") ~ "Nutrients",
      Parameter %in% c("POC","PON") ~ "Other",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    ParamGroup = factor(ParamGroup, levels = names(pal))
  )

# Keep order nice
flux_plotA$Parameter <- factor(
  flux_plotA$Parameter,
  levels = unique(flux_plotA$Parameter)
)

# -----------------------------
# 5) Plot (full + zoom)
# -----------------------------
zoom_lim <- 2.5

p_base <- ggplot(flux_plotA, aes(x = Parameter, y = Flux_umol_hr, fill = ParamGroup)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.75, width = 0.65) +
  geom_jitter(
    aes(color = ParamGroup),
    width = 0.15, alpha = 0.55, size = 1.7,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal, drop = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pal, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = expression(Flux~(mu*mol~hr^-1)), fill = NULL) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "right")

p_full <- p_base +
  labs(title = "Teres — All fluxes (S2 total for C/N)",
       subtitle = "Flux = Q × (IN − EX)")

p_zoom <- p_base +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-zoom_lim, zoom_lim)) +
  labs(title = paste0("Zoom around 0 (±", zoom_lim, " µmol hr⁻¹)"))

p_full_zoom <- (p_full + p_zoom) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1.2, 1), guides = "collect")

print(p_full_zoom)

# ==============================================================================
# B) CN-only across scenarios (LIKE your reference 2nd figure)
#    - X axis = Scenario (S1 + S2)
#    - facet = Element (Carbon / Nitrogen)
#    - values = TOTAL flux across taxa per event (Unique_code)
# ==============================================================================

keep_scen <- c("S1_fixed_Oceanic", "S2_fixed_Coastal")

# 1) CN rows + parse Scenario + Element from Parameter
cn_long <- flux_long_teres %>%
  filter(Source == "C/N scenarios") %>%
  mutate(
    Scenario = str_extract(Parameter, "^S\\d+_fixed_(Oceanic|Coastal)"),
    Element  = case_when(
      str_detect(Parameter, "Carbon$")   ~ "Carbon",
      str_detect(Parameter, "Nitrogen$") ~ "Nitrogen",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Scenario), !is.na(Element))

# ---- QC: show what scenarios exist RIGHT NOW ----
cat("\nCN scenarios found in flux_long_teres:\n")
print(cn_long %>% count(Scenario) %>% arrange(Scenario))

# 2) Total across taxa per event: Unique_code × Scenario × Element
cn_total_event <- cn_long %>%
  group_by(Unique_code, Scenario, Element) %>%
  summarise(
    Flux_umol_hr_total = sum(Flux_umol_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 3) Keep only S1 + S2 (and set factor order)
cn_total_event <- cn_total_event %>%
  filter(Scenario %in% keep_scen) %>%
  mutate(
    Scenario = factor(Scenario, levels = keep_scen),
    Element  = factor(Element, levels = c("Carbon","Nitrogen"))
  )

# If one of the scenarios is missing, warn clearly
missing_scen <- setdiff(keep_scen, unique(as.character(cn_total_event$Scenario)))
if (length(missing_scen) > 0) {
  warning(
    "Missing scenario(s) in CN plot data: ",
    paste(missing_scen, collapse = ", "),
    "\nThis usually means you filtered cn_keep_scenario in CHUNK 1 or S1 was not created upstream."
  )
}

# nicer x labels
scenario_labels <- c(
  "S1_fixed_Oceanic" = "S1 — Fixed Oceanic",
  "S2_fixed_Coastal" = "S2 — Fixed Coastal"
)

S1_col <- "#1b9e77"

p_cn_scenarios <- ggplot(cn_total_event, aes(x = Scenario, y = Flux_umol_hr_total)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, fill = S1_col, alpha = 0.65, width = 0.65) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.55, size = 1.7) +
  facet_wrap(~Element, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = scenario_labels, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "C/N flux across scenarios (Flux = Q × (IN − EX))",
    subtitle = "TOTAL across taxa (Bact+Euk+Pro+Syn); Positive = uptake; Negative = release",
    x = NULL,
    y = expression(Flux~(mu*mol~hr^-1))
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))

print(p_cn_scenarios)

ggsave(
  file.path(out_dir, paste0(fig_prefix, "_CN_total_byScenario_S1S2.png")),
  p_cn_scenarios, width = 34, height = 16, units = "cm", dpi = 300
)
ggsave(
  file.path(out_dir, paste0(fig_prefix, "_CN_total_byScenario_S1S2.pdf")),
  p_cn_scenarios, width = 34, height = 16, units = "cm"
)

################################################################################
# End CHUNK 2
################################################################################
```


```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=10}
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) הגדרת נתיבים - העתיקי לכאן את שמות התיקיות שנוצרו לך
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
dir_geodia <- "SMALL_run_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_GEODIA" # השם של תיקיית ה-Geodia
dir_agelas <- "SMALL_run_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_AGELAS" # השם של תיקיית ה-Agelas

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) טעינת הנתונים
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
df_geo <- read.csv(file.path(dir_geodia, "SMALL_Model_Statistics_Output.csv")) %>%
  mutate(Species = "Geodia barretti")

df_age <- read.csv(file.path(dir_agelas, "SMALL_Model_Statistics_Output.csv")) %>%
  mutate(Species = "Agelas oroides")

# איחוד הטבלאות
df_compare <- bind_rows(df_geo, df_age)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) השוואת מאזן פחמן (Carbon Budget)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# נבחר את הפרמטרים המרכזיים
c_params <- c("r10_totCprod", "DOCupt", "BacCupt", "CO2rel")

df_c <- df_compare %>% 
  filter(name %in% c_params) %>%
  mutate(mean_abs = abs(mean)) # נשתמש בערך מוחלט להשוואה נוחה

p_carbon <- ggplot(df_c, aes(x = name, y = mean_abs, fill = Species)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_abs - sd, ymax = mean_abs + sd), 
                position = position_dodge(0.9), width = 0.2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Carbon Budget Comparison",
       subtitle = "Comparing uptake, production and respiration",
       y = "umol C / cm3 / d", x = "") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1")

print(p_carbon)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) השוואת מאזן חנקן (Nitrogen Budget)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
n_params <- c("denit", "DNRA", "nitr_NH4", "nitr_NO2", "anammox")

df_n <- df_compare %>% 
  filter(name %in% n_params) %>%
  mutate(mean_abs = abs(mean))

p_nitrogen <- ggplot(df_n, aes(x = name, y = mean_abs, fill = Species)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_abs - sd, ymax = mean_abs + sd), 
                position = position_dodge(0.9), width = 0.2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Nitrogen Fluxes Comparison",
       y = "umol N / cm3 / d", x = "") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2")

print(p_nitrogen)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) חישוב יעילות גידול (NGE - Net Growth Efficiency)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# NGE = Production / (DOC_uptake + Bacteria_uptake)
nge_calc <- df_compare %>%
  filter(name %in% c("r10_totCprod", "DOCupt", "BacCupt")) %>%
  select(Species, name, mean) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = name, values_from = mean) %>%
  mutate(NGE = abs(r10_totCprod) / (abs(DOCupt) + abs(BacCupt)))

print("--- Estimated Net Growth Efficiency (NGE) ---")
print(nge_calc)
```



```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}




```
